item.title: 哪些工具用于撰写无剽窃论文？ | What Tools are used for Writing a Plagiarism-Free Dissertation?
item.description:
有几种工具可以帮助确保您的论文是原创的。 其中包括网络上可用的抄袭检查器，例如软件中的 Turnit 和 Research Prospect。 这些程序会扫描您的内容是否存在抄袭，并生成一份报告，将类似内容链接到您自己的内容。 通过删除此内容，您的论文将是独一无二且不可重复的。 继续阅读以了解有关这些工具以及如何使用它们的更多信息。 当您的文本与在 Grammarly 上找到的内容匹配时，它会突出显示文本并告诉您在哪里可以找到原始来源。 尽管 Grammarly 是撰写无剽窃论文的有用工具，但它不能保证您的作品完全是原创的。 它提供了一个由计算语言学家和深度学习工程师组成的内置团队，他们不断调整“优秀的写作”算法，研究数百万篇研究论文以提高其准确性。 它负责 Word 中的引用，并根据您选择的格式自动构建参考列表。 该程序功能非常丰富，将为您节省无数小时的工作时间。 它甚至可以通过允许您存储研究材料来帮助您撰写无抄袭论文。 您可以下载适用于 Windows 或 Mac 的 Mendeley 免费试用版以开始使用。 印象笔记：

博士生需要跟踪一切。 您可以标记文档、突出显示重要句子、创建 PDF 文件，甚至录制音频。 此外，您可以为所有笔记创建一个笔记本结构。 这样，您可以使所有内容井井有条并轻松搜索。

一篇好论文是从笔记中写出来的。 当您进行头脑风暴和撰写结论时，记笔记至关重要。 思维导图在您写作时也会派上用场，因为它们可以帮助您绘制思路。 该应用程序还允许您搜索其他用户的笔记。 第一种也是最明显的方法是手动阅读文章以查看是否有任何文本被复制。 这个过程需要很长时间，因为学生必须从不同来源收集信息，编译相关点，并重新表述内容。 但还有另一种方法——使用抄袭检查软件。 该软件是免费的，它会扫描您的文本以查找任何可能的抄袭。 然后它将突出显示已复制的文本。 它还允许您上传文本文件或 Doc 文档。 您最多可以免费检查 5,000 字，并收到有关是否有任何内容被抄袭的深入报告。 结果会立即显示，包括来源和抄袭百分比。 转氨酶：

许多学院和大学都使用 Turnitin 来确保学生工作的质量。 在使用 Turnitin 时，您必须确保您使用的是原始资源，因为免费的在线工具可能会给您带来不准确的结果。 要使用该服务，只需在网站上创建一个帐户，写下您的注册密钥和班级名称，然后填写任何其他部分。 该服务的结果将显示哪些作品已提交给 Turnitin，以及其中有多少是原创作品。



原文:

There are several tools that help in ensuring that your dissertation is original. Among them are the plagiarism checkers available on the web, such as the Turnit in software and Research Prospect. These programs scan your content for plagiarism and produce a report that links similar content to your own content. By removing this content, your dissertation will be unique and unrepeatable. Read on to learn more about the tools and how to use them. Grammarly: If you want to write a plagiarism-free dissertation, you can use Grammarly to check your work.The dissertation writing services provider also use grammarly and other tools for ensure the dissertation is free from plagiarism This plagiarism-checking software works by comparing your text with similar content on the Internet. When your text matches the content found on Grammarly, it highlights the text and tells you where you can find the original source. Although Grammarly is a useful tool for writing a plagiarism-free dissertation, it cannot guarantee that your work will be completely original. Features of Grammarly : The tool's features are impressive, including a strong spell checker and a dedicated style guide. Grammarly uses AI technology to suggest rewrites based on the target audience and tone of the text. It offers an in-built team of computational linguists and deep learning engineers who constantly tweak the "good writing" algorithms, studying millions of research papers to improve its accuracy. Mendeley: Mendeley is an online reference management system. It takes care of citations within Word and automatically builds a reference list based on the format you have selected. The program is very feature-rich and will save you countless hours of work. It can even help you write a plagiarism-free dissertation by allowing you to store your research materials. You can download a free trial of Mendeley for Windows or Mac to get started. Evernote:

A PhD student needs to keep track of everything. Evernote syncs between desktop and mobile devices so you can keep track of everything you need to write about. You can label documents, highlight important sentences, create a PDF file, and even record audio. Evernote can even save entire web pages for you! Moreover, you can create a notebook structure for all your notes. This way, you can keep everything organized and search it easily.

A good paper is written from notes. Taking notes is crucial when you're brainstorming and writing your conclusion. Mind maps also come in handy when you're writing, as they help you plot your train of thought. Evernote is a reliable note-taking application that connects to your email and makes it easy to access your notes anywhere. The app also lets you search for notes from other users. Plag Tracker: There are many ways to check if a piece of content has been plagiarized. The first and most obvious method is manually reading the piece to see if any text has been copied. This process takes a long time, as students will have to gather information from different sources, compile related points, and rephrase the content. But there is another method - using a plagiarism checking software. This software is free, and it scans your text for any possible plagiarism. It will then highlight the text that has been copied. PlagTracker works by checking the entire text against a database of over 20 million scholarly works and websites. It also allows you to upload a text file or Doc document. You can check up to 5,000 words for free and receive an in-depth report about whether or not any content has been plagiarized. The results are instantly displayed, including the source and the percentage of plagiarism. Turnitin:

Turnitin is a web-based plagiarism detection software that checks your writing for similarities to other works. Turnitin is used by many colleges and universities to ensure the quality of student work. While using Turnitin, you must ensure that you are using original sources, as free online tools can give you inaccurate results. To use the service, simply create an account with the website, write your enrollment key and class name, and fill out any other sections. The results of the service will show you which pieces of work have been submitted to Turnitin, and how many percent of them are original.
item.author: miguelsteven

item.title: 如何通过 4 个简单的步骤使用 WPForms 设置 Sendinblue 插件 | How To Setup Sendinblue Addon With WPForms In 4 Easy Steps
item.description:
安装 Sendinblue 插件

在开始之前，您首先需要确保在您的 WordPress 网站上安装并激活了 WPForms。
安装 WPForms 并验证您的许可证后，您将能够快速安装和激活 Sendinblue 插件。

将您的 Sendinblue 帐户连接到 WPForms

安装并激活 Sendinblue 插件后，您就可以连接您的帐户了。 为此，请转到 WPForms » 设置并单击集成选项卡。 然后，单击 Sendinblue 选项以展开其设置。 从那里，继续并单击添加新帐户按钮以输入您的帐户信息。 这将显示两个字段，您需要在其中添加您的 Sendinblue 帐户 API 密钥，以及该帐户的昵称。 要查找您的 API 密钥，请首先登录您的 Sendinblue 帐户。 登录后，单击位于页面右上角的主菜单，然后选择 SMTP & API。 继续并复制此值，然后返回到您的 WPForms 设置。 您需要将 API 密钥粘贴到标有 Sendinblue API 密钥的字段中。 然后，将昵称添加到 Sendinblue 帐户昵称字段。 这仅供内部参考，如果您以后决定连接其他 Sendinblue 帐户，可能会有所帮助。 准备好后，继续并单击连接到 Sendinblue 按钮。

完成连接后，Sendinblue 集成旁边会显示绿色的已连接状态。

在表单中添加 Sendinblue 集成

成功连接 Sendinblue 帐户后，您就可以将其链接到表单了。 为此，您首先需要创建一个新表单或编辑现有表单。 在表单构建器中，转到营销 » Sendinblue。 然后，在 Sendinblue 选项卡中，单击添加新连接按钮。 单击此按钮将打开一个覆盖，您可以在其中为此连接提供昵称。 您可以输入任何您喜欢的昵称，因为这仅供内部参考。 命名连接后，将出现其他设置。 选择帐户：从您已连接的任何 Sendinblue 帐户中进行选择。

要执行的操作：选择用户填写此表单时要执行的操作。 此处选择的选项还将确定显示哪些附加设置。

参考博客
如何通过 4 个简单的步骤使用 WPForms 设置 Sendinblue 插件



原文:

Installing the Sendinblue Addon

Before getting started, you’ll first need to make sure that WPForms is installed and activated on your WordPress site.
Once you’ve installed WPForms and your license is verified, you’ll be able to quickly install and activate the Sendinblue addon.

Connecting Your Sendinblue Account to WPForms

Once the Sendinblue addon is installed and activated, you’re ready to connect your account. To do this, go to WPForms » Settings and click on the Integrations tab. Then, click on the Sendinblue option to expand its settings. From there, go ahead and click the Add New Account button to enter in your account information. This will reveal two fields where you’ll need to add your Sendinblue account API key, as well as a nickname for the account. To find your API key, first log into your Sendinblue account. Once you’ve logged in, click on the main menu, which is located on the top right corner of the page, and select SMTP & API. Go ahead and copy this value, and head back over to your WPForms settings. You’ll need to paste your API key into the field labeled Sendinblue API Key. Then, add a nickname to the Sendinblue Account Nickname field. This is for internal reference only and can be helpful if you later decide to connect additional Sendinblue accounts. When you’re ready, go ahead and click the Connect to Sendinblue button.

WPForms will now connect your site to your Sendinblue account. When it’s finished connecting, it will show a green Connected status next to your Sendinblue integration.

Adding a Sendinblue Integration to Your Forms

Once you’ve successfully connected your Sendinblue account, you’re ready to link it to a form. To do this, you’ll first need to create a new form or edit an existing form. Within the form builder, go to Marketing » Sendinblue. Then, in the Sendinblue tab, click on the Add New Connection button. Clicking this button will open an overlay where you can provide a nickname for this connection. You can enter any nickname you like, as this is for internal reference only. After naming the connection, additional settings will appear. Select Account: Choose from any of the Sendinblue accounts you’ve connected.

Action to Perform: Select the action you want taken when a user fills out this form. The option selected here will also determine which additional settings are displayed.

Reference Blog
How To Setup Sendinblue Addon With WPForms In 4 Easy Steps
item.author: gecok99160

item.title: 编译器即服务：打字稿实验 | Compiler as a service: An experiment with typescript
item.description:
无缝卸载打字稿编译

我选择了我的 macbook pro 在负载下绝对是蜗牛的问题，主要是因为我想在学习一些东西的同时玩耍和实验。

当然，我的标准 VS Code、终端、浏览器、slack、邮递员和其他一些程序正在运行，但有时我会更改代码中的某些内容，作为响应，macbook 开始准备起飞。

你看我有这个打字稿项目，不是太大，但也不是太小。 我让一个构建任务在后台运行，每当我更改代码时，它都会增量编译代码。

这是通常的 ram 用法：

查找和修复 VS Code ram 使用情况相当容易，但是那个节点进程呢？ 那是我不知道如何解决的问题。 我的意思是它编译的东西，它需要很多果汁，这就是我所知道的。

我不能在这个问题上扔硬件！ 或者我是这么想的。 好吧，即使是这样，我也无法升级我的 RAM 或 CPU。 但是，我工作时确实有一台不错的电脑闲置。 它具有 16G 的 RAM，以及用于 CPU 的 Ryzen 1600。 有趣的是，Ryzen 1600 应该是 6 核处理器，但不知何故，AMD 制造厂搞砸了，发布了一些 8 核的 1600。 不知何故，我很幸运。

问题

问题很简单，使用 PC 编译驻留在我的 macbook 上的代码。

但我已经开始依赖 nodemon 并在更改时自动重新加载。 这是我不想错过的。 所以让我们也加入这个约束。 该解决方案应该像下面所示的那样工作，只要我更改文件并保存它，nodemon 就会重新启动我的服务器：

解决方案

如果我们监听文件系统中的变化，尤其是对特定目录的更改，然后我们创建一个 zip，通过网络将其发送到 PC，PC 对其进行编译，然后发送回一个 zip，我们解压缩它会怎样？ 瞧，我们已经找到了一个可行的解决方案！ 除此之外，我们还有很多滞后！ 这会减慢到破坏整个目的的地步。

我非常相信，如果我想出自己的东西，来回发送代码将是一个瓶颈。 如果我们依靠原生的东西会更好，我的意思是这是其他人必须解决的问题。 当然有人有。 它被称为 NFS（网络文件系统），它相当古老且被广泛使用。

思路很简单，在PC上挂载macbook的磁盘，然后让PC编译。

 +--------------------------+ | +--------------+ | | |Compiler | | | +-+------------+ | | | | | | | | |Compile mounted | | |project | | | | | | | | | | +---v---------+ | | | | +---------+ | | | | +-------------->+ Mounted | | | Macbook | Mount | Project | | | | | | | +---------+ | | | | | | +-------------+ | | | | | | | | | | | | | | PC | +--------------------------+

图1

正如名称Network File System所暗示的那样，它可以让您访问远程机器的磁盘，就像您在本地访问它一样。 这就是它的美妙之处。 我在机器上更改的任何内容都会立即传输到远程机器（忽略一些网络延迟），反之亦然。

解释

图 1阐明了这种简单的架构。 远程机器安装了编译器，要实际编译它，只需编译目录即可。

是的，那个目录是一个挂载的目录，但这没关系！ 尽管如此，它还是一个目录，编译器非常擅长与它交谈。

好吧，好吧，确定编译器可以处理挂载的目录，但这对我们的事业有帮助吗？ 绝对地！

该目录安装在远程机器上，它也有编译器。 远程机器继续向编译器提供一些资源并让它完成工作是非常简单的。

卸载成功！

设置

我尝试了这个解决方案，首先我需要在我的 Mac 上设置 NFS 服务器。 它易于配置，让您可以控制很多事情。

现在我的 macbook 可以安装了，我需要告诉我的 PC 实际安装它。 我的 PC 运行 Ubuntu，因此 PC 的所有命令都是特定于 linux 的。

本质上，我需要使用mount命令并告诉它我的 macbook 的 IP 地址和它共享的目录。

零信任

它使任何设备都可以找到网络上的其他设备并与它们联系。 因此，如果一个设备运行了零配置守护程序，并连接到网络，它就有能力找到其他启用零配置的设备。

但是，“了解”网络上的设备意味着什么？ 答案的一部分是设备的名称，或者更具体地说是主机名。

网络上的每个设备都有一个可以使用的名称，默认情况下其他设备可以通过主机名访问它： <device-name>.local

那么访问不是很正确，就像DNS一样，mDNS也是一个将主机名映射到IP地址的协议。 仅此而已，它将主机名解析为有效的 IP 地址。

一旦你有了IP，你基本上可以做所有你想做的网络。

美妙之处在于，您不必再记住 IP 地址了，如果您的设备重新启动并且您的路由器给它一个不同的 IP 地址，那根本没关系。 我们现在有了设备的主机名，并使用 mDNS 来解析它。

它默认出现在 macOS 中，实际上您可以立即对其进行测试。 如果您有一个服务器正在运行（可以尝试使用python -m SimpleHTTPServer运行），您可以使用该主机名访问它。

设置 NFS

让我们利用新发现的.local域知识继续我们的 NFS 之旅。 在我的设置中，我的 PC 的名称是kaer-morhen ，我的 macbook 的名称是bianco 。

再来一次：

我在 PC 上创建了一个目录为/mnt/bianco 。 如果出现新设备，它将遵循相同的模式。 现在，安装很简单，如前所述，我们将不得不使用 mount 命令，如下所示：

 sudo mount bianco.local:/System/Volumes/Data/Users/iostreamer /mnt/bianco

这有一个限制，即每次启动 PC 时都必须安装它。 为了自动化它，我按照这里提到的步骤在/etc/fstab中创建了一个条目，让操作系统为我处理它。 现在所有的安装工作都是在幕后自动完成的！

瞧！ 每当 PC 启动时，我们的 macbook 将自动安装在 PC 上。

设置编译器

对于这个特定的项目，我需要 typescript 编译器。 安装它很简单：

要编译项目，只需在项目目录中运行命令tsc 。 这会解析您的 tsconfig.json，获取源目录，编译文件并将它们放在输出目录中。 但是我们还需要它来监视对源目录所做的更改，然后在有任何更改时重新编译。 这可以通过在监视模式下编译来完成： tsc -w 。 这会阻塞你的 shell 并开始观察变化。

这是我编写的脚本，它通过SSHs到远程机器，转到相应的目录并在监视模式下开始编译。 我将此脚本命名为rcw （远程编译监视）。 它基本上将当前工作目录作为字符串提取，并替换文件夹名称，使其与远程机器上的挂载文件夹匹配：

 #!/usr/bin/env zsh cwd=`pwd` cwd="${cwd/Users/mnt}" // Syntax for replacing substrings cwd="${cwd/iostreamer/bianco}" ssh -tt iostreamer@kaer-morhen.local "cd $cwd && tsc -w"

让我们尝试运行它，更改文件并查看是否自动编译更改：

瞧，它不起作用！

我们错过了什么？ 我们错过了什么吗？ 让我们来了解一下！

问题

为什么它不起作用？ 我们已经正确安装，它甚至可以正确编译，甚至可以在本地查看编译后的文件。 那么是什么阻止它对文件所做的更改做出反应呢？

好吧，坦率地说，它确实有效。 根据我的经验（YMMV），这只是笨拙而缓慢。

什么是inotify？

那么inotify是一个 Linux API/Sub 系统。 每当对目录或其文件进行更改时，您都可以要求此 API 通知您。 当您运行tsc -w时，即在监视模式下编译时，这正是 typescript 编译器所做的。 它设置了一个观察者，当源目录发生变化时会通知它。

在我们的例子中，运行在 linux(PC) 上的 NFS 客户端是处理由 macbook 完成的更改的客户端。 这些变化都是立竿见影的，非常迅速。 您可以通过在本地创建文件然后SSHing到远程计算机并使用watch命令以假设 0.1 秒的间隔查看文件的内容来进行验证。 只要您在本地更改文件的内容，您就会看到它立即通过watch命令得到反映。

问题似乎出在 NFS 客户端和 inotify 上。 并不是说它不起作用，而是根据我的经验，它很慢，以至于你不能依赖它。 据我观察，文件内容立即更改，但 NFS 客户端需要时间联系 inotify。 最终它会，但谁愿意等呢？

解决方案：第 2 部分

谷歌搜索 5 分钟后，我发现了一个名为notify-forwarder 的有趣项目。 它的工作原理非常简单，手动转发。

这是一个在 2 种模式下工作的程序：

  • 手表
    • 在监视模式下工作时，它会在本地侦听更改并将这些更改传输到指定的机器。
    • 观看时，必须指定远程ip，以及远程目录路径。
  • 收到
    • 在接收模式下工作时，它只是侦听对目录所做的更改。

更准确地说，这个程序只是告诉文件已经做了some更改。 并且在监听事件时（接收模式），它模拟一个ATTRIB事件。 当文件的属性发生更改时会触发此事件。

明显的缺点是，并非所有编译器/构建系统都尊重ATTRIB事件。

值得庆幸的是，打字稿可以。

现在，我们所要做的就是在我的 PC 上以接收模式运行这个程序。 然后在本地观看模式。 对于 PC，我不想手动运行它，所以我创建了一个服务，可以由systemd处理。

注意：我之前没有使用 systemd 服务的经验，并按照本指南创建它。

在接收模式下运行的脚本

脚本的 .service 文件

[Unit] Description=Notify Forwarder [Service] Type=simple ExecStart=/bin/bash /usr/local/bin/notify_receive [Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target

对于我的 macbook，我需要一个在监视模式下运行notify-forwarder的脚本，监视当前目录并将更改发送到 PC（kaer-morhen）。 我将其命名为nk （通知 Kaer-Morhen）

这是相同的脚本：

 ip=`dscacheutil -q host -a name kaer-morhen.local | grep ip_address | awk -v FS=': ' '{print $2}'` cwd=`pwd` cwd="${cwd/Users/mnt}" cwd="${cwd/iostreamer/bianco}" notify-forwarder watch -c $ip . $cwd

这就是当它们结合在一起时的样子：

结论

这是一天的hack，我学到了很多东西！ 但是与这一学习相比，所有这些都消失了。 你看，在开始之前，我认为顺利完成这样的事情需要一些天才级别的黑客或代码。

但它没有。 我只是从一个问题跌跌撞撞到另一个问题，直到我设法将一个部分优雅，部分混乱的解决方案拼凑在一起。 这绝对不是天才级别的代码。

完成它>等待学习一些天赐的工具并创造完美的东西。

未来

这绝对不是Compiler as a Service产品，我怀疑它会持续很长时间。 但这不会阻止我在家中将其货币化。 我的室友有类似的设置，我正在考虑一个类似 AWS 的模型，其中 1 CPU 分钟 = 他们洗 2 个盘子。



原文:

Seamlessly offloading typescript compilation

I picked the problem of my macbook pro being an absolute snail under load, mostly because I wanted to play around and experiment while learning something.

Sure I got my standard VS Code, a terminal, a browser, slack, postman and some other programs running, but there comes a time when I change something in my code and in response the macbook starts preparing for a lift off.

You see I have this typescript project, not too big, but not too small either. I keep a build task running in background which incrementally compiles code whenever I change it.

And here is the usual ram usage:

Looking up and fixing VS Code ram usage is fairly easy, but what about that node process? That’s the one I have no idea how to fix. I mean it compiles stuff and it takes a lot of juice, that’s all I know.

I can’t throw hardware at this problem! Or so I thought. Well I can’t upgrade my RAM or my CPU even for that matter. But, I do have nice PC sitting idle when I work. It has 16G of RAM, and a Ryzen 1600 for CPU. Fun fact, Ryzen 1600 is supposed to be a 6-core processor but somehow AMD manafacturing plants messed up and released a few 1600s with 8 cores. And somehow I got lucky.

The problem

The problem is straightforward, use the PC to compile the code residing on my macbook.

But I have come to depend on nodemon and auto reload on change. And that’s something I don’t want to miss out on. So let’s throw in this constraint as well. The solution should work as good as shown below where nodemon restarts my server as soon as I change a file and save it:

The solution

What if we listened for changes in our filesystem, especially if done to a particular directory, and then we create a zip, send it to PC over network, the PC compiles it, and sends back a zip, we unzip it? Voila, we have got ourselves a working solution! And along with that we have got ourselves a whole lot of lag! This would slow down to the point of defeating the whole purpose.

I am quite convinced that sending code back and forth will be a bottleneck if I come up with something of my own. It’d be better if we lean on something native, I mean this is a problem someone else must have solved. And sure someone had. It’s called NFS(Network File system), and it’s quite old and widely used.

The idea is simple, mount macbook’s disk on PC and then ask PC to compile.

                          +--------------------------+
                          | +--------------+         |
                          | |Compiler      |         |
                          | +-+------------+         |
                          |   |                      |
                          |   |                      |
                          |   |Compile mounted       |
                          |   |project               |
                          |   |                      |
                          |   |                      |
                          |   |                      |
                          +---v---------+            |
                          |             |            |
+---------+               |             |            |
|         +-------------->+ Mounted     |            |
| Macbook |   Mount       | Project     |            |
|         |               |             |            |
+---------+               |             |            |
                          |             |            |
                          +-------------+            |
                          |                          |
                          |                          |
                          |                          |
                          |                          |
                          |                          |
                          |                          |
                          |      PC                  |
                          +--------------------------+

Figure 1

And as the name Network File System suggests, it lets you access remote machines’ disk as if you are accessing it locally. And that’s the beauty of it. Anything I change on my machine, gets transmitted to the remote machine instantly(well ignoring some network latency) and vice versa.

Explaination

Figure 1 sheds some light on this simple architecture. The remote machine has the compiler installed, and to actually compile it, all that needs to be done is compile the directory.

And yes, that directory is a mounted one but that doesn’t matter! It’s a directory nonetheless and the compiler is well versed in talking to it.

Well, ok, sure the compiler can handle a mounted directory but does it help our cause? Absolutely!

The directory is mounted on remote machine, and it has the compiler as well. It’s pretty straightforward for the remote machine to go ahead and provide some resources to the compiler and let it do its job.

Offloading succesfull!

Setup

I tried this solution and first I needed to setup NFS server on my mac. NFS Manager is a nice little free tool that came handy. It’s easy to configure and lets you control a lot of things.

Now that my macbook is mountable, I needed to tell my PC to actually mount it. My PC runs a Ubuntu, so all the commands for PC will be linux specific.

In essence, I need to use the mount command and tell it the IP address of my macbook and the directory it’s sharing.

Zero Conf

It enables any device to find other devices on the network and contact them. So if a device has the zero-conf daemon running, and is brought on to a network, it has the capability to find other zero-conf enabled devices.

But what does it mean to ‘know’ devices on the network? Well a part of the answer is the name of the device, or more specifically the hostname.

mDNS(Multicast DNS) is another service on which Zero Conf relies on and it powers the hostname resolution for connected devices.

Each device on the network has a name it can use, and by default other devices can access it through the hostname: <device-name>.local

Well access is not quite right, just like DNS, mDNS is also a protocol which maps hostnames to IP addressess. And that’s all it does, it resolves the hostname into a valid IP address.

Once you have the IP you can basically do all the networking you want to do.

The beauty is, you don’t have to remeber ip addresses anymore, if your device restarts and your router gives it a different IP address, it simply doesn’t matter. We have a hostname for the device now, and mDNS to resolve it.

It comes by default in macOS, and in fact you can test it right away. <your-laptop's-name>.local is the hostname of your macbook. And if you have a server running(can try running with python -m SimpleHTTPServer), you can access it using that hostname.

Setting up NFS

Let’s continue our NFS journey with this newfound knowledge of .local domains. In my setup, the name of my PC is kaer-morhen and my macbook is named bianco.

Once again:

PC/Remote machine = kaer-morhen

Macbook = bianco

I have created a directory on PC as /mnt/bianco. If a new device comes up it’ll follow the same pattern. Now, mounting is simple, as discussed before, we will have to use the mount command, like this:

sudo mount bianco.local:/System/Volumes/Data/Users/iostreamer /mnt/bianco

This comes with a limitation that one’d have to mount it everytime I boot the PC. To automate it, I followed the steps mentioned here and created an entry in /etc/fstab and let the OS handle it for me. Now all that mounting stuff is done behind the scenes automatically!

Voila! Our macbook will be automatically mounted on PC whenever the PC boots.

Setting up Compiler

For this particular project, I need the typescript compiler. Installing it is as simple as:

To compile the project all one needs to do is run the command tsc in the project directory. This parses your tsconfig.json, picks up source directories, compiles file and puts them in the output directory. But we also need it to watch for changes done to the source directories and then recompile if anything changes. That can be done with compiling in watch mode: tsc -w. This would block your shell and start watching for changes.

Here’s a script I wrote which SSHs into the remote machine, goes to the appropriate directory and starts compilation in watch mode. I named this script rcw(Remote compile watch). It basically picks up the current working directory as string, and replaces folder names such that it matches the mount folder on remote machine:

#!/usr/bin/env zsh

cwd=`pwd`
cwd="${cwd/Users/mnt}" // Syntax for replacing substrings
cwd="${cwd/iostreamer/bianco}"
ssh -tt iostreamer@kaer-morhen.local "cd $cwd && tsc -w"

Let’s try to run it, change the files and see if automatically compiles the changes or not:

Lo and behold, it doesn’t work!

What did we miss? Did we miss something? Let’s find out!

The catch

Why did it not work? We have mounted correctly, it even compiles properly and one can even see the compiled files locally. Then what’s stopping it to react to changes done to files?

Well, to be blunt, it does work. It’s just clunk and slow in my experience(YMMV).

What is inotify?

Well inotify is a Linux API/ Sub system. You can ask this API to tell you whenever a change is done to a directory or its files. When you run tsc -w, i.e. compile in watch mode, that’s exactly what the typescript compiler does. It sets up a watcher, which is notified when source directories change.

In our case, the NFS client running on linux(PC) is the one handling changes done by macbook. And these changes are all immediate, very snappy. You can verify by creating a file locally and then SSHing in to remote machine and using the watch command to see the contents of the file at let’s say an interval of 0.1s. As soon as you change the contents of the file locally, you’d see it getting reflected immediately through the watch command.

The issue seems to be with NFS client and inotify. It’s not that it doesn’t work, but in my experience it’s quite slow, to the extend you can’t depend on it. From what I observed, the content of the file change immediately but the NFS client takes its time to contact inotify. Eventually it does, but who wants to wait?

The solution: Part 2

After Googling for like 5 minutes I came across this interesting project called notify-forwarder. It works on a very simply principle, forward it manually.

It’s a program which works in 2 modes:

  • watch
    • When working in watch mode, it listens for changes locally and transmits those changes to the specified machine.
    • When watching, you have to specify the remote ip, as well as the remote directory path.
  • receive
    • When working in receive mode, it simply listens for changes done to the directory.

To be more precise, this program simply tells some change has been done to a file. And when listening for events(receive mode), it simulates an ATTRIB event. This event is fired when an attribute of the file is changed.

And the obvious drawback is that, not all compilers/build systems respect ATTRIB events.

Thankfully, typescript does.

Now, all we have to do is run this program in receive mode on my PC. And then in watch mode locally. For PC, I didn’t want to run it manually, so I created a service, which can be handled by systemd.

Note: I had no prior experience with systemd services and followed this guide to create it.

Script to run in receive mode

.service file for the script

[Unit]
Description=Notify Forwarder

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/bin/bash /usr/local/bin/notify_receive

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

For my macbook, I needed a script which runs notify-forwarder in watch mode, watches the current directory and sends the changes to PC(kaer-morhen). I named it nk (Notify Kaer-Morhen)

Here’s the script for same:

ip=`dscacheutil -q host -a name kaer-morhen.local | grep ip_address | awk -v FS=': ' '{print $2}'`
cwd=`pwd`
cwd="${cwd/Users/mnt}"
cwd="${cwd/iostreamer/bianco}"
notify-forwarder watch -c $ip . $cwd

And this is how it looks when it all comes together:

Conclusion

This was a day’s hack and I got to learn so much! But all of it fades when compared to this one learning. You see, before starting I thought achieving something like this smoothly would need some genius level hacks or code.

But it did not. I just stumbled from one problem to another till I managed to hack together a part elegant, a part messy solution. This is definitely not a genius level code.

Getting it done > Waiting to learn some godsent tools and creating that perfect thing.

Future

This is definitely not a Compiler as a Service product and I doubt it’s going to be for a long time. But that’s not going to stop me from monetising it in my home. My roommate has a similar setup, and I am thinking of an AWS like model where 1 CPU minute = 2 dishes they wash.
item.author: iostreamer

item.title: Emir Marlo 代表乌克兰参加 Djooky 奖 | Emir Marlo Represents Ukraine at Djooky Awards
item.description:
由麦当娜和比莉·艾利什主办的每年在美国国会唱片公司举办的 Djooky 音乐奖的激动人心的活动已经开始。

令 Djooky Awards 土耳其追随者兴奋的消息来自乌克兰。 今年，乌克兰将由土耳其裔埃米尔马洛代表。 将与他的歌曲“Voices”竞争的埃米尔已经成为 Djooky 的最爱之一。 由于俄罗斯对乌克兰发动战争，世界许多地方继续提供支持。 这位年轻的歌手通过“声音”引起了人们对战争结束和乌克兰不可分割的领土完整的关注。 这首歌将于 7 月与环球音乐集团和 Spinnup 合作发行，已经引起了人们的好奇。 用美好的时刻填满你的生活，做你自己，意识到你生命中的每一刻。 时光荏苒，生命是我们拥有的最重要的东西。 这就是为什么我们必须珍惜我们的生命，并按照我们认为正确的方式生活。 美国见！

埃米尔马洛是谁？

埃米尔·阿塔奥卢 (Emir Ataoğlu) 于 2005 年 1 月 16 日出生于伊斯坦布尔，继续在伊斯坦布尔的一所高中接受教育。 他对音乐的热情从很小的时候开始接受吉他训练，随后这位在家里制作业余歌曲的年轻歌手与 Djooky 一起完成了他的职业处子秀。 这位年轻的歌手还喜欢打排球（他在 COVID-19 大流行开始时就开始爱好排球）。



原文:

The excitement of the Djooky Music Awards, held every year in the USA at Capitol Records, hosted by Madonna and Billie Eilish, has begun.

The news that excited the Turkish followers of Djooky Awards came from Ukraine. This year, Ukraine will be represented by Emir Marlo of Turkish origin. Emir, who will compete with his song "Voices", has already become one of the Djooky favourites. Emir Marlo is at the top of Europe's "DJOOKY HITS" list, is also the youngest competitor in the history of Djooky Awards.

Due to the war launched by Russia against Ukraine, support continues from many parts of the world. The young singer draws attention to the end of the war and the inseparable territorial integrity of Ukraine with "Voices". The song, which will be released in partnership with Universal Music Group and Spinnup in July, has already managed to be a matter of curiosity. “Voices”, the singer's call to live to the fullest; fill your life with beautiful moments, be yourself and be conscious of every moment of your life. Time flies and life is the most important thing we have. That is why we must value our lives and live as we believe is right. See you in the USA!

Who is Emir Marlo?

Emir Ataoğlu, born on January 16, 2005 in Istanbul, continues his education in an high school in Istanbul. His passion for music started with guitar training at an early age, and then the young singer, who made amateur songs at home, made his professional debut with Djooky. The young singer also enjoys playing Volleyball (which he started as a hobby at the start of the COVID-19 pandemic).
item.author: davidssink

item.title: 富达在元界推出多层次学习中心 | Fidelity Launches Multi-Level Learning Center in Metaverse
item.description:
富达进入元界

富达投资周四宣布“The Fidelity Stack”盛大开幕，这家经纪公司将其描述为“旨在提供一种学习投资基础知识的新方式的第一个沉浸式虚拟世界体验”。 富达是最大的金融服务公司之一； 它目前管理着 11.3 万亿美元的资产。

一整层楼专门提供有关富达元界 ETF (FMET) 的信息，这是该公司专注于元界投资的新交易所交易基金。 富达解释说：

建于 Decentraland 的 Fidelity Stack 采用多层设计，包括大厅、舞池和屋顶空中花园，供用户步行甚至通过传送探索。

富达营销渠道和新兴平台负责人 Kathryn Condon 评论道：

无论是通过区块链技术的兴起还是新数字世界的发展，我们相互交往和使用金钱的方式都在迅速变化。 我们进军元宇宙就是考虑到这一点。

上个月，花旗预测，到 2030 年，元界可能会带来 13 万亿美元的机会，拥有 50 亿用户。全球投资银行高盛和摩根士丹利认为，元界是一个 8 万亿美元的机会。

Source 来源



原文:

Fidelity Investments, a major financial services firm with $11.3 trillion in assets under administration, has entered the metaverse with the opening of an eight-story learning center and the launch of a metaverse exchange-traded fund (ETF). The Fidelity Stack features “a multi-level design complete with a lobby, dance floor, and rooftop sky garden for users to explore on foot – or even through teleport.”

Fidelity Enters the Metaverse

Fidelity Investments announced Thursday the grand opening of “The Fidelity Stack,” which the brokerage firm described as its “first immersive metaverse experience aimed at offering a new way to learn investing basics.” Fidelity is one of the largest financial services firms; it currently has $11.3 trillion in assets under administration.

The Fidelity Stack is an eight-story building in the metaverse where visitors can learn about different ways of investing. One whole floor is dedicated to providing information about Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET), the firm’s new exchange-traded fund focusing on metaverse investments. Fidelity explained:

The Fidelity Stack, built in Decentraland, features a multi-level design complete with a lobby, dance floor, and rooftop sky garden for users to explore on foot – or even through teleport.

“In the Invest Quest at The Fidelity Stack, users are challenged to traverse the building learning the basics of ETF investing while gathering ‘orbs’ along the way,” the announcement continues.

Decentraland is an Ethereum-based metaverse opened to the public in January 2020. In February, global investment bank JPMorgan chose Decentraland as the metaverse platform to open its lounge.

Kathryn Condon, head of marketing channels and emerging platforms at Fidelity, commented:

The way we engage with each other and with our money is rapidly changing, whether that’s through the rise of blockchain technology or development of a new digital universe. Our foray into the metaverse is designed with this in mind.

Last month, Citi predicted that the metaverse could be a $13 trillion opportunity with five billion users by the year 2030. Global investment banks Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley believe that the metaverse is an $8 trillion opportunity.

Source
item.author: coinparative

item.title: 使用 2 台显示器进行学习和工作 | Use 2 monitors for learning and work
item.description:
在封面图片上，我在第一台显示器上观看 youtube 教程，同时在第二台显示器上编写代码。

自从 3 年前我买了一个额外的显示器插入我的笔记本电脑，我无法想象没有它我自己会做任何严肃的事情。

作为一名 JavaScript 全栈开发人员，我总是学习新事物（前端、后端、移动、桌面、Chrome 扩展、报废、云部署等）

无论是有偿工作还是副业，工作流程大致相同。 为了感觉舒服，我需要同时在全屏 2 个应用程序中保持打开状态。

用例

  1. 我观看视频教程以了解有关新主题的基本想法，并在导师之后重复所有步骤

    • 第一台显示器 – youtube 视频
    • 第二个监视器 - 代码编辑器

  2. 我阅读文档/书籍并在思维导图中做笔记，以深入理解材料。

    • 第一台显示器 – 文档站点
    • 第二台显示器——思维导图编辑器

  3. 我编码

    • 第一个监视器 - 编码结果（网页或移动应用程序模拟器）
    • 第二个监视器 - 代码编辑器

当您只有一台显示器时，您应该：

  • 在全屏窗口/应用程序之间切换或
  • 应用程序之间的分屏

在这两种情况下，闪烁的全屏应用程序或管理部分屏幕打开的应用程序的宽度不足会占用您大量的认知能量，并且您会更快地感到疲倦。 此外，您可能会伤害您的眼睛。

所以两个显示器是解决方案。 同时打开的两个全屏应用程序让您在管理信息、吸收新知识和轻松完成工作方面拥有巨大优势。

例如，这就是我使用 2 个监视器对 NextJS 文档进行思维导图的方式：

硬件设置

如果你在谷歌上搜索“多显示器”之类的东西——然后查看图像，你就会对它们的设置产生错误的认识，因为很多图像都是关于水平设置的，这会在一天之内扼杀你的脖子。 相反，您应该选择垂直设置——这样您就可以将眼睛从一台显示器切换到另一台显示器，而不会拉伤脖子。

你不需要一个棘手的小工具架——任何大小合适的罐子或盒子都是合适的。 我多年来一直使用宜家的 Pyssla 罐子作为显示器的支架，它非常好：

操作系统设置

就像谷歌搜索一样，操作系统会将您推向水平设置。 但是，您可以更改桌面设置中的位置。 当操作系统设置跟随现实生活中显示器的位置时很好，这样您就可以直观地工作。

没钱买吗？

即使你是穷人：学生、转行、初入职场、缺钱，我强烈建议你尽快购买便宜的二手显示器，因为它有时会提高你的效率。

也许你已经注意到我的第一张照片：我的显示器很旧，有两条彩色垂直线的缺陷。 我的笔记本电脑很旧，没有钥匙“；/：”。 所以我什至羞于向你展示它们。 但它们一起是学习和工作的酷工具。 我更喜欢这样的一对，而不是我拥有的 mackbook。



原文:

On the cover image I'm watching youtube tutorial on 1st monitor and simultaneously write code in 2nd monitor.

Since I bought an extra monitor to plug into my laptop 3 years ago, I can’t imagine myself doing anything serious without it.

As a JavaScript full-stack developer I always learn new things (Frontend, Backend, Mobile, Desktop, Chrome extensions, Scrapping, Cloud deployment etc.)

Whether it’s a paid job or a side project, the working process is generally the same. And to feel comfortable I need to keep opened in full screen 2 apps at the same time.

Use cases

  1. I watch a video tutorial to get basic ideas about the new subject, and repeat all steps after the tutor

    • 1st monitor – youtube video
    • 2nd monitor – code editor

  2. I read documentation/book and take notes in mindmap for deep understanding of material.

    • 1st monitor – documentation site
    • 2nd monitor – mindmap editor

  3. I code

    • 1st monitor – result of coding (web page or mobile app emulator)
    • 2nd monitor – code editor

When you have only 1 monitor, you should either:

  • switch between full-screen windows/apps or
  • split screen between apps

In both cases, blinking full screen apps or managing lack of width in part screen opened apps will take a lot of your cognitive energy, and you get tired much faster. Also, you can harm your eyes.

So two monitors is the solution. Simultaneously opened, two full-screen apps give you a big advantage to manage information, absorb new knowledge and do your work with fewer efforts.

Example, that's how I mindmaped NextJS docs using 2 monitors:

Hardware setup

If you google something like “multiple monitors” – and look at images, you get the wrong idea about their setup, because lots of images are about Horizontal setup, which will kill your neck in one day. Instead, you should choose a vertical setup – so you can switch your eyes from one monitor to another without straining your neck.

You don’t need for that a tricky gadget holder – any jar or box with proper size is suitable. I use an Ikea's Pyssla jar as a stand for monitor for years, and it is pretty good:

Operational System setup

Just as google search, OS will push you to horizontal setup. However, you can change positioning in desktop settings. That relative position of 1 to 2 affects on how mouse cursor will be moving between monitors. It is good when OS settings follow along with real life monitors’ position, so you will work intuitively.

Have not money for it?

Even if you are a poor: student, career switcher, beginner in job, with lack of money, I highly recommend you to buy even a cheap second hand monitor as soon as possible, because it will increase your efficiency at times.

Maybe you have noticed on the first picture with me: my monitor is old, with defect of two colored vertical lines. And my laptop is old without a key ";/:". So I even ashamed to show them to you. But together they are a cool tool for study and work. I prefer such couple over mackbook that I have.
item.author: apayrus

item.title: 技术团队职业规划的 5 个阶段 | 5 Stages of Career Planning for a Tech Team
item.description:
这个故事最初发表在Vectorly 的博客上。

如果您想知道如何编写职业发展计划以及职业发展计划的阶段是什么，本文将帮助您找到答案。 这是软件工程师职业发展大主题的第二部分，您将在其中找到：

阅读本文后，工程经理或技术主管将能够创建一个符合其公司目标的职业发展框架，以及建立一支高绩效团队并留住顶尖工程师。

之前，我们讲述了什么是软件工程师职业发展框架以及为什么公司需要为其公司实施工程师职业阶梯。

职业发展定义

软件工程师职业发展是一个系统化的职业发展路线图，包括技能、成就、期望和职业未来的愿景。 为简化起见，职业阶梯描绘了个人的职业进步。

有不同类型的职业发展框架示例，具体取决于公司的规模及其目标。

使用职业阶梯模板，经理可以轻松创建自己的职业发展框架。 然而，如果他或她想激励顶尖工程师、留住最优秀的人才、专业地培养工程师，仅仅建立一个良好的职业发展框架并实施它是不够的。

职业发展规划的5个阶段

一个好的管理者应该让职业发展规划成为一个持续的过程。 这就是为什么：

  • 首先，经理应定期进行评审，以检查团队的技能如何满足其当前等级（和期望水平）的要求，并监控其专业发展的进度。

  • 其次，职业发展框架应至少每年更新一次，因此其中包含的要求和技能与市场相关。

此周期还包括会议日程、议程和必备工具包。

以下是职业规划的五个阶段：

  1. 建立职业发展框架
  2. 检查进度并调整 PDP
  3. 做出提升等级的决定
  4. 检查进度并调整 PDP
  5. 更新职业发展框架

现在，让我们深入研究细节并获取上述每个阶段的议程和工具。

建立职业发展框架

在这个阶段，经理创建一个职业阶梯并在公司内实施。 这可能是职业规划过程中最耗时、最困难的阶段。 然而，Vectorly 已经准备了一份关于如何构建软件工程师职业发展框架的分步指南，它将帮助您创建自己的职业阶梯框架，使用模板并根据公司的需求对其进行自定义。

作为这个阶段的结果，队友将清楚地了解他们的职业道路以及他们应该发展哪些技能，以便获得升职。

简而言之，在这个阶段，经理需要：

  • 创建技能列表
  • 设置成绩
  • 将技能映射到成绩
  • 举行一对一会议以制定 PDP 计划

工具包：

有关更多详细信息，请查看我们关于如何构建软件工程师职业发展框架的分步指南。

检查进度并调整 PDP

在实施职业发展框架并建立 PDP 计划后，需要进行重复的技能审查，以检查员工在职业发展计划中发展重点技能的进度。 此外，在此一对一会议期间，经理应更新当前的 PDP。

因此，在这个阶段，经理需要：

  • 进行审查以评估当前的技能水平
  • 对 PDP 进行调整

工具包：

  • 一对一模板
  • 成长计划

该会议应定期举行，如图所示 - Q1 和 Q4。

做出提升等级的决定

在建立职业发展框架六个月后，是时候就升职做出决定了。 经理应该进行审查，以了解员工在培养所需职位所需技能方面的成功程度。 在此审查分析的基础上，经理和 HR 将决定谁将获得晋升和加薪。

通过这样做，决策对所有相关方（工程师、团队领导和 HR）都是透明的。

综上所述，在这个阶段，经理需要：

  • 进行审查以评估当前的技能水平
  • 保持1：1讨论水平和加薪

工具包：

  • 一对一模板

应该定期做出关于升职的决定 - 一年两次（图片上的第一季度和第三季度）。

检查进度并调整 PDP

此阶段重复 #2，因为这是经理检查员工如何实施当前 PDP 并对其进行必要更改的时间。

经理需要：

  • 进行审查以评估当前的技能水平
  • 对 PDP 进行调整

工具包：

  • 一对一模板
  • 成长计划

该会议应定期举行，如图所示 - Q1 和 Q4。

更新职业发展框架

在职业阶梯建立并成功实施到公司的工作中之后，经理的目标是对框架进行调整。 工程师的技能矩阵和职业路径应该每年更新一次，以确保团队的职业发展目标与当前业务目标相关，符合行业要求。

因此，每年一次，经理需要：

  • 更新技能列表
  • 更新成绩
  • 将更新的技能映射到成绩
  • 保持 1:1 的专业发展和讨论水平提升

工具包：

综上所述，职业规划成功的关键因素是规律性。 工程师职业发展框架的实施过程包含一组定期会议，它们是：

每年一次：创建/更新职业发展框架
每六个月一次：决定加薪和升职，并创建新的 PDP
每三个月一次：进行审查以评估技能并对当前 PDP 进行调整

这个 5 阶段的职业规划周期将有助于使技术团队的每个成员的专业发展系统化，为工程师提供公司内部的职业发展机会，以及对所有相关方透明地做出晋升和加薪决策。

仍然对如何为您的团队创建职业发展和组织职业规划有疑问？ 不用担心 - Vectorly 专家会在25 分钟的免费电话中帮助您解决问题。

这个故事最初发表在Vectorly 的博客上。



原文:

The story was originally published on Vectorly’s blog.

If you’re wondering how to write a career progression plan and what are the stages of career progression planning, this article will help you find the answers. This is the second part of the big topic on software engineer career progression, in which you’ll find:

After reading the article, an engineering manager or a tech lead will be able to create a career progression framework that meets the goals of their company, as well as build a high-performing team and retain top engineers.

Previously, we told about what is a software engineer career progression framework and why do companies need to implement an engineer career ladder for their company.

Career progression definition

Software engineer career progression is a systematized career growth roadmap, which inсludes skills, achievements, expectations and vision of a career future. To simplify, the career ladder charts individual career progress.

There are different types of career progression framework examples, depending on the size of a company and its goals.

Using a career ladder template, a manager can easily create their own career progression framework. However, if he or she wants to motivate top engineers, retain the best talent, and develop engineers professionally, it’s not enough to just build a good career progression framework and implement it.

5 stages of career progression planning

A good manager should make career progression planning a continuous process. And that’s why:

  • First of all, a manager should regularly hold reviews to check how the skills of a team meet the requirements of their current grades (and desired levels) and monitor the progress of their professional development.

  • Secondly, the career progression framework should be updated at least once a year, so the requirements and skills included in it are relevant to the market.

Vectorly has come up with a 5-stage career progression planning process. This cycle also includes a meetings schedule, with an agenda and a must-have toolkit.

Here are five stages of career planning:

  1. Build a career progression framework
  2. Check progress & make adjustments to PDP
  3. Make decisions on level raise
  4. Check progress & make adjustments to PDP
  5. Update the career progression framework

Now, let’s dive into details and get the agenda and the tools for each of the stages mentioned above.

#1. Build a career progression framework

At this stage, a manager creates a career ladder and implements it in the company. This is probably the most time-consuming and difficult stage of the career planning process. Yet Vectorly has prepared a Step-by-Step Guide on How to Build a Software Engineer Career Progression Framework, which will help you to create your own career ladder framework, using templates and customizing them to the needs of your company.

As a result of this stage, teammates will get a clear understanding of their career path and what skills they should develop, in order to get a grade raise.

In short, at this stage, a manager needs to:

  • Create a list of skills
  • Set grades
  • Map skills to grades
  • Hold 1-on-1 meetings to build a PDP plan

Toolkit:

For more detailed info, check out our Step-by-Step Guide on How to Build a Software Engineer Career Progression Framework.

#2. Check progress & make adjustments to PDP

After the career progression framework has been implemented and a PDP plan has been built, a repeated skill review needs to be held, in order to check the progress of an employee on developing the focus skills from a professional development plan. Also, during this 1-on-1 meeting, a manager should make updates to the current PDP.

So, at this stage, a manager needs to:

  • Hold a review to assess the current level of skills
  • Make adjustments to PDP

Toolkit:

  • 360-degree review
  • 1-on-1 templates
  • Growth plan

This meeting should be held regularly, as you can see in the picture - Q1 and Q4.

#3. Make decisions on level raise

Six months after building a career progression framework, it’s time to make decisions on grade raises. A manager should hold a review, in order to see how well employees have succeeded in developing the required skills for their desired position. On the basis of this review analysis, the manager and HR will make decisions about who will get promoted and get a pay raise.

By doing this, the decisions are transparent to all the parties involved - engineers, team leads, and HR.

To sum up, at this stage, a manager needs to:

  • Hold a review to assess the current level of skills
  • Hold 1:1 to discuss level and pay raise

Toolkit:

  • 360-degree review
  • 1-on-1 templates

Decisions on grade raise should be made regularly - twice a year (Q1 and Q3 on the picture).

#4. Check progress & make adjustments to PDP

This stage repeats #2, as this is the time when a manager checks how the current PDP is being implemented by an employee and makes necessary changes to it.

A manager needs to:

  • Hold a review to assess the current level of skills
  • Make adjustments to PDP

Toolkit:

  • 360-degree review
  • 1-on-1 templates
  • Growth plan

This meeting should be held regularly, as you can see in the picture - Q1 and Q4.

#5. Update the career progression framework

After a career ladder has been built and successfully implemented into the work of a company, a manager’s goal is to make adjustments to the framework. Skill matrices and career paths of engineers should be updated once a year, in order to make sure that the team’s professional development goals are relevant to the current business goals and meet the requirements of the industry.

So once a year, a manager needs to:

  • Update the list of skills
  • Update grades
  • Map the updated skills to grades
  • Hold 1:1s on professional development & discuss level raises

Toolkit:

To sum up, the key factor of successful career planning is regularity. The process of implementation of the engineer career progression framework contains a set of regular meetings, which are:

Once a year: create/update a career progression framework
Once every six months: make decisions on pay and grade raise, and create new PDP
Once every three months: hold a review to assess the skills and make adjustments to the current PDP

This 5-stage career planning cycle will help to make the professional development of every member of a tech team systematic, provide engineers with career development opportunities within the company, as well as make decisions on promotions and pay raise transparent to all parties involved.

Still have questions on how to create a career progression and organize career planning for your team? Don’t worry - Vectorly expert will help you to figure it out on a free 25-min call.

The story was originally published on Vectorly’s blog.

item.title: 4 /100DaysOfFlutter : Dart：枚举、类和对象 | 4 /100DaysOfFlutter : Dart: Enumerations, Classes and Objects
item.description:
这条线将被说成名为技能的字符串列表具有游泳跑空手道的值； 您也可以使用自己的阅读方式和支持方式。

对于多种类型的元素，我们可以在列表中使用称为对象的东西。

 class Insan extends LivingThings { String name ; int age ; Insan ( this . name , this . age ); }

现在我可以使用这些数据类型来创建具有这些数据类型的自己的变量。

 Insan Abhay = new Insan ( "Abhay" , 19 );

和之前一样，Insan 是变量的类型，Abhay 是变量名就像，这里我们同时使用 String 和 int 在 Class 中传递参数

对象：对象提供了使变量成为我们创建的类的能力，

实际上发生的任何事情，区域，内存都是一个对象，因为上面创建的名为Abhay的变量将在内存中发生，然后它是一个对象，对象的类型也可以是您自己的类型，例如 Insan

 Insan Abhay = new Insan ( "Abhay" , 19 );

对象也称为实例； 您可以自行检查，只需打印变量名称

print ( Abhay ); //Instance of 'Insan'

制作类有助于我们制作更好的对象； 类是在函数之外创建的

检查代码here with example press 。 并在浏览器中打开并开始练习。

如果在某个时候没有，请随时在推特上联系我@abhayprajapati_

好的，再见。



原文:

This line wiil be spoken as List of String named skills has values Swiming running Karate; you can also use your own way of reading and pro-nounching it.

List can also be called as array.

a List can contain only one data type of element.
for mutliple type of element we can use something called as Objects in the list.

class Insan extends LivingThings { String name; int age; Insan(this.name, this.age);
}

Now I can use this data types to make own variables with this data types.

 Insan Abhay = new Insan("Abhay", 19);

as before Insan is the type of variable, Abhay is the variable name 🆕 key word is for before it is new Variables made which has combination of data types/ mostly new key used when for a single variables we use mutilple dataytypes. Like, here we are using String and int at the same time passing the parameter's in the Class

Objects: Objects gives ability to make the variables the classes we make,

in reality anything that takes place, area, memory is a Objects as the Variable named Abhay which is created above is going to take place in the memory then it is an Object, Object have type can your own type also, Like Insan

 Insan Abhay = new Insan("Abhay", 19);

Object are also known as Instances; you can check by your self just print the Variable name

print(Abhay);
//Instance of 'Insan'

Making classes helps us to make better Objects; classes are created outside of the Function

Check The code here with example press . and open the in the browser and start practicing.

item.title: 将 Prisma 添加到混音 | Adding Prisma to Remix
item.description:
您可能知道，我是Prisma的忠实粉丝，由于我们在设置 Remix 项目时选择了独立堆栈，所以它已经随 Prisma 一起提供。

在 Remix 中设置 Postgres

由于它已经安装了 Prisma，我们可以在prisma/schema.prisma文件中找到模式。

在这里面，让我们添加最基本的帖子设置。
当我们在这里时，将数据源更改为Postgres 。

 datasource db { provider = " postgres " url = env ( " DATABASE_URL " ) } model Post { slug String @ id title String content String createdAt DateTime @ default ( now ()) updatedAt DateTime @ updatedAt }

现在打开您的.env文件和正确的DATABASE_URL到您的 Prisma 数据库实例。

对我来说，它看起来像这样：

 DATABASE_URL = ' postgresql://USER:PASSWORD@127.0.0.1:5432/YOUR_DB ' ;

设置完成后，运行以下命令来创建数据库。

我们应该在我们的数据库客户端中填充并显示数据库。

将我们的模型更改为 Postgres

到目前为止，我们的模型一直在使用我们在本地设置的纯 JSON 文件。
让我们将其更改为从 Postgres 数据库加载。

打开app/models/post.server.ts文件并将其修改为如下所示。

 import { prisma } from ' ~/db.server ' ; export async function getPosts () { return prisma . post . findMany (); } export async function getPost ( slug : string ) { return prisma . post . findUnique ({ where : { slug } }); }

这也使它更清洁。

注意：继续并在您的数据库中添加一个帖子。 使用 Prisma 播种机或手动添加一个。

现在，当我们运行我们的应用程序时，我们不必进行任何其他更改，它只会反映这个新的数据源！

天作之合：Remix ❤️ Prisma。

如果你想查看完整的代码，它托管在GitHub 上。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

As you might know, I'm a big fan of Prisma, and since we choose the indie stack when we set up our Remix project, it already comes with Prisma.

Setting up Postgres in Remix

Since it already comes with Prisma installed, we can find the schema over in prisma/schema.prisma file.

Inside this, let's add the most basic post setup.
And while we are here, change the data source to Postgres.

datasource db { provider = "postgres" url = env("DATABASE_URL")
} model Post { slug String @id title String content String createdAt DateTime @default(now()) updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
}

Now open your .env file and the correct DATABASE_URL to your Prisma database instance.

For me it looks like this:

DATABASE_URL = 'postgresql://USER:PASSWORD@127.0.0.1:5432/YOUR_DB';

Once you setup, run the following command to create the database.

We should have the database populated and visible in our database client.

Changing our model to Postgres

So far, our model has been using the plain JSON file we had set up locally.
Let's change it to be loaded from the Postgres database.

Open up the app/models/post.server.ts file and modify it to look like this.

import { prisma } from '~/db.server'; export async function getPosts() { return prisma.post.findMany();
} export async function getPost(slug: string) { return prisma.post.findUnique({ where: { slug } });
}

This makes it way cleaner as well.

Note: Go ahead and add a post to your database. Either use the Prisma seeder or manually add one.

Now when we run our app, we don't have to make any other changes, and it will simply reflect this new data source!

A match made in heaven: Remix ❤️ Prisma.

If you want to see the completed code, it's hosted here on GitHub.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter
item.author: dailydevtips1

item.title: 基于区块链的云计算 2：AWS 的 BaaS | Blockchain-based Cloud Computing 2: BaaS by AWS
item.description:
根据您计划迁移到云中的用例，以及您为它选择的提供商，他们每个人都有自己的机会和挑战。 然而，每当谈到云计算时，我们很可能会发现自己要么在亚马逊 AWS要么在微软 Azure附近。 由于这两个提供商之间的比较表明有很多关于它们的讨论。 然而，它们在基于区块链的云计算环境中提供了哪些功能？

与区块链相关，它们提供两种不同的服务：

  • 亚马逊量子分类帐数据库 (QLDB)
  • 亚马逊管理区块链

与比特币等区块链实现不同，QLDB 专注于所使用的分布式账本技术的不变性和验证以及可扩展性方面，并拥有一个拥有和管理账本的中央可信机构。

唯一的问题是——区块链的主要优势不是它的去中心化性质吗？ 嗯，它主要是在其众所周知的加密实现中，但是有所谓的Blockchain-Trilemma ，简而言之，它说区块链的可扩展性、安全性和去中心化的三个属性相互排斥，因此实现不能同时拥有所有这些属性。 即使在查看不同的加密货币时，很明显各个开发人员在实施时都专注于不同的方面。

亚马逊管理区块链

另一方面，亚马逊管理的区块链不仅仅是一个交易日志，就像 QLDB 一样，它是一个分布式账本，拥有分散的所有权。 它与Hyperledger Fabric 框架和Ethereum 兼容，但使用此服务创建的网络也可以保持私有。 该服务负责每个节点的硬件、网络连接以及每台机器上运行的客户端软件。 通过这种方式，多个 AWS 账户可以成为一个网络的一部分。

市场选项

除了这两个完全充实的区块链服务之外，AWS 市场还通过Hyperledger Fabric 框架提供软件解决方案，例如 Sawtooth、Cello 等等。 就像下载他们的GitHub 存储库并在本地计算机上编译和运行代码一样，只需单击几下，一切都可以简单地部署在云中。

即插即用

现在更进一步，这些解决方案也可以相互结合，因此不是排他性的。 例如，可以根据如下架构在 Amazon Managed Blockchain 上运行 Hyperledger Explorer ：

结论

现在我们知道在为我们的下一个云中区块链应用程序寻找 AWS 时会发生什么。 如需更深入地了解以太坊和基于 Hyperledger Fabric 的区块链或 AWS 上的所谓区块链模板之间的详细差异，请随时查阅亚马逊的官方开发人员指南。

在下一篇文章中，我们将介绍该领域中的 Microsoft Azure 方法！

亚历山大在 https://www.pexels.com/ 上的封面图片



原文:

Depending on the use case you plan to lift-and-shift into the Cloud, and which provider you chose for it, each of them comes with their own set of chances as well as challenges. However, whenever it comes to Cloud computing we most probably find ourselves either here Amazon AWS or around there Microsoft Azure. As comparisons between these two providers show there is much to discuss about them. Yet, what are the features they provide in the context of Blockchain-based Cloud computing?

AWS Blockchain Solutions

AWS, which is short for Amazon Web Services, is an on-demand cloud computing platform provided by Amazon. Related to Blockchains they provide two different services:

  • Amazon Quantum Ledger Database (QLDB)
  • Amazon Manged Blockchain

QLDB

The QLDB, unlike a Blockchain implementation like Bitcoin, is focused on the immutability and verification as well as the scalability aspect of the utilized distributed ledger technology and has a central, trusted authority that owns and manages the ledger.

QLDB, illustration by Amazon

The only question is – Isn’t the main advantage of a Blockchain its decentralized nature? Well, it is mostly in its well-known crypto implementations, yet there is the so-called Blockchain-Trilemma, which, in short, says that the three properties of a Blockchain scalability, security and decentralization exclude each other, so an implementation cannot have all of these properties at once. Even when looking at different crypto currencies it becomes apparent that the respective developers focused on different aspects when it came to their implementation.

The Blockchain Trilemma by bingx

Amazon Manged Blockchain

Amazons Manged Blockchain on the other hand is more than a transaction log, like the QLDB, it is a distributed ledger with decentralized ownership. It is compatible with the Hyperledger Fabric framework and Ethereum, yet the network created with this service can also remain private. The service takes care of the hardware for each node, network connections as well as the client software running on each machine. Multiple AWS accounts can be part of one network this way.

Amazon Manged Blockchain, illustration by Amazon

Marketplace Options

Beside these two fully flesh out Blockchain services the AWS marketplace also provides software solutions, again, by the Hyperledger Fabric Framework such as Sawtooth, Cello and many more. Just like downloading for instance the their GitHub Repository and compiling and running the code on your local machine everything is simply deployed in the cloud with just a few clicks.

Plug’n’Play

Taking everything a step further now, these solutions may also be combined with one another and thus are not exclusive. For example, one could run a Hyperledger Explorer on Amazon Managed Blockchain according to an architecture like this:

Running Hyperledger Explorer on Amazon Managed Blockchain

Conclusion

Now we know what to expect when looking at AWS for our next Blockchain application in the cloud. For a deeper insight into the detailed differences between Ethereum and Hyperledger Fabric based Blockchains or so-called Blockchain Templates on AWS feel free to consult the official Developer Guide by Amazon.

In the next post we are going to look at the Microsoft Azure approach in this domain!

cover image by Aleksandar on https://www.pexels.com/
item.author: jessica_veit

item.title: 2022 年排名前 5 位的静态站点生成器 | Top 5 Static Site Generators in 2022
item.description:
静态网站（网站）并不新鲜。 事实上，它们从 Web 1.0 时代就已经存在。 在那些昏暗而遥远的日子里，互联网只有少数贡献者，因此管理和维护静态网站相当轻松。

然而，从那时起，创作者和消费者的需求发生了巨大变化。 因此，我们需要一种新技术来生成静态站点，以适应数字革命的资源和创新精神。

这就是静态站点生成器的用武之地。

在本文中，我们将讨论已经统治 Jamstack 世界一段时间的最佳静态站点生成器。 挑选五台发电机是一项艰巨的工作——今天有 300 多台发电机，其中大多数都非常出色。 但是我们已经确定了五个值得特别提及的内容，因为它们在 Jamstack 和 Web 开发人员社区中广受欢迎。

首先，让我们考虑一下为什么静态站点生成器如此重要……

网站的发展是一个渐进但无情的过程。

自 90 年代中期互联网出现（至少在大众意识中）以来，网站不仅从技术角度发展，而且从文化角度发展。 它们在我们社会中的作用和用例不断扩大。 随着这种增长，消费者在贡献和创建数字内容方面表现出增加。

因此，由 ISP 运行的 Web 服务器提供服务的旧静态网站已经过时。 这些页面需要更好的标记支持、更时尚的样式和更动态的内容。 当 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 的三巨头席卷网络世界时，我们进入了客户端-服务器架构范式。 这预示着 Web 2.0 时代的到来。 现在我们在 3.0。

然而，这在传统的客户端-服务器架构中产生了问题。 作为开发人员，我们需要自己承担管理服务器和数据库的角色，无论是升级、修补安全修复程序还是执行一般维护问题。

更重要的是，动态网站的传统客户端-服务器工作流程很慢——而且随着每一项新的创新，它变得越来越慢。 在原始模型下，开发人员将编写代码、测试、构建应用程序并将其发送到服务器。 用户将从服务器请求一个页面（资源），服务器将执行计算，生成所需的标记并将它们作为响应发送给用户。 步骤太多了。


传统的客户端-服务器架构

进入 Jamstack

该架构基于三个关键特性：

  • 客户端Java Script
  • 可重用的 A PI
  • 预建标记。

让我们先放大prebuilt markup部分。 我们不会在服务器端计算和创建它们，然后通过请求将它们发送给客户端； 相反，我们在CDN （内容交付网络）上托管静态 HTML 标记的预构建版本，并在客户端请求时从那里提供它。

此外，页面的预构建特性意味着它们尽可能像静态页面一样执行。

这反过来又产生了对Static Site Generators (SSG) 的需求。 这些是帮助开发人员创建预构建标记的库、工具和框架（主要是开源）。


好的，足够的历史

让我们开始吧。 这是我们的技巧五个静态站点生成器。

事实上，它不仅仅是一个静态站点生成器。 它有服务器渲染、API 支持、路由预取、智能构建和更多功能的选项。

数据获取的可能性包括：

  • 预构建和预渲染。 这包括静态站点生成 (SSG) 和服务器端渲染 (SSR)。
  • 在运行时动态更新内容。 这依赖于客户端渲染 (CSR) 和增量静态回归 (ISR)。

作为一个框架， Next.js默认捆绑了许多功能，因此您无需花费任何时间进行设置。 这些功能包括：

  • 反应基础。
  • 内置 TypeScript 配置和支持。
  • 内置 CSS 支持。
  • 用于有机地创建端点的 API 路由。
  • 使用代码拆分优化捆绑。
  • 开箱即用的图像优化。
  • 内置国际化支持。
  • 内置分析支持。

而这仅仅是开始。

此外，在Vercel或Netlify等服务上部署 Next.js 应用程序非常简单。

您可以在此处了解有关 Next.js 的更多信息。

您可以使用静态站点生成 (SSG) 来预构建应用程序/站点启动时最重要的内容。
  • 您可以将静态站点生成与延迟静态生成和智能页面呈现相结合，以在运行时更新数据。
  • 这些尖端技术提供了出色的开发人员体验。
  • 各种插件生态系统允许开发人员将 Gatsby 与所有流行的 CMS 和数据库集成。
  • 有很多主题和入门工具包可供开发人员快速上手。

    • 同样，盖茨比项目是在麻省理工学院的赞助下获得许可的。 此外，每周 500,000 的下载量和 GitHub 上的 50,00 颗星使它成为最受欢迎的 SSG 之一。

    您可以在此处了解有关盖茨比的更多信息。 与 Next.js 一样，在Netlify 、 Gatsby Cloud或Vercel等云托管服务上部署 Gatsby 站点很容易。

    如果您想了解如何在您的 Gatsby 网站上集成 Bugfender，您可以查看我们的博客文章，了解两者如何协同工作。

    这很简单，入门的学习曲线很浅。

    在此列表中的所有技术中，它提供了最快的开始到完成构建时间。

  • 用于创建内容的降价样式为开发人员提供了灵活性，因为许多其他内容平台都支持这种格式。
  • 主题系统易于实现，并提供 300 多个主题可供选择。
  • 除了 HTML 输出，Hugo 还支持其他格式，如 JSON、AMP 和各种自定义类型。

    • 您可以在此处了解有关 Hugo 的更多信息，或在此处阅读有关部署和托管 Hugo 应用程序的更多信息。

    它是一种简单、相对较新但流行的 SSG。 包括web.dev 、 eslint.org和`netlify.com`在内的几个知名网站都是使用这种技术构建的。

    它具有灵活的配置，您需要零配置开始。

  • 您无需更改项目的目录结构即可在项目中使用 Eleventy。 它与您的项目目录结构一样工作。
  • 它适用于多种模板语言，包括 HTML、Markdown、JavaScript 和 Pug。
  • 您可以在此处找到入门项目以进行快速介绍。 如果您想深入了解，可以在此处找到有关部署和托管 Eleventy 应用程序的所有详细信息。

    它在GitHub Pages中广受欢迎，您可以在其中通过几个简单的步骤来托管您的网站。

    • 它只需要三个简单的命令就可以让您的博客/网站启动并运行。
    • 它支持 Markdown、Liquid、HTML 和 CSS 作为网站内容的模板语言。
    • 它在 GitHub 上有 44,000 颗星。

      好的，那么如何选择静态站点生成器（SSG）？

      有这么多可用的选项，这是一个关键问题。 实际上，答案取决于几个因素。

      • 初级技能组合。 这可能会严重影响您的选择。 例如，当您是 ReactJS 开发人员时，您不太可能选择基于 Python 的 SSG。
      • 最终目标。 你在建什么？ 如果它像博客或投资组合这样简单，那么您的重点是内容，因此 Jekyll、Hugo、Hexo 和 Eleventy 将是一个不错的选择。 如果它是一个需要良好模板的更复杂的项目，则混合数据获取机制更合适。 在这种情况下，使用 Next.js、Gatsby、Grindsome 或 Sapper 可能会更好。
      • 特点。 还有一些额外的因素需要牢记。 社区有多活跃？ 它有多少个 GitHub 星？ 它被下载了多少次？ 它是开源的吗？

      通过思考这些问题，您将能够确定最适合您的特定需求和情况的 SSG。

      在我们走之前……

      以下是静态站点生成器的一些更好的选择，按它们所构建的编程语言分类。

      • 香草 JS ：十一。
      • 角：斯嘉丽。
      • 戈朗：雨果。
      • 红宝石：杰基尔、石板和中间人。
      • 苗条： Sapper.js 。

      如果您需要更多建议，请访问此页面。 它列出了 Jamstack 站点的所有静态站点生成器。

      我们希望您发现这篇文章很有见地。 如果您正在为您的网站或应用程序使用静态站点生成器，请随时在下面的评论中分享您的经验。



    • 原文:

    Static sites (websites) are not new. In fact they’ve been around since the era of Web 1.0. Back in those dim and distant days the internet only had a few contributors, so managing and maintaining a static website was fairly hassle-free.

    However, the needs of creators and consumers have changed dramatically since then. So we need a new technology to generate static sites, befitting the resources and innovative spirit of the digital revolution.

    This is where static site generators come in.

    In this article we’ll talk about best static site generators that have been ruling the Jamstack world for a while now. It was a tough job to pick five – more than 300 generators exist today, and most of them are superb. But we’ve identified five that deserve special mention, due to their massive popularity in the Jamstack and web developer community.

    First of all, let’s consider why static site generators are so crucial…

    The evolution of websites has been a gradual, but relentless process.

    Since the dawn of the internet (at least in popular consciousness) in the mid-90s, websites have developed not just from a technological perspective, but from a cultural one. Their role, and use cases, in our society have continued to expand. And with that growth, consumers have shown increase in contributing and creating digital content.

    So the old static websites, served from a ISP-run web server, have been rendered obsolete. The pages needed better markup support, sleeker styles and more dynamic content. When the triumvirate of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript took the web world by storm, we entered a client-server architecture paradigm. This heralded the era of Web 2.0. Now we’re at 3.0.

    However, this has created problems in the traditional client-server architecture. As developers, we’ve needed to take on the role of managing servers and databases ourselves, whether it be upgrading, patching a security fix, or performing general maintenance issues.

    What’s more, the traditional client-server workflow for a dynamic website is slow – and with each new innovation, it’s getting slower. Under the original model, developers would write code, test, build the app and ship it to a server. Users would request a page (resource) from the server, and the server would perform the computation, produce the required markups and send them to the user as a response. That’s far, far too many steps.


    A Traditional Client-Server Architecture

    Enter Jamstack

    In late 2016, Jamstack arrived as an architectural concept. The architecture is based on three key features:

    • Client-side J avaScript
    • Reusable A PIs
    • Prebuilt M arkup.

    Let’s zoom into the prebuilt markup part first. The word ‘prebuilt’ means that all templated markups are created at the time of application deployment. We don’t compute and create them on the server side and then send them to the client with a request; instead, we host the prebuilt version of the static HTML markup on a CDN (Content Delivery Network) and serve it from there when a client requests it.

    CDNs are faster and more secure in nature than regular servers, hence they serve pages much faster. Also, the prebuilt nature of the pages means they perform like static pages, wherever possible.

    This, in turn, has created the need for Static Site Generators(SSGs). These are the libraries, tools and frameworks (mostly Open Source) that help developers to create prebuilt markups.


    Jamstack Architecture

    Ok, enough history

    Let’s get into it. Here are our tip five static site generators.

    1. Next.js

    Next.js by Vercel is the most popular open-source ReactJS framework right now. In fact it’s much more than just a Static Site Generator; it’s got options for server rendering, API supports, route pre-fetching, smart building and many more functions.

    Next.js allows you to fetch data into your pages based on your use cases. The data fetching possibilities include:

    • Prebuilding and Prerendering. This includes Static-Site Generation(SSG) and Server-Side Rendering (SSR).
    • Updating content dynamically at run time. This relies on Client-Side Rendering(CSR) and Incremental Static Regression(ISR).

    As a framework, Next.js bundles many features by default so you don’t have to spend any time setting things up. These features include:

    • React basis.
    • In-built TypeScript configuration and support.
    • Built-in CSS Support.
    • API routes to create endpoints organically.
    • Optimized bundling with code splitting.
    • Image optimizations out of the box.
    • Built-in internationalization support.
    • In-built analytics support.

    And that’s just the start.

    The Next.js project is MIT licensed, with more than 2.5 million weekly downloads and over 80,000 stars on GitHub. What’s more, deploying a Next.js app on services like Vercel or Netlify is extremely straightforward.

    You can learn more about Next.js here.

    2. Gatsby

    Gatsby (also known as GatsbyJS) is another open-source ReactJS framework designed to build fast, secure static sites with data from various platforms. Gatsby’s biggest strength is its unified data layer, which pulls data from various sources and renders it in the markup.

    Gatsby empowers developers with myriad features out of the box:

    • You can use static-site generation (SSG) to prebuild the content that matters most when the app/site launches.
    • You can combine the static-site generation with deferred static generation and intelligent page rendering to update data at run time.
    • Gatsby is based on ReactJS and uses GraphQL technology to transform and fetch data. These cutting-edge technologies provide a great developer experience.
    • Gatsby has got a massive plugin ecosystem, be it for images, styling, data transformation, analytics and… well, a whole bunch of other stuff.
    • The variety of plugin ecosystems allow developers to integrate Gatsby with all popular CMS and databases.
    • There are plenty of themes and starter kits available for developers to get up to speed.

    Again, the Gatsby project is licenced under the auspices of MIT. Furthermore, the 500,000 weekly downloads and 50,00 stars on GitHub make it one of the popular SSGs to try out.

    You can learn more about Gatsby here. Like Next.js, it is easy to deploy a Gatsby site on cloud hosting services like Netlify, Gatsby Cloud or Vercel.

    If you want to learn how to integrate Bugfender on your Gatsby site, you can check our blog post on how both work together.

    3. Hugo

    Hugo is another popular open-source static site generator built with the Go language. It’s simple, with a shallow learning curve to get started.

    Hugo uses the markdown format for content, templates for layouts, i18n bundles and a plethora of configuration and data files to build the static sites.

    Some of the key features of Hugo are:

    • It offers the quickest start-to-completion build time of all the technologies in this list.
    • The markdown style for creating content provides developers with flexibility, as many other content platforms support this format.
    • i18n support allows devs to build multi-language sites quickly.
    • The theming system is easy to implement and provides more than 300 themes to choose from.
    • Aside from HTML output, Hugo supports other formats like JSON, AMP and various custom types.

    The Hugo project is licensed under Apache-2.0 and has received 60,000 stars on GitHub. You can learn more about Hugo here or read more about deploying and hosting a Hugo app here.

    4. Eleventy

    Eleventy is a JavaScript-based open-source static site generator. It is a simple, comparatively new, but popular SSG. Several well-known websites including web.dev, eslint.org and `netlify.com` are built using this technology.

    Eleventy has some incredible features that make it an ideal SSG choice for those just getting started with web development.

    • It has a flexible configuration, and you need zero configuration to start with.
    • You don’t have to change your project’s directory structure to use Eleventy in your project. It works with your project directory structure just as it is.
    • It works with many template languages including HTML, Markdown, JavaScript and Pug.
    • Eleventy is not a JavaScript framework, so it doesn’t come with any unnecessary boilerplate code.

    Eleventy is another MIT-licensed project and one of the fastest-growing, with close to 12,000 GitHub stars. You can find the starter project here for a quick introduction. If you want to go deeper, all details about deploying and hosting an Eleventy app can be found here.

    5. Jekyll

    Jekyll is a static site generator written in Ruby, known for making the creation of static blogs and websites extremely simple. It is widely popular with GitHub Pages, where you can host your site with a few simple steps.

    • Jekyll is a Ruby Gem that requires the Ruby version 2.5.0 or higher.
    • It requires only three simple commands to get your blog/site up and running.
    • It supports Markdown, Liquid, HTML & CSS as the template language for site content.
    • Jekyll is blog-aware, which means it has all batteries included to support features like pages, categories, permalinks and layouts.

    Jekyll is open-sourced under… yep, you guessed it… MIT. It has 44,000 stars on GitHub.

    Ok, so how do I select a Static Site Generator(SSG)?

    With so many options available, this is a critical question. Actually, the answer depends on a few factors.

    • Primary skill set. This may heavily influence your choice. It is very unlikely that you would select a Python-based SSG when you are a ReactJS developer, for example.
    • End goal. What are you building? If it’s something simple like a blog or portfolio, your focus is content and so Jekyll, Hugo, Hexo, and Eleventy would be a great choice. If it’s a more complex project that requires good templating, a hybrid data-fetching mechanism is more appropriate. In this case you might be better off with Next.js, Gatsby, Grindsome or Sapper.
    • Characteristics. There are some additional factors to bear in mind. How active is the community? How many GitHub stars does it have? How many times has it been downloaded? Is it open-sourced?

    By thinking about these questions, you’ll be able to identify the SSG most suited to your particular needs and circumstances.

    Before We Go…

    Here are a few more great options for Static Site Generators, categorized by the programming languages they are built on.

    • Reactjs : Gatsby, Next.js, React Static.
    • Vue : Gridsome, Nuxt, VuePress.
    • Vanilla JS : Eleventy.
    • Angular : Scully.
    • Golang : Hugo.
    • Python : Pelican, Cactus, MkDocs.
    • Java : JBake.
    • Ruby : Jekyll, Slate and Middleman.
    • Svelte : Sapper.js.

    If you need more suggestions, head over to this page. It lists all Static Site Generators for Jamstack sites.

    We hope you found this article insightful. If you’re using a Static Site Generator for your site or app, feel free to share your experience in the comment below.

当您聘请最好的Salesforce 开发公司时，您可以避免将业务映射到错误方向的风险。 与印度合适的 Salesforce 顾问合作将确保严格的决策、规划、设计和开发，以及支持 Salesforce 的业务解决方案的实施和维护。

结论

当然可以帮助您找到合适的 Salesforce 合作伙伴。

资料来源：- 印度斯坦时报



原文:

The right Salesforce development company provides customized offerings as per your requirement and back you throughout the process

Salesforce is the powerhouse in the world of CRM. Utilizing this technology can stir organizational operations and promote a significant enhancement in customer retention. In order to efficiently leverage this platform to maintain customer interaction, you might require a suite of consistent and quality solutions in Salesforce development. The right Salesforce development company provides customized offerings as per your requirement and back you throughout the process. If you are not sure which company is best fitted for your business goals, then this list of top 10 Salesforce development companies India is all that you need to get started.

List of Best Salesforce Consultants in India

When you hire the best Salesforce development company, you save yourself the risk of mapping your business in the wrong direction. Partnering with the right Salesforce consultants in India will ensure rigorous decision-making, planning, designing and developing along with the implementation and maintenance of Salesforce-enabled business solutions.

Let us now have a look at the top 10 Salesforce companies in India that are contributing to the transformational business solutions with Salesforce development.

1. The NineHertz

The NineHertz is the leading Salesforce development company India. Founded in 2008, the company integrates a multitude of CRM solutions, offers data synchronization in real time, works on custom-made results and backs businesses with extensive Salesforce support and maintenance services. The NineHertz makes for one of the most reliable Salesforce development companies with certified consulting partners to deliver resilient, expertly detailed and on-time projects.

2. Accenture

Accenture is yet another company with some of the best Salesforce developers who team up to design, develop and render revolutionary results with the help of Salesforce solutions. The company has successfully serviced over 1500 projects in Salesforce and established a recognition for their innovative ways of enhancing business practices.

3. 4C Wipro

4C, now known as Wipro company, has been a player in Salesforce solutions for more than 10 years and it continues to power businesses from around the world with its Salesforce integration. 4C aims to simplify the business roadmap for brands and boost their journey towards building something more useful. The company has achieved over 1500 Salesforce projects with their one-of-a-kind approach to technology implementations.

4. Deloitte Digital

Deloitte Digital is recognized for their outstanding expertise in Salesforce-based solutions in different sectors. The company is spread across 35 countries and it impeccably unfolds futuristic outcomes before its clients to help them achieve their business goals.

5. TCS

TCS utilizes a diverse cloud-based platform in Salesforce in order to make sure that they are able to treat clients to fruitful and cost-effective services and let them enjoy a complete ensemble of Salesforce products. The company has a team of over 4,00,000 qualified and experienced Salesforce experts and developers with expertise in different industries.

6. Tech Mahindra

Tech Mahindra brings their expertise forth to encompass a comprehensive portfolio of large organizations. Trusted and recognized across the global market, this company is a reliable option for accentuating implementations, maintenance, and the development of Salesforce.

7. IBM

IBM, with its qualified team of Salesforce developers, ensures proper utilization of your data power across Salesforce clouds. The company enables IBM Watson as well as Salesforce Einstein to look after your workflows and shape your decision-making in every aspect, be it sales or services.

8. Capgemini

Capgemini has been providing the Salesforce-powered results for over a decade. The company offers solutions that help businesses with modernized practices and has also bagged many awards for their remarkable contribution.

9. Cognizant

Cognizant is one of the established companies in India that offers a suite of services including consulting, designing, implementing as well as supporting the business roadmap for brands. The company delivers custom solutions based on clients' requirements that prove to be the powerhouse of a transformational future of a business.

10. Zensar

Zensar is counted among the top Salesforce development partners for its successful delivery of the award-winning projects in different industries such as finance, retail, and manufacturing. Salesforce consultants at Zensar bring end-to-end services to transform applications.

Conclusion

These are the 10 best Salesforce Development Companies India that have made to the list on the basis of their recognition, quality services and a perfect suite of solutions. These companies have the top Salesforce developers in India who work towards providing a custom-made group of Salesforce solutions to their clients. In case you want to hire one for your business, this list will
certainly help you in spotting the suitable Salesforce partner.

Source :- Hindustan Times
