item.author: mohira21

item.title: Marko 用于站点，Solid 用于应用程序 | Marko for Sites, Solid for Apps
item.description:
我已经坐下来写这篇文章 2 年了。 在我心中，这是我在阅读@swyx的典型Svelte for Sites，React for Apps之前就打算写的文章。 当我在 2020 年 3 月签约加入Marko团队时（我知道，这是搬迁工作的最佳时机），我很清楚一些事情。 我将有机会研究两种最具示范性的方法，以了解 JavaScript 框架的未来可能是什么样子。

有些人想知道为什么我会着手开发第二个框架，但我从未见过冲突。 在许多方面，它们完全不同。 每个设计决策都是通过不同的权衡来做出的，其中每个“正确”的答案都允许不同。 但这让我如此兴奋的原因是Solid和Marko代表了重要轴上最强大的方法。 最容易将其解释为一堆独立的范围，但现实是我们生活在一个多维世界中，一个解决方案并不总是对所有决策都完全站在一边。 但是作为开发人员，我们不断改变我们的观点以进行这些 2D 比较。

所以任务很简单：不要改变世界，改变视角。 而不是专注于中间，成为每个人的一切，而是专注于更容易实现的任务，在分裂的地方成为最好的版本。 支持多匹马没有错。

那么网站与应用程序？

好吧，长期以来，这一直是网络的二分法。 它甚至早于单页应用程序，尽管这就是它在过去十年中如此受到关注的原因。 我们似乎有两个非常不同的用例试图利用相同的技术。 因此，即使在不可行的情况下，也许有一种方法可以实现统一，这确实是有道理的。 我今天谈论的框架/库代表了双方的发球台。

网站的 Marko

非常重视页面加载，它是第一个从一开始就引入乱序流和部分水合的 JavaScript 框架。

从一开始就很清楚，它最终是一种语言而不是框架。 直到五年后 Svelte 做了类似的事情，这种思路才真正普及。

组件是自动发现的，添加 JavaScript 的影响很小。 它具有人们使用服务器模板语言习惯的所有特征，但它是完全同构的，可以自动在浏览器中交互。

它不假思索地发送最少的 JavaScript。 它的“Islands”是自动的，它的流式传输就像添加一个带有承诺的<await>标签一样简单。 它的多页转发方法不担心持久的客户端状态或客户端路由，就像您期望的网站一样工作。 它具有最简洁的语法，可以让您编写所有 JavaScript 框架中最少的代码。 这是一种轻松获得无与伦比的页面加载性能而又不脱离简单性的方法，最终我们只是在网站中制作页面。

适用于应用程序的实体

固体来自一个非常不同的家。 这是一个长期存在的项目，需要不断变化和转向以寻找客户。 虽然我们永远无法重写我们拥有的东西，但我们会迅速拆除并更换零件。 它进化为在它自己出现时减轻了重量。

组件只是函数， <div>只是 HTMLElement，状态只是响应性。 它可以缩小到 jQuery 替代品和并发时间片，并具有使用相同技术在非 Web 平台上运行的可移植性。 没有 VDOM 或必需的抽象。 它是一只变色龙，当它需要的时候，它正是它需要的样子。

虽然它利用了编译，但只有 JSX 被编译。 它在很大程度上是一个“Just JavaScript”库。 一切都建立在简单的基元组合之上，每个角落都有一个逃生口，可以完全控制应用程序的运行方式。 锦上添花的是，这种建模事物的方式也非常高效，这使得 Solid 在其运行的所有环境中都成为基准之王。

这种适应性使得移植库变得容易，并且人们可以选择从他们喜欢的 DSL 编译到 Solid。 在 Hooks 等现代趋势之前，它一直在倡导这种可组合的原始方法。 它让“Just JavaScript”在一个看起来像是深入自定义 DSL 和编译语言的世界中获得了新的生命，并为开发人员返回了他们在最苛刻的应用程序上执行所需的所有控制权。

假二分法？

我承认我是以虚假的名义引诱你到这里来的。 如果有的话，我们已经看到以 JavaScript 为中心的 Web 技术正在崩溃。 起初，静态（SSG）和动态（SSR）体验之间存在明显差异，但这种差异已经消失，很快站点（MPA）和应用程序（SPA）之间的差异也会出现。

当我们在与 Astro 一起使用时在极其昂贵的页面中看到 Solid acing 页面加载指标时：

或者 Marko 带来令人难以置信的客户端渲染性能和可组合性及其新的编译反应性，您开始意识到我们只是看到了一条最终统一光谱对立面的道路。

这让我充满希望，因为这些框架在哲学上大不相同。 语言与库，HTML 与 JavaScript 首先，可变性与不变性，隐式与显式状态更新，双向数据绑定与单向流。 尽管存在这些差异，但我们仍然在这里。

声称 Marko 比 Svelte 更苗条，或者说 Solid 比 React 更具反应性，这甚至是不公平的。 这些框架处于相反的边缘，具有不同的价值和理念，但它们都具有无与伦比的性能、最小的包大小，并且可以服务于跨越整个范围的用例。 如果这些框架可以做到这一点，那么介于两者之间的任何框架也可以。

结论

很快我们就可以生活在一个站点与应用程序不会影响您选择的工具的技术价值的世界中，这不是很好吗？ 也不是 JSX vs Custom DSL，还是 HTML-first vs JS-first 心态？ 当然，总会有迎合开发者体验的偏好和解决方案。 但是你可以选择对你说话的框架，我无法说服你有一个“赢家”。 不管你心中的哲学是什么，这里都有一条路。

我让马尔科有机会看到这一点。 挑战我的喜好和世界观。 与Solid一样，我研究了一种方法，该方法似乎比它的时代提前了几年做所有正确的事情，并利用这种经验来探索最好的版本是什么样的。 现在，我在其他框架中看到了不同程度的潜力。 这里真的有一个适合每个人的答案。 这很令人兴奋。 网络确实是一个了不起的地方。



原文:

I've been sitting on writing this article for 2 years. In my heart this was the article I was going to write even before I read @swyx's quintessential Svelte for Sites, React for Apps. When I signed on to join the Marko team back in March 2020 (I know, perfect timing to relocate for work) something was very clear to me. I would have the opportunity to work on two of the most exemplary approaches to what the future of JavaScript frameworks could look like.

Some wondered why I would take on working on a second framework but I never saw a conflict. In many ways, they couldn't be more different. Every design decision made with different set of tradeoffs, where the "right" answer for each is allowed to be different. But the reason this excited me so much was that Solid and Marko represented the most powerful approaches on the axes that matter. A pincer movement of sorts for the JavaScript framework world.

Evan You, creator of Vue gave a great talk on tradeoffs of frameworks where he positioned Vue as that middle ground between React and Angular. It's easiest to explain this as a bunch of independent ranges but the reality is we live in a multi-dimensional world and one solution isn't always fully on one side for all decisions. But we as developers keep on changing our perspective to pull out these 2D comparisons.

So the task was simple: Don't change the world, change the perspective. And instead of being focused on the middle and being everything to everyone focus on the more achievable task at being the best version in the places that are divisive. Nothing wrong with backing multiple horses.

So Sites Vs Apps?

Well, this has been the dichotomy of the web for a long time. It even predates Single Page Apps, although that is why it has come into focus so much the past decade. We have seemingly two very different use cases trying to leverage the same technology. So it does make sense that maybe there is a way to make this unified even when it hasn't been feasible. And the frameworks/libraries I'm talking about today represent both sides to a tee.

Marko for Sites

Marko was created at eBay sometime in the early 2010s and was open sourced in 2014. It was built for eCommerce and the demanding nature of support global customers where not every device and network is made equal. A huge emphasis placed on page load, being the first JavaScript framework to introduce both Out of Order Streaming and Partial Hydration right from its inception.

Marko also was one of the earliest compiler-based frameworks as its origins were from server side templating languages. It was very clear from its beginning it was ultimately a language rather than a framework; a line of thinking that wasn't really popularized until Svelte did similar half a decade later.

Marko is a Superset of HTML where the whole world lives in Single File Components and a new user can enter this world simply by changing the extension of the HTML files they got from a designer to .marko or using the HTML they copied and pasted from StackOverflow. Components are auto discovered and the impact of adding JavaScript feels minimal. It has all the characteristics people using server templating languages are used to but it is fully isomorphic to become interactive in the browser automatically.

And it sends the least JavaScript without thinking. Its "Islands" are automatic, and its streaming as easy as adding an <await> tag with a promise. Its Multi-Page forward approach isn't worried about persistent client state or client routing and just works like a website the way you'd expect. It has the tersest syntax that lets you write the least code of all JavaScript Frameworks. It's an effortless way to get unmatched page load performance without getting away from the simplicity, that in the end we are just making pages in a website.

Solid for Apps

Solid came from a very different home. First created as a side project in 2016 when I was at a startup creating private Social Media. It was a long lived project that needed to keep on changing and pivoting to find its customer. While we could never rewrite what we had we would quickly rip out and replace parts. Solid was modular from the start, built to work with Web Components as a component-agnostic solution. It evolved to shedding that weight as it came into its own.

Solid's power is that everything is a primitive, right down to the JSX. Components are just functions, <div> are just HTMLElements, and state is just reactivity. It scales down to a jQuery replacement and up to concurrent time-slicing, with the portability to run on non-web platforms using the same techniques. There is no VDOM or required abstractions. It is a chameleon that is exactly what it needs to be when it needs to be.

While it leverages compilation, only the JSX is compiled. It is very much a "Just JavaScript" library. Everything is built upon composing simple primitives and every corner has an escape hatch for complete control over how your application runs. The icing on the cake is this way of modelling things is also extremely performant making Solid a benchmark king in all environments it runs on.

This adaptability has made it easy to port libraries, and for people to opt into compiling down to Solid from their preferred DSLs. And it has been championing this composable primitive approach well before modern trends like Hooks. It gives "Just JavaScript" a new life in a world looking like it is heading deep into custom DSLs and compiled languages and returns to developers all the control they need to execute on the most demanding of applications.

False Dichotomy?

I admit I lured you here under a false pretense. If anything we've seen JavaScript centric web technologies collapsing on themselves. At first there was a clear difference between Static(SSG) and Dynamic(SSR) experiences but that has disappeared, and soon the difference between Sites(MPA) and Apps(SPA) will as well.

When we are seeing Solid acing page load metrics in impossibly expensive pages when used with Astro:

Or Marko bringing incredible client side rendering performance and composability with its new compiled reactivity you start realizing we are just seeing a path to finally unify the opposite sides of the spectrum.

And this makes me hopeful, because these frameworks philosophically couldn't be more different. Language vs Library, HTML vs JavaScript first, Mutability vs Immutability, Implicit vs Explicit state updates, 2-way data-binding vs Unidirectional flow. And despite these differences we are here.

It would even not be unfair to claim that Marko is more svelte than Svelte, or Solid is more reactive than React. These frameworks sit at the the opposite edges with different values and philosophy but they both have unmatched performance, the smallest bundle sizes, and can serve use cases that span the whole spectrum. If these frameworks can do this any framework in between could as well.

Conclusion

Isn't it great that soon we could live in a world where Site vs App won't impact your choice of tool on technical merit? Nor JSX vs Custom DSL, or a HTML-first vs JS-first mentality? Sure, there will always be preferences and solutions that cater on developer experience. But you can choose the framework that speaks to you, and I wouldn't be able to convince you that there's a "winner". There is a path here regardless of what philosophy you hold close to your heart.

It took me giving Marko a chance to see this. To challenge my preferences and view of the world. As with Solid I looked at an approach that seemed to do all the right things years ahead of its time and leveraged that experience to explore what the best version of that looks like. Now, I see that potential to varying degrees in other frameworks. There really is an answer here for everyone. And that is exciting. The web is truly an amazing place.
item.author: ryansolid

item.title: 使用 Webpack 和 TypeScript 进行依赖注入 | Dependency Injection with Webpack and TypeScript
item.description:
对代码进行检查很丑陋，因此您可能会决定根据平台导出不同的对象：

 interface PlatformApi { getIdToken : () => Promise < string > ; getDeviceName : () => string ; } function getPlatformImplementation (): PlatformApi { if ( onAndroid ) { return { getIdToken : async () => { // Android implementation }, getDeviceName : () => { // Android implementation } } } if ( onIOS ) { return { getIdToken : async () => { // iOS implementation }, getDeviceName : () => { // iOS implementation } } } // ... } export const platform : PlatformApi = getPlatformImplementation ();

这更好，允许任何人通过调用platform.getDeviceName()进行平台调用。

但它仍然不理想。 您正在为所有平台捆绑代码，留下一些 Webpack 不知道如何通过 tree-shaking 摆脱的无用 JS 重量。

模块更换

我们的救星是 Webpack 的NormalModuleReplacementPlugin 。 您可以制作一个“存根”模块，所有内容都可以导入和调用：

但是在不同的 webpack 配置中，为每个平台做不同的模块替换。 例如，对于 Android，首先使用 Android 特定代码制作一个导出对象的模块，该对象实现共享PlatformApi接口：

安卓平台.ts：

 export const platform : PlatformApi = { getIdToken : async (): Promise < string > => { // Android implementation }, getDeviceName : (): string => { // Android implementation }, }

然后告诉 Webpack 用你的平台模块替换存根模块：

您必须执行花哨的替换功能，因为人们可能会从任何地方引用您的模块。 检查只是寻找以/MyAppPlatform结尾的任何导入，然后用它旁边的AndroidPlatform模块替换最后一部分。 将您的应用程序名称添加到存根模块是一个好主意，这样您就不会意外地替换您正在使用的库深处的某些模块。 这发生在我身上！

无论如何，使用 Webpack 模块替换依赖注入的方法，你会得到：

  • 类型安全，因为所有平台和调用者都必须符合相同的接口
  • 高效捆绑，因为每个平台都只使用其代码构建
  • 通过platform.getDeviceName()轻松使用模块


原文:

The web is a great way to render an app's interface on any platform, but you often need to do the same action differently based on what platform you're on. WebView callbacks are message-based on iOS, and direct object calls on Android and Electron.

Doing checks all over your code is ugly, so you might decide to export a different object depending on the platform:

interface PlatformApi { getIdToken: () => Promise<string>; getDeviceName: () => string;
} function getPlatformImplementation(): PlatformApi { if (onAndroid) { return { getIdToken: async () => { // Android implementation }, getDeviceName: () => { // Android implementation } } } if (onIOS) { return { getIdToken: async () => { // iOS implementation }, getDeviceName: () => { // iOS implementation } } } // ...
} export const platform: PlatformApi = getPlatformImplementation();

This is better and allows anyone to make a platform call by calling platform.getDeviceName().

But it's still not ideal. You're bundling up code for all platforms, leaving some useless JS weight that Webpack doesn't know how to tree-shake away.

Module replacement

Our savior here is Webpack's NormalModuleReplacementPlugin. You can make a "stub" module that everything imports and calls:

MyAppPlatform.ts:

export const platform: PlatformApi = { getIdToken: async (): Promise<string> => "", getDeviceName: (): string => "",
}

But then in different webpack configs, do a different module replacement for each platform. For example, with Android, first make a module that exports an object that implements the shared PlatformApi interface with Android-specific code:

AndroidPlatform.ts:

export const platform: PlatformApi = { getIdToken: async (): Promise<string> => { // Android implementation }, getDeviceName: (): string => { // Android implementation },
}

Then tell Webpack to swap out the stub module with your platform module:

webpack.config.js


const webpack = require('webpack'); // ... plugins: [ new webpack.NormalModuleReplacementPlugin( /\/MyAppPlatform$/, (resource) => { resource.request = resource.request.replace(/MyAppPlatform$/, "AndroidPlatform") }), ],

You have to do the fancy replacement function because people might be referencing your module from anywhere. The check is just looking for any import that ends in /MyAppPlatform, then replacing that last section with the AndroidPlatform module that's right beside it. It's a good idea to add your app name to your stub modules, so you don't accidentally replace some module deep inside a library you're using. This happened to me!

Anyway, with the Webpack module replacement approach to dependency injection, you get:

  • Type safety because all platforms and callers have to conform to the same interface
  • Efficient bundling because every platform is built with only its code
  • Easy use of the module with platform.getDeviceName()
item.author: randomengy

item.title: 9 必须具备掌握探索性测试的技能 | 9 Must Have Skills To Master Exploratory Testing
item.description:
探索性测试是一种测试技术，允许测试专业人​​员确保开发的应用程序是否按预期工作。 手动测试的存在只是因为探索性测试。 它永远不能被自动化取代，因为它不需要固定的脚本或预先确定的方法。

在这里，我们将讨论探索性测试人员必须具备的 9 项技能，才能在生产环境中成功部署应用程序。

采取并行方法

当您的团队成员多次调查特定问题时，通常会出现这种情况。 要找到解决方案，需要以前所未有的方式进行调查性思考。 采用间接方法调查问题的横向思维是探索性测试中必备的技能。

如果您能够跳出框框思考，您将能够从不同的角度查看网站，并找到与之互动或玩耍的新方式。 通过采用不同的方法，您将能够发现以前未检测到的错误。

采取理性方法的能力

如果您系统地思考并遵循“规则手册”，您将能够发现关键错误，但可能会错过某些可能在生产过程中影响系统的简单错误。 如果您想发现那些隐藏的错误，就必须具备采取理性方法和推理思考的能力。 例如，如果您正在为您的应用程序执行跨浏览器测试，那么您将需要分析并创建一个适当的浏览器兼容性矩阵，并共享一个应用程序可以正常运行的浏览器列表。

做一个好的沟通者

在开始执行探索性测试之前，必须具备良好的沟通技巧。 有时您会发现自己正在与开发团队或利益相关者开会，您必须向他们解释您将要采取的范围和策略。 让其他测试人员了解您发现的错误以及它们对业务的重要性以及应该相应地优先考虑的问题也需要良好的沟通技巧。 通过有效的沟通，您还能够以详细和相关的方式解释您的发现。 对于探索性测试人员来说，沟通能力是一项改变游戏规则的技能。

讲故事的能力——让错误检测变得有趣

讲故事的能力是探索性测试专业人​​员必须具备的重要技能之一。 当您检测到错误时，下一步是向开发人员解释为什么修复此错误对业务至关重要。 你的工作是创建一个虚构的用户，让听众了解当他无法使用特定功能时该用户会有什么感受。 由于没有开发人员喜欢其他人告诉他们他们的代码是错误的，因此您将被要求以友好的方式向他们解释，而不是直接向他们指出错误。

与团队合作的能力

协调是探索性测试最重要的技能。 当单个测试人员未检测到特定错误或问题但团队中的其他人检测到时，可能会出现这种情况。 正确地说，多个大脑比一个大脑工作得更好。 理想的做法是与您的团队一起工作，排除个人偏见。

在技​​术上是健全的

然而，探索性测试是一个完全不同的故事。 您不需要进行任何编码，但您应该具备全栈开发人员的知识。 从用户界面开始，到数据库架构结束，您必须能够理解代码所传达的内容以及界面不同层的一般编码。 如果没有足够的技术知识，您可能会忽略一些关键错误。

成为你团队的夏洛克

仅仅通过观察，熟练的探索性测试专业人​​员将能够发现其他人可能遗漏的错误。 只需通过观察系统，熟练的探索性测试专业人​​员就能够在测试周期开始之前很久就发现可能的错误并提出问题。 观察能力可以说是探索性测试的基础。

批判性思考和有效时间管理的能力

当您在执行测试时进行批判性思考时，您会发现各种可以解释代码的论点，从而产生新的观察结果。 具有批判性思维能力的探索性测试人员随时准备就相关风险提出可行的方法。 通过批判性分析，您可以用开箱即用的思维来处理产品，帮助您在野外测试您的软件。 目前大部分项目都在遵循敏捷方法论，也就是说在固定的时间内，

需要完成开发以及用户故事的测试阶段。 由于探索性测试占用了大量时间，因此测试人员在时间管理方面应该足够精通，以便能够在提供的有限时间内完成测试。



原文:

Exploratory testing is a testing technique that allows testing professionals to ensure whether the developed application is working as intended. Manual testing exists only because of exploratory testing. It can never be replaced with automation since it does not requires a fixed script or a pre-decided approach.

Exploratory testing requires you to think out of the box and find out an innovative approach to test an application by putting yourself in the user’s shoe and check how the application is behaving. Here, we shall discuss 9 skills exploratory testers must have in order to successfully deploy an application in production.

Taking the Parallel Approach

Often a situation may arise when a particular problem has been investigated multiple times by the members of your team. To reach the solution it will require an investigative thinking in a way that has not been done before. Lateral thinking that is taking an indirect approach to investigate an issue is a must-have skill in exploratory testing.

If you are able to think out of the box, you will be able to view the website from a different angle and find out new ways to interact or play with it. By taking a different approach, you will be able to discover bugs that have not been detected before.

Ability to Take Rational Approach

If you think systematically and follow the “rulebook”, you will be able to uncover critical bugs, but may miss out on certain simple bugs that may affect the system during production. The ability to take a rational approach and think with reasoning is must if you want to uncover those hidden bugs. For instance, if you are performing cross browser testing for your application then you will need to analyze and create a proper browser compatibility matrix and share a list of browsers on which the application is meant to run properly.

Be a Good Communicator

It is necessary to have good communication skills before you start executing exploratory testing. There will be times when you will find yourself sitting in a meeting with the development team or the stakeholders, where you would have to explain them about the scopes and strategies you are about to undertake. Letting other testers know about the bugs you found and how they are critical to business and should be prioritized accordingly also requires good communication skills. With effective communication, you would also be able to explain your findings in a detailed and relevant manner. Ability to communicate is a game-changer skill for exploratory testers.

Storytelling Ability — Make Bug Detection Interesting

Storytelling ability is one of the important skill for exploratory testing professional must have. When you detect a bug, the next step is to explain it to the developers that why getting this bug fixed is critical for business. Your job would be to create a fictional user and make the listeners understand that how that user will feel when he is unable to use the specific functionality. Since no developers like others to tell them that their code is wrong, you will be required to explain it to them in a friendly manner instead of directly pointing out the bug to them.

Ability to Work with a Team

Coordination is the most important skill for exploratory testing. Situations may arise when a particular bug or problem is not detected by an individual tester but detected by someone else from the team. It is rightly said that multiple brains work better than one. The ideal thing is to work collectively with your team and rule out personal biased opinions.

Be Technically Sound

Testing domains like visual testing or accessibility testing do not require much technical knowledge. However, exploratory testing is a completely different story. You won’t be required to do any coding, but you should have the knowledge of a full-stack developer. Starting from the user interface and ending at the database architecture, you must be able to understand what the code conveys and general coding of the different layers of the interface. Without sufficient technical knowledge, chances are there that you may overlook some critical bugs.

Be the Sherlock of Your Team

Just by mere observation, a skilled exploratory testing professional will be able to spot bugs that may have been missed out by others. Just by observing the system a skilled exploratory testing professional is able to find out probable bugs and raise questions long before the testing cycle began. Observational skills can be said to be the foundation of exploratory testing.

Ability to Think Critically & Effective Time Management

When you are thinking critically while performing testing, various arguments will come to your find that can explain the codes, leading to a new observation. Exploratory testers having critical thinking skill are ready to suggest actionable approach with respect to the risk associated. With critical analysis you can approach the product with an out of the box thinking, helping you to test your software in the wild. At present, most projects are following Agile Methodology, that means in a fixed time, both

development, as well as the testing phase of a user story, needs to be completed. Since exploratory testing takes up quite a lot of time, a tester should be proficient enough when it comes to time management so that he is able to complete the testing within the limited amount of time provided.

item.description:
在加利福尼亚寻找建议的小型企业会计服务。 别担心，我们始终提供最好的小型企业会计服务。 无论您是需要为初创企业做会计，还是需要会计，或者您是指定的企业，需要一些小企业账户方面的帮助，只需在上方输入您的位置，我们就会为您提供快速、免费的报价您所在地区最好的小型企业会计师。 加利福尼亚州的小型企业会计记录所有付款和费用，并将财务数据与销售数据分开。 这是一个重要的事实，可以帮助管理者实际跟踪和处理他们的资金——尤其是在开始阶段。 和其他小型企业会计一样，除了让您在不同时间执行时了解您的行业，私人公司簿记还有助于创建订单和完成财务。 我们提供一个真正的团队，了解贵公司财务的来龙去脉，然后将他们的专业知识与创造性工具相结合。 我们广泛的金融服务模式让您可以控制公司的财务状况，而不会感到压力和沮丧。 我们为人口众多的国家/地区的 50 多家商业和住宅房地产公司提供服务。 我们的高级管理团队拥有累积多年的外包经验。 我们要求您通过我们获得最佳的财务和会计外包体验。 我们获得支持您的企业财务成功的专业知识、技术和定制解决方案。 什么是小企业会计？ 分析金融交易日记账分录——日记账分录有两个账户——借方和贷方。 试算表 - 试算表已准备好测试借方总额是否等于贷方总额。 财务报表 拥有损益表、资产负债表、现金流量表和附注的财务报表是会计制度的命运产物。 结算分录 为组织下一个会计系统，完成偶尔标准的临时账户，包括收入、费用和提款账户。 会计流程的最后一步是设计结账后试算表，以在结账分录后测试借方和贷方金额的相等性。我们还在加利福尼亚州提供工资单、簿记和小型企业会计服务等其他服务。 我们始终提供与会计相关的服务，如果您有时间专注于真正的行业而不是处理一系列组织任务，您的小企业可以盈利多少？Analytix Answers 为您提供更好地管理会计和簿记功能的选项. 利用我们团队成员的先进技术专长，通过提高效率和强大的会计和簿记解决方案帮助您实现更高的盈利能力，从而最大限度地减少您在这些功能上花费的时间。 为小型企业会计定制的及时、费用有效、可扩展的解决方案，从 analytix 转化为您公司的业务增长。

item.title: 使用 Vue 构建在线课程网站 | Build an Online Course Site with Vue
item.description:
分享您的知识的一个好方法是在线课程。 我们可以构建自己的课程页面，而不是被知名课程平台提供的枯燥且不灵活的课程页面所困，这样我们就可以完全按照自己的喜好进行设计和用户体验。

在本教程中，我将向您展示如何使用 Vue 3 和 Vite 创建一个单页应用程序课程网站。 这些功能将包括基于降价的内容、嵌入式 Vimeo 视频和课程导航。

我们将使它成为一个静态站点，这样您就不需要后端了。 以下是主页、课程页面和课程页面的外观：

在本教程的最后，我还将向您展示如何（可选）招收学生，以便您可以跟踪学生进度并保护课程内容，以便您可以通过课程获利。 对于这一部分，我们将集成CourseKit ，它是一个用于托管在线课程的无头 API。

您可以在此处查看成品演示并在此处获取源代码。

使用 Vite 设置

让我们继续使用Vite设置我们的单页应用程序课程网站。

按照提示将项目命名为vue-course并选择 Vue 框架。

然后继续并进入目录并安装依赖项。

 cd vue-course npm install

我们还需要Vue Router来设置课程页面。

 $ npm install --save vue-router

完成后，让我们启动开发服务器并开始构建！

将路由器添加到项目

现在让我们创建一个文件来配置路由器：

我们现在需要编辑src/main.js并将路由器添加到我们的应用程序中。

配置路由器并创建页面

我们的课程应用程序将包含三个页面：

  • 将显示可用课程的主页。
  • 一个课程页面，将显示特定课程及其课程的信息。 这将有一个动态路线/courses/:courseId 。
  • 将显示特定课程的课程页面。 这将有一个动态路线/courses/:courseId/lessons/:lessonId 。

由于我们使用的是 Vue Router，我们将为每个页面创建一个组件。 让我们将它们放在目录src/pages中。

 $ mkdir src/pages $ touch src/pages/Home.vue $ touch src/pages/Course.vue $ touch src/pages/Lesson.vue

现在让我们配置路由器。 我们将导入路由器 API 和页面组件。 然后，我们将使用上述路径设置路线。 最后，我们将从文件中创建和导出路由器。

将页面添加到 App 组件

我们现在将转到App组件并清除内容。 然后，我们将创建自己的模板，在其中声明RouterView组件作为路由的出口。

完成后，我们已经设置了课程应用程序的页面结构。

创建课程数据文件

由于我们没有使用后端 API，我们的课程和课程的数据将存储在嵌套的 JavaScript 数组中。 该数组将用于填充我们应用程序的内容。

该数组将由具有id 、 title 、 description的课程对象和课程对象的子数组组成。

课程对象将有一个id 、 title和description ，并且还将包括一个vimeoId ，它将是课程视频的 ID（这将在下面解释）。

提示：确保您的 ID 是唯一且连续的。

创建主页

现在让我们开始构建我们的页面，从主页开始。 我们将首先从我们刚刚创建的模块中导入courses数组。

在组件模板中，我们将映射数组并将数据传递到新组件CourseSummary中。

该组件将显示每门课程的标题和描述，并提供指向该课程的链接，允许用户选择他们想要学习的课程。 我们通过 props 传入课程信息。

完成后，添加了一些 CSS 后，我们的主页将如下所示（为简洁起见，我不会在这里展示，但您可以在源代码中看到它。）。

创建课程页面

我们将创建的下一个页面是课程页面。 请注意，页面路径/courses/:courseId有一个用于课程 ID 的动态段，这是我们如何知道要显示哪个课程的数据的方式。

让我们使用 Vue Router 中可组合的useRoute在运行时提取动态段。

现在我们可以使用 ID 从带有数组find的课程数据中获取相关课程数据。

提示：如果 find 返回 null，您可能应该显示 404 页面。

我们现在可以为课程定义一个模板。 标题将包括页面顶部的面包屑和课程的详细信息，包括标题和描述。

然后，我们将获得带有“开始课程”文本的第一课的链接。 我们还将通过映射lessons子属性并将数据传递到另一个组件LessonSummary来显示课程中包含的课程的摘要。

课程摘要组件

与CourseSummary组件类似，该组件将接收带有课程数据的道具，这些数据可用于将标题和描述显示为可点击的链接。 这将允许用户直接导航到课程。

完成后，课程页面将如下所示：

创建课程页面

与课程页面类似，课程页面在 URL 中包含动态段。 这一次，我们有courseId和lessonId允许我们使用数组查找来检索正确的课程和课程对象。

每节课都会有一个相关的视频。 在此演示中，我们将使用Vimeo视频，尽管您可以使用任何允许嵌入到您自己的站点中的视频服务。

您需要做的就是在视频上传后获取视频的 ID 并将其添加到课程数据模块中。 在运行时，我们将嵌入一个 Vimeo 视频播放器并使用其 ID 加载视频。 为此，让我们安装Vue Vimeo Player组件。

 $ npm install vue-vimeo-player@next --save

课程页面组件

现在让我们为我们的课程页面组件创建一个模板。 与课程页面一样，我们将在模板顶部提供面包屑和课程标题。

然后，我们将使用Vimeo组件并通过我们的数据中的 vimeo ID 向它传递一个道具video 。

完成并继续按钮

我们将添加到课程页面的最后一件事是完成并继续按钮。 这允许用户在观看完视频后导航到下一课。

让我们创建一个名为CompleteAndContinueButton的新组件。 这将使用 Vue Router 的useRouter组合来导航到下一课（其 ID 作为道具传入）。

我们将在课程页面模板中的Vimeo组件下直接添加此组件。 请注意，我们需要获取下一个课程 ID 并将其作为道具传递。 我们将创建一个函数nextLessonId()来找到它。

参数更改时重新加载页面

这意味着完成和继续按钮将更改路线，但页面上的数据将保持不变。

在这种情况下，我们更愿意重新加载页面组件。 我们可以通过向路由器视图添加一个key属性并将完整的路由路径传递给它来做到这一点。 这意味着它将路由参数的每个组合视为单独的页面。

 <template> <div class= "App" > <main> <router-view :key= "$route.fullPath" ></router-view> </main> </div> </template>

完成后，这就是我们的课程页面的样子。 当然，该视频是可播放的，学生观看完后可以导航到下一课。

添加学生注册

目前，我们的应用程序具有课程的基本功能：学生可以选择课程、选择课程并观看视频。

不过，在线课程还有其他重要方面我们没有包括在内。

首先，个性化。 学生希望能够跟踪他们已经完成的课程，以防他们没有一口气完成课程。

其次，我们可能希望保护我们的内容，以便只有付费学生才能看到它。 这样我们就可以通过我们的课程获利。

这两个功能都需要一个允许学生注册的身份验证系统，以便我们知道他们购买了哪些课程以及他们完成了哪些课程。

课程包

创建课程后端是一项艰巨的任务。 另一种方法是使用CourseKit ，这是一个用于在线课程的无头 API，我们可以轻松地将其插入我们创建的应用程序中。

将 CourseKit 添加到我们的项目中

要将 CourseKit 添加到此项目，我们将创建一个帐户并在那里传输我们的课程数据。 然后，我们将使用 CourseKit JavaScript 客户端通过 API 调用数据。

这是添加 CourseKit 后课程页面的样子。 请注意在用户进行身份验证之前内容是如何隐藏的。

这是集成了 CourseKit 的该站点的完整演示。

试用 CourseKit

如果您想尝试一下，请在此处创建一个免费帐户：

开始使用 CourseKit



原文:

A great way to share your knowledge is with an online course. Rather than being stuck with the boring and inflexible lesson pages offered by the well-known course platforms, we can build our own so we can make the design and UX exactly how we like.

In this tutorial, I’ll show you how to create a single-page app course site using Vue 3 & Vite. The features will include markdown-based content, embedded Vimeo videos, and lesson navigation.

We’ll make this a static site so you won’t need a backend. Here’s what the home page, course page, and lesson page will look like:

At the end of the tutorial, I’ll also show you how to (optionally) enroll students so you can track student progress and protect lesson content so you can monetize your course. For this part, we’ll integrate CourseKit which is a headless API for hosting online courses.

You can view a demo of the finished product here and get the source code here.

Set up with Vite

Let’s go ahead and set up our single-page app course site using Vite.

Follow the prompts and name your project vue-course and select the Vue framework.

Then go ahead and change into the directory and install dependencies.

cd vue-course
npm install

We’ll also need Vue Router for setting up the course pages.

$ npm install --save vue-router

With that done, let’s fire up the dev server and start building!

Add router to project

Let's now create a file to configure the router:

We’ll now need to edit src/main.js and add the router to our app.

src/index.js

import { createApp } from 'vue'
import App from './App.vue'
import router from './router' const app = createApp(App)
app.use(router)
app.mount('#app')

Configure router and create pages

Our courses app will have three pages:

  • A home page that will show the available courses.
  • A course page that will show the info of a specific course and its lessons. This will have a dynamic route /courses/:courseId.
  • A lesson page that will show a specific lesson. This will have a dynamic route /courses/:courseId/lessons/:lessonId.

Since we’re using Vue Router, we’ll create a component for each of these pages. Let’s put these in the directory, src/pages.

$ mkdir src/pages
$ touch src/pages/Home.vue
$ touch src/pages/Course.vue
$ touch src/pages/Lesson.vue

Let's now configure the router. We'll import the router APIs and the page components. We'll then setup the routes with the paths mentioned above. Finally, we'll create and export the router from the file.

src/router.js

import { createRouter, createWebHistory } from 'vue-router'
import Home from './pages/Home.vue'
import Course from './pages/Course.vue'
import Lesson from './pages/Lesson.vue' const routes = [ { name: 'home', path: '/', component: Home }, { name: 'course', path: '/courses/:courseId', component: Course }, { name: 'lesson', path: '/courses/:courseId/lessons/:lessonId', component: Lesson }
] const router = createRouter({ history: createWebHistory(), routes,
}) export default router

Add pages to App component

We’ll now go to the App component and clear out the contents. We'll then create our own template where we declare the RouterView component that serves as an outlet for our routes.

src/App.vue

<template> <div class="App"> <main> <router-view></router-view> </main> </div>
</template>

With that done, we’ve set up the page structure of our course app.

Create course data file

Since we aren’t using a backend API, the data for our courses and lessons will be stored in a nested JavaScript array. This array will be used to populate the content of our app.

The array will consist of course objects with an id, title, description, and a sub-array of lesson objects.

The lesson objects will have an id, title, and description, and will also include a vimeoId which will be the ID for the lesson’s video (this will be explained below).

Tip: ensure your IDs are unique and sequential.

src/courses.js

const courses = [ { id: 1, title: "Photography for Beginners", description: "Phasellus ac tellus tincidunt...", lessons: [ { id: 1, title: "Welcome to the course", description: "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet...", vimeoId: 76979871 }, { id: 2, title: "How does a camera work?", description: "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet...", vimeoId: 76979871 }, ... ] }, { id: 2, title: "Advanced Photography", description: "Cras ut sem eu ligula luctus ornare quis nec arcu.", lessons: [ ... ] }, ...
] export default courses

Create home page

Let’s now start building our pages, beginning with the home page. We’ll first import the courses array from the module we just created.

In the component template, we’ll map the array and pass the data into a new component CourseSummary.

src/pages/Home.vue

<script setup>
import courses from '../courses'
import CourseSummary from '../components/CourseSummary.vue'
</script> <template> <div class="Home page"> <header> <h1>Vue Online Course Site</h1> </header> <CourseSummary v-for="course in courses" :key="course.id" :course="course" /> </div>
</template>

CourseSummary component

This component will display each course's title and description and will provide a link to the course, allowing the user to select the course they want to take. We pass in the course information via props.

src/components/CourseSummary.vue

<script setup>
defineProps({ course: { type: Object, required: true }
})
</script> <template> <section class="summary"> <div> <div class="title"> <h2> <router-link class="no-underline cursor-pointer" :to="{ name: 'course', params: { courseId: course.id } }" > {{ course.title }} </router-link> </h2> </div> <p> <router-link class="no-underline cursor-pointer" :to="{ name: 'course', params: { courseId: course.id } }" > {{ course.description }} </router-link> </p> </div> </section>
</template>

With that done, here’s what our home page will look like once a bit of CSS has been added (I won’t show that here for brevity but you can see it in the source code.).

Create course page

The next page we’ll create is the course page. Note that the page path /courses/:courseId has a dynamic segment for the course ID which is how we know which course’s data to show.

Let’s use the useRoute composable from Vue Router to extract the dynamic segment at runtime.

src/pages/Course.vue

import { useRoute } from 'vue-router'
const route = useRoute()
const courseId = route.params.courseId
console.log(courseId) // 1

Now we can use the ID to get the relevant course data from the courses data with an array find.

Tip: if the find returns null you should probably show a 404 page.

src/pages/Course.vue

import courses from '../courses'
import { useRoute } from 'vue-router'
const route = useRoute()
const courseId = route.params.courseId
const course = courses.find(course => course.id === parseInt(courseId))
const { title, lessons } = course

We can now define a template for the course. The header will include a breadcrumb at the top of the page and details of the course including the title and description.

We’ll then have a link to the first lesson with the text “Start course”. We’ll also display summaries of the lessons included in the course which we create by mapping over the lessons sub-property and passing data to another component LessonSummary.

src/pages/Course.vue

<script setup>
import courses from '../courses'
import { useRoute } from 'vue-router'
import LessonSummary from '../components/LessonSummary.vue'
const route = useRoute()
const courseId = route.params.courseId
const course = courses.find(course => course.id === parseInt(courseId))
const { title, lessons } = course
</script> <template> <div class="Course page"> <header> <p> <router-link :to="{ name: 'home' }">Back to courses</router-link> </p> <h1>{{ title }}</h1> <p>{{ description }}</p> <router-link class="button primary icon" :to="`/courses/${courseId}/lessons/${course.lessons[0].id}`" > Start course </router-link> </header> <div> <LessonSummary v-for="(lesson, index) in lessons" :key="index" :course-id="courseId" :lesson="lesson" :num="index + 1" /> </div> </div>
</template>

LessonSummary component

Similar to the CourseSummary component, this one will receive props with the lesson’s data which can be used to show a title and description as a clickable link. This will allow users to navigate directly to a lesson.

src/components/LessonSummary.vue

<script setup>
defineProps({ courseId: { type: String, required: true }, num: { type: Number, required: true }, lesson: { type: Object, required: true }
})
</script> <template> <section class="summary"> <div> <div class="title"> <h2> <router-link class="no-underline cursor-pointer" :to="'/courses/' + courseId + '/lessons/' + lesson.id" > {{ num }}. {{ lesson.title }} </router-link> </h2> </div> <p> <router-link class="no-underline cursor-pointer" :to="'/courses/' + courseId + '/lessons/' + lesson.id" > {{ lesson.description }} </router-link> </p> </div> </section>
</template>

With that done, here’s what the course page will look like:

Create lesson page

Similar to the course page, the lesson page includes dynamic segments in the URL. This time, we have both a courseId and lessonId allowing us to retrieve the correct course and lesson objects using array finds.

src/pages/Lesson.vue

<script setup>
import courses from '../courses'
import { useRoute } from 'vue-router'
const route = useRoute()
const { courseId, lessonId } = route.params
const course = courses.find(course => course.id === parseInt(courseId))
const lesson = course.lessons.find(lesson => lesson.id === parseInt(lessonId))
</script>

Vimeo embed

Each lesson will have an associated video. In this demo, we’ll be using a Vimeo video, though you could use any video service that allows embedding in your own site.

All you need to do is grab the video’s ID after it has been uploaded and add it to the courses data module. The ID is normally a number like 76979871.

At runtime, we’ll embed a Vimeo video player and load the video using its ID. To do this, let’s install the Vue Vimeo Player component.

$ npm install vue-vimeo-player@next --save

Lesson page component

Now let’s create a template for our Lesson page component. Like the course page, we’ll provide a breadcrumb and the lesson title at the top of the template.

We’ll then use the Vimeo component and pass it a prop video with the vimeo ID from our data.

src/pages/Lesson.vue

<script setup>
import courses from '../courses'
import { useRoute } from 'vue-router'
import { vueVimeoPlayer } from 'vue-vimeo-player'
const route = useRoute()
const { courseId, lessonId } = route.params
const course = courses.find(course => course.id === parseInt(courseId))
const lesson = course.lessons.find(lesson => lesson.id === parseInt(lessonId))
</script> <template> <div class="Lesson page"> <header> <p> <router-link :to="'/courses/' + course.id">Back to {{ course.title }}</router-link> </p> <h1>{{ lesson.title }}</h1> </header> <div class="Content"> <vue-vimeo-player :video-id="lesson.vimeoId" :options="{ responsive: true }" /> </div> </div>
</template>

Complete and continue button

The last thing we’ll add to the lesson page is a Complete and continue button. This allows the user to navigate to the next lesson once they’ve finished watching the video.

Let’s create a new component called CompleteAndContinueButton. This will use Vue Router’s useRouter composable to navigate to the next lesson (whose ID is passed in as a prop).

src/components/CompleteAndContinueButton.vue

<script setup>
import { useRouter } from 'vue-router'
const router = useRouter()
const props = defineProps({ lessonId: { type: String, required: true }
})
function completeAndContinue () { router.push(`/courses/${course.id}/lessons/${props.lessonId}`)
}
</script> <template> <button class="button primary" @click="completeAndContinue"> Complete and continue </button>
</template>

We’ll add this component directly under the Vimeo component in the lesson page template. Note that we’ll need to get the next lesson ID and pass it as a prop. We’ll create a function nextLessonId() to find this.

src/pages/Lesson.js

<script setup>
import courses from '../courses'
import { useRoute } from 'vue-router'
import { vueVimeoPlayer } from 'vue-vimeo-player'
import CompleteAndContinueButton from '../components/CompleteAndContinueButton.vue'
const route = useRoute()
const { courseId, lessonId } = route.params
const course = courses.find(course => course.id === parseInt(courseId))
const lesson = course.lessons.find(lesson => lesson.id === parseInt(lessonId))
const currentIndex = course.lessons.indexOf(lesson)
const nextIndex = (currentIndex + 1) % course.lessons.length
const nextLessonId = course.lessons[nextIndex].id.toString()
</script> <template> <div class="Lesson page"> <header> <p> <router-link :to="'/courses/' + course.id">Back to {{ course.title }}</router-link> </p> <h1>{{ lesson.title }}</h1> </header> <div class="Content"> <vue-vimeo-player :video-id="lesson.vimeoId" :options="{ responsive: true }" /> <CompleteAndContinueButton :courseId="courseId" :lessonId="nextLessonId" /> </div> </div>
</template>

Reloading page on param change

One of the quirks of Vue Router is that changing route params does not reload the page component. This means the complete and continue button will change the route but the data on the page will stay the same.

In this case, we'd prefer to reload the page component. We can do this by adding a key attribute to the router view and passing to it the full route path. This means it will treat each combination of route params as separate pages.

<template> <div class="App"> <main> <router-view :key="$route.fullPath"></router-view> </main> </div>
</template>

With that done, here’s what our lesson page will look like. The video is, of course, playable, and the student can navigate to the next lesson once they’ve finished watching.

Add student enrollments

Right now, our app has the basic functionality of a course: a student can select a course, select a lesson, and watch the video.

There are other important aspects of online courses that we have not included, though.

Firstly, personalization. Students want to be able to track the lessons they’ve already completed in case they don't finish the course in one go.

Secondly, we may want to protect our content so only paying students can see it. That way we can monetize our course.

Both these features require an auth system allowing students to enroll so we know which courses they’ve purchased and which lessons they’ve completed.

CourseKit

Creating a course backend is an arduous task. An alternative is to use CourseKit, a headless API for online courses which we could easily plug into the app we’ve created.

CourseKit is designed to provide exactly the features we’re missing in our app: student management and role-based access to content.

Adding CourseKit to our project

To add CourseKit to this project we'd create an account and transfer our course data there. We’d then use the CourseKit JavaScript client to call the data through the API.

Here’s what the lesson page would look like if we added CourseKit. Note how the content is hidden until the user authenticates.

Here’s the full demo of this site with CourseKit integrated.

Try CourseKit

CourseKit is currently in public beta, meaning it is launched and it works, but some features (e.g. analytics) are still in progress.

If you’d like to try it out, create a free account here:

Get started with CourseKit
item.author: anthonygore

item.title: 向您的软件开发合作伙伴询问的热门问题 | Top Questions to Ask Your Software Development Partner
item.description:
SilverTouch 技术

发表于实际上，在联系那家软件开发公司之前，您应该已经弄清楚了这个问题的答案。 您应该根据自己的一套标准来处理软件业务，例如反映他们经验的产品组合和服务。 任何成功的软件产品开发公司的产品组合中都应该包含一系列成功案例。 要发现他们是否适合您的组织，请检查他们以前的一些工作。 此外，不要害怕向公司以前的客户索取推荐信。 当您将所有内容加起来时，您应该能够判断他们是否可以提供您需要的结果。

我将与谁一起工作，团队的结构如何？

这是一个比你想象的更复杂的问题。 答案不仅应该与您自己的项目管理风格完美匹配，而且从软件开发公司的角度来看，它还应该结构良好。 在与组织进行咨询或简单对话期间，您将能够判断工作流程是否经过深思熟虑。 您应该完全了解项目管理风格、您选择的软件开发业务将在您的项目中使用的方法、将使用的冲刺或实现等等。 您的查询越具体（尤其是在第一个响应非常广泛的情况下），您就越能够预测会发生什么、可以提供多少以及如何提供。

他们采用的项目管理风格也可能不是您想要实施的，或者只是不适合您的偏好。 这是识别并在必要时解决某些潜在瓶颈的绝佳机会。

我的联系人是谁？

聘请专门的开发人员需要良好的沟通。 没有开诚布公的对话，项目就无法按需推进，但太多人的太多沟通会混淆整个过程。 最好的策略是指定一个特定的联系点。 然后，他们将能够回答您的所有疑问，并让您了解项目的发展。

我们将如何沟通？

当涉及到外部软件开发公司和招聘公司之间的任何合作时，沟通对于成功至关重要。 确保您选择的公司了解及时、一致和诚实沟通的必要性。
设置一些沟通方式和联系时间。 软件开发业务将知道他们期望收到您的消息的频率，并且您将知道他们应该何时可用。 此外，确定一般的工作时间和沟通时间（例如，您会要求他们在周末回复您的建议，还是他们可以等到周一？）找到一个协议点并坚持下去，但在签署之前这样做任何事物。

你的开发过程是什么样的？

不同的定制软件开发公司使用不同的项目管理风格。 无论是敏捷还是瀑布式，这都适合您公司的工作方式，这一点至关重要。 项目经理使用敏捷是因为它允许客户在整个项目中提供反馈，同时降低开支。

你能估计完成日期吗？

从一开始就确定准确的截止日期至关重要。 公司计算软件开发时间的方法应该是完全透明的，随着项目的进展这种变化的可能性也应该是完全透明的。 当你在谈论它时，询问他们如何计算与提议的时间表相关的成本。

交货后会发生什么？

这是一个重要的问题。 即使在开发完成后，该项目仍将需要

我为什么要选择你们公司？

显然，您将专注于金钱、影响和过程。 但是，通过提出这样的问题，您将获得一些心理和“人情味”的背景。 您选择一家特定的软件开发公司是有原因的，但他们认为原因是什么？ 为什么那家软件开发公司相信他们可以满足您的需求？ 在他们看来，是什么让他们在竞争中脱颖而出？
这样一个问题的答案可能会相当令人惊讶，甚至是开箱即用的，因此如果你有机会问它不是一个坏主意。

海登沃兹沃斯—— Apr 17 4月17日



原文:

SilverTouch technologies

Posted on Apr 26

Actually, you should have figured out the answer to this issue before contacting that software development firm. You should approach software businesses based on your own set of criteria, such as a portfolio and services that reflect their experience. A portfolio of success stories should be included in any successful software product development company's portfolio. To discover if they're a good fit for your organization, ask to examine some of their previous work. Also, don't be afraid to ask for references from the company's previous customers. When you add it all up, you should be able to tell if they can deliver the outcomes you require.

Who will I be working with, and how is the team structured?

This is a more complicated question than you might assume. Not only should the answer be a perfect match for your own project management style, but it should also be well-structured from the software development company's perspective. During a consultation or a simple conversation with the organization, you will be able to tell if the workflow is well-thought-out. You should be completely informed of the project management style, the methodology that the software development business of your choice will use in your project, the sprints or implementations that will be used, and so on. The more specific your queries are (especially if the first response was very broad), the more you'll be able to predict what to expect, how much can be provided, and how.

It's also possible that the project management style they employ isn't something you're looking to implement, or just isn't suited to your preferences. This is an excellent opportunity to identify and, if necessary, resolve certain potential bottlenecks.

Who is My Contact person?

Hiring dedicated developers requires excellent communication. Projects cannot advance as needed without open conversation, yet too much communication from too many people muddles the entire process. The best strategy is to designate a specific point of contact. They will then be able to answer all of your queries and keep you informed about the project's development.

How will we communicate?

When it comes to any collaboration between an external software development company and the hiring corporation, communication is vital to success. Make sure the company you've picked understands the need for timely, consistent, and honest communication.
Set up some communication methods and a schedule for when you'll be in touch. The software development business will know how frequently they can expect to hear from you, and you will know when they should be available. Also, decide on working hours and communication times in general (for example, will you require them to respond to your suggestions on weekends, or can they wait until Monday?) Find a point of agreement and stick to it, but do so before signing anything.

What does your development process look like?

Different project management styles are used by different bespoke software development companies. It's critical that this fits your company's working style, whether agile or waterfall. Agile is used by project managers because it allows clients to provide feedback throughout the project while also lowering expenses.

Can you estimate a date for completion?

It's critical to establish precise deadlines from the start. The company's method for calculating software development time should be totally transparent, as should the possibility of this changing as the project progresses. Ask how they compute the cost in relation to the proposed timetable while you're talking about it.

What happens after delivery?

It's an important question to ask. Even after the development has been completed the project constantly will need

Why should I choose your company?

Obviously, you'll be focusing on money, impacts, and the process. However, by asking such a question, you will gain some psychological and "human-touch" context. You've chosen a particular software development firm for a reason, but what do they believe that reason is? Why does that software development firm believe they can meet your needs? What sets them apart from the competition, in their opinion?
The answer to such a question could be rather surprising and even out of the box, therefore it's not a bad idea to ask it if you get the chance.

Hayden Wadsworth - Apr 17

item.title: 手动在 WordPress 上设置 Seers Cookie 同意横幅 | Setup Seers Cookie Consent Banner on WordPress Manually
item.description:
尼克斯宾塞为先知

发表于要在您的网站上正确集成 Seers CMP 并手动标记 cookie 设置脚本，请遵循以下过程：

第1步：
登录/注册到Seers并转到Seers 仪表板

第2步：
单击Cookie 同意并添加域以扫描必要和不必要的 cookie。 扫描您的网站可能需要 5-10 分钟。

第 3 步：
扫描后，点击设置图标并选择：

  1. 一个标准：一键设置手动集成
  2. 自定义：高级设置

选择手动集成并复制横幅脚本：

<script data-key="$2y$10$9jv3BGFlOaP5WsWmbODWFuUE33LrD3b5HzH52ePv2622WRJYOHWG" data-name="CookieXray" src="https://cdn.seersco.com/banners/22768/17303/cb.js" type="text/javascript"></script>

第4步：
登录您的 WordPress 网站

在左侧功能区中：

  1. 点击“外观”选项
  2. 然后从子菜单中，单击“主题文件编辑器”选项

在主题文件编辑器中，搜索header.php以接近标题部分代码。

将脚本粘贴到您网站模板的标题部分，以显示 cookie 同意横幅。



原文:

Nick Spencer for Seers

Posted on Apr 26

To integrate Seers CMP properly on your website and manually markup cookie-setting scripts, follow the process below:

Step 1:
Login/Signup to Seers & Go to Seers Dashboard

Step 2:
Click on Cookie Consent and Add Domain for Scanning necessary and unnecessary cookies. It might take 5-10 minutes to scan your website.

Step 3:
After scanning, Click on Settings icon and Select:

  1. One Standard: One Click Setup for Manual Integration
  2. Customized: Advanced Settings

Select Manual Integration and Copy the Banner Script:

<script data-key="$2y$10$9jv3BGFlOaP5WsWmbODWFuUE33LrD3b5HzH52ePv2622WRJYOHWG" data-name="CookieXray" src="https://cdn.seersco.com/banners/22768/17303/cb.js" type="text/javascript"></script>

Step 4:
Login to your WordPress site

In Left ribbon:

  1. Click "Appearance" option
  2. Then from sub menu, Click on "Theme File Editor" option

In Theme File Editor, Search for header.php to approaching the header section code.

Paste script into Header Section of your website template in order to display the cookie consent banner.

item.title: 探索高阶函数 | A Quest to Understanding Higher Order Functions
item.description:
当您开始寻求成为一名程序员时，在线研究不同的主题可能会令人生畏。 最初只配备基础知识， MDN Web Docs和Stack Overflow等资源同时很有帮助和压倒性。 当面对一个最初令人费解的概念时，情境学习可以帮助理解和更深入地理解当前不熟悉的主题。 情境学习基于个人觉得熟悉的东西，但有一些几乎每个人都会熟悉的东西（即使他们不知道）——英雄之旅。

英雄的旅程或“单一神话”是约瑟夫坎贝尔假设的几乎每个英雄任务驱动的故事的基本框架。 除非一个人陷入困境并否认媒体或文学（如果是的话，绝对没有判断力），你可能已经在星球大战、霍比特人、奥德赛、哈利波特、莉洛和史迪奇等知名故事中目睹了英雄的旅程、狮子王、黑客帝国或任何印第安纳琼斯电影（仅举几例）。 使用单一神话作为我们的上下文学习基础 - 让我们通过我们自己的英雄旅程的镜头探索学习高阶函数的不同方式！

召唤冒险/出发

很久以前，在 JavaScript 的土地上，七月的一个特别下雨的日子，一位来访的王子被一条龙捉住了。 居住的公主被龙的大胆激怒了，担心如果她让他们的王子被龙吃掉，邻国会以此为借口发动战争。 没有办法，她发誓要亲自救他，立即开始准备。

拿到基本必需品之后，是时候决定她应该使用什么来对抗巨龙了。 这是她第一次与龙发生争执，公主不知道她应该期待什么。 她想要一些能够根据自己所处的情况适应并返回不同结果的东西。经过几分钟的仔细思考，她得到了她的“啊哈！” 片刻。 只有一种武器具有她所寻找的灵活性。 公主宣布她将创造 JavaScript 著名的武器类型：致命且具有魔法功能的武器。

在 JavaScript 中自定义同时声明您创建函数的意图并命名即将制作的武器。 这样的武器需要一个有价值的标识符，这样吟游诗人就可以在讲述她的英勇事迹时唱出它的名字。 作为一个务实的人，公主给这把武器起了一个特定的名字——屠龙者。

功能武器的灵活性来自于它具有处理不同情境输入并根据这些输入返回无数不同结果的能力。 但公主并不想制造基础机能武器，她下定决心要制造传说中的高阶机能武器。 这是一个以通过接受其他函数作为输入甚至返回其他函数来添加增强功能的能力而闻名的函数。 首先，公主拿出她可靠的记事本来写下她的想法并确定她的策略。

并不是说她对自己要创建的东西有一个基本的了解，她收集了她需要的括号、花括号、分号和函数调用，以便制作她准备将她的函数焊接在一起的武器。 从 dragonSlayer 开始，她通过将参数锤入括号中并在旁边和旁边制作不可穿透的花括号来创建函数的基础。 接下来是大括号内处理过程中的微妙部分，即要返回的函数（以下称为子武器）。 子武器首先需要与初始功能相同的处理（除了放弃功能命名的传统）。 然后它被灌输了一个条件语句——这将控制剑能量的流动。 成功调用后，剑将由发光定义，并执行它被告知要完成的任何任务/结果。 否则，剑将没有定义，即未定义。

 function dragonSlayer(requiredAction, desiredResult){ return function (actualAction){ if(requiredAction === actualAction){ return desiredResult; } } }

强大的武器——屠龙者

这么费时费力的辛苦劳作之后，是时候赶紧制作出那些能让剑发动的工具了。 所有函数都必须与函数调用配对才能激活。 根据其随附的武器名称和括号，进行函数调用以接收不同的输入（通俗地称为arguments ）并将这些输入传达给武器以代替参数。 出于功能武器的目的，将它们视为类似于音频/视频记录器。 高阶函数需要额外的函数调用来激活其中的武器。 公主决定创建一个变量来保存第一个武器调用。 然后，此变量将用于调用子武器并调用最终武器返回。 在选择了几个不同的变量后，公主决定使用经典的var 。 她在上面蚀刻了一个识别名称，并用赋值运算符附加了一个基本的函数调用。 瞧。 她甚至没有停下脚步，开始了她拯救倒霉王子的旅程的下一阶段！

 var callToAction = dragonSlayer(); callToAction();

激活工具：变量和函数调用

引发

在穿越沙漠和苔原的危险旅程之后，她到达了巨龙的巢穴。 她尽可能地爬到巢穴附近，躲在一块岩石后面，凝视着上面。 她发现王子被金链子绑在龙的宝库上。 不幸的是，冲动的王子同时发现了她。 看到她，他大喊‘救救我，坏蛋公主！ 你是我唯一的希望！”

她隐身的机会现在不存在了——她必须立即采取行动。 她告诉她的变量她希望完成的动作和想要的结果“做鬼鬼祟祟，让自己转身”，“刺龙眼”。 然后，她确保她的下一个 functionCall 已准备好记录她的操作以进行最终调用。 公主冲上前去，开始胡作非为，但她匆匆忙忙没有转身。 她趴在她的屁股上，屠龙者一动不动地躺在她的手中。 她又羞又怕地跑出了山洞。 巨龙对这次彻底的失败嗤之以鼻，并没有费心去追随显然没有威胁的事情。

 function dragonSlayer(requiredAction, desiredResult){ return function (actualAction){ if(requiredAction === actualAction){ return desiredResult; } } } var callToAction = dragonSlayer(“do the hokey pokey and turn yourself about”, “stab dragon eye ball”); callToAction("do the hokey pokey");

公主几乎看不见山洞了，她想“好吧，不——这不是事情的发展方向。我不是为了逃跑而费这么多麻烦。” 她冲回山洞，再次告诉变量她的意图（“竖起中指”，“指向龙喉”）。 而她正是这样做的！ 屠龙者发光并活了过来，靠在巨龙的喉咙上。

 function dragonSlayer(requiredAction, desiredResult){ return function (actualAction){ if(requiredAction === actualAction){ return desiredResult; } } } var callToAction = dragonSlayer("throw up the middle finger", "point against dragon throat"); callToAction("throw up the middle finger");

巨龙投降了她的爪子和翅膀。 巨龙喊道。 听龙的话，公主的怒火减轻了。 她理解多愁善感的价值，作为一个内向的人，她理解界限和个人空间的重要性。 公主同情巨龙的困境，将屠龙者从巨龙的喉咙中取出，并承诺在她回来后，只要巨龙能沟通，她就会尽其所能纠正这种情况，而不是采取绑架的方式。

返回

公主对交流的重要性有了新的认识，将王子甩在肩上，开始长途跋涉回家。 在她回来后，她下令让这条龙完全独自一人。 王子和公主成了笔友，从此过上了幸福的生活。



原文:

When starting your quest to become a programmer, researching different topics online can be daunting. Initially armed with only the basics, sources like MDN Web Docs and Stack Overflow are simultaneously helpful and overwhelming. When faced with an initially puzzling concept, contextual learning can help with comprehension and a deeper understanding of currently unfamiliar topics. Contextual learning is based on what individuals find familiar, but there is something almost everyone will be familiar with (even if they don’t know it) - the hero’s journey.

The hero’s journey or ‘the monomyth’ is what Joseph Campbell posits is the underlying framework for almost every hero-quest driven story. Unless a person has been under a rock and denied media or literature (absolutely no judgment if so), you’ve probably witnessed the hero’s journey within well known tales such as Star Wars, the Hobbit, the Odyssey, Harry Potter, Lilo and Stitch, the Lion King, the Matrix, or any Indiana Jones flick (to name just a few). Using the monomyth as our contextual learning basis - lets explore a different way of learning about higher-order functions through the lens of our own hero's journey!

Call to Adventure/Departure

A long time ago, in the land of JavaScript, on a particularly rainy day in July, a visiting prince was captured by a dragon. The resident princess was enraged by the audacity of the dragon and worried the neighboring kingdom would see this as an excuse to go to war if she let their prince get eaten by a dragon. There was no help for it, she vowed she would rescue him herself and immediately began to prepare.

After grabbing the basic necessities it was time to determine what she should use in order to fight the dragon. This being her first impending altercation with a dragon, the princess didn't know what she should expect. She wanted something that would be able to adapt and return different results depending on the situation she found herself in. After a few minutes of careful thought, she had her ‘aha!’ moment. There was only one type of weapon with the flexibility she was looking for. The princess declared she would create the type of weapon JavaScript was known for: the deadly and magical function weapon.

It was custom in JavaScript to simultaneously declare your intent to create a function and name the soon to be crafted weapon. Such a weapon would need a worthy identifier so the bards would be able to sing its name when recounting her brave deeds. Being a pragmatic person, the princess gave the weapon a name specific to its purpose - dragonSlayer.

The function weapon's flexibility came from its being imbued with the ability to process different situational inputs and return countless different results based on those inputs. But the princess didn't want to create a basic function weapon, she was determined to create the fabled Higher Order Function weapon. This was a function known for its ability to add enhanced functionality by either accepting other functions as inputs or even returning other functions. To get started, the princess whipped out her trusty notepad in order to write out her thoughts and determine her strategy.

Not that she had a basic idea of what she was creating, she gathered the parentheses, curly brackets, semi-colons, and function calls she would need in order to craft her weapon she was ready to weld her function together. Starting with dragonSlayer, she created the base of the function by hammering her parameters into the parentheses and with the impenetrable curly brackets fashioned next and alongside. Next came the delicate part of the process fashioning within the curly brackets, the function to be returned (henceforth known as the sub-weapon). The sub-weapon first required the same treatment as the initial function (except for forgoing the tradition of function naming). It was then imbued with a conditional statement - that would control the flow of the sword's energy. When successfully invoked, the sword would be defined by a glowing light and perform whatever task/result it was told to complete. Else, the sword would have no definition ie be undefined.

function dragonSlayer(requiredAction, desiredResult){
   return function (actualAction){
      if(requiredAction === actualAction){
         return desiredResult; 
      }
   }
}

The formidable weapon - dragonSlayer

After such intensive and time-consuming labor, it was time to hurriedly create those tools that would allow the sword to be invoked. All functions had to be paired with a function call in order to be activated. Fashioned out of its accompanying weapon's name and parentheses, function calls are made to intake different inputs (colloquially known as arguments) and communicate these inputs to the weapon to take the place of the parameters. For the purposes of the function weapons, think of them as similar to an audio/video recorder. Higher Order Functions require additional function calls to activate the weapons within them. The princess decided to create a variable to hold the first weapon call. This variable would then be used to call the sub-weapon and invoke the final weapon return. Having the choice of a few different variables, the princess decided to use a classic var. She etched an identifying name on to it and attached a basic function call to it with an assignment operator. Voila. dragonSlayer and its calls had been made. Not even stopping for a brief respite, she embarked on the next stage of her journey to save the hapless prince!

var callToAction = dragonSlayer();
callToAction();

The activation tools: variable and function call

Initiation

After a perilous journey through desert and tundra, she arrived at the dragon's lair. Creeping as close to the lair as she dared, she hid behind a rock and peered above it. She spotted the prince tied up with gold chains atop the dragon's hoard. Unfortunately, the impulsive prince spied her at the same time. Upon seeing her, he yelled 'Help me, Princess Badass! You're my only hope!"

Her opportunity for stealth now non-existent - she had to take immediate action. She told her variable the action she hoped to accomplish and the desired result “do the hokey pokey and turn yourself about”, “stab dragon eye ball”. She then made sure her next functionCall was ready to record her action for the final invocation. The princess rushed forth and started the hokey pokey, but in her haste she failed to turn herself about. She fell on her bum and dragonSlayer lay stagnant in her hand. Mortified and frightened she raced out of the cave. The dragon chortled at this utter failure and didn't bother to follow what was obviously no threat.

function dragonSlayer(requiredAction, desiredResult){
   return function (actualAction){
      if(requiredAction === actualAction){
         return desiredResult; 
      }
   }
}

var callToAction = dragonSlayer(“do the hokey pokey and turn yourself about”, “stab dragon eye ball”);

callToAction("do the hokey pokey");

dragonSlayer in action - initial attempt

The princess was almost out of sight of the cave when she thought "Okay, nope - this is not how this is going to go. I didn’t go to all this trouble just to run away." She rushed back to the cave and once again told the variable her intentions ("throw up the middle finger", "point against dragon throat"). And she did exactly that! dragonSlayer glowed and sprang to life, leveling itself against the dragon's throat.

function dragonSlayer(requiredAction, desiredResult){
   return function (actualAction){
      if(requiredAction === actualAction){
         return desiredResult; 
      }
   }
}

var callToAction = dragonSlayer("throw up the middle finger", "point against dragon throat");

callToAction("throw up the middle finger");

dragonSlayer in action - successful attempt

The dragon threw up her claws and wings in surrender. "Please don't!" yelled the dragon. "I know it probably wasn't the best way to go about it, but I was sick of you humans robbing me blind. There are a lot of sentimental items in my hoard... I didn’t want to hurt anyone. I just want to be left alone." Listening to the dragon, the princess's rage lessened. She understood the value of sentimentality and, as an introvert, the importance of boundaries and personal space. Sympathizing with the dragon's plight, the princess removed dragonSlayer from the dragon's throat and pledged to do what she could upon her return to rectify the situation as long as the dragon communicated in the future, instead of resorting to kidnapping.

Return

With a newfound respect for the importance of communication, the princess threw the prince over her shoulder and began the long walk home. Upon her return, she issued a decree that the dragon should be left completely alone. The prince and the princess became penpals and they all lived happily ever after.
item.author: zoekhall

item.title: 关于 Asahi Linux 的更多注意事项 - 02 | Some More Notes on Asahi Linux - 02
item.description:
我得到了以太网适配器，瞧！ 它立即起作用。 我安装了对话框包，然后使用了 wifi-menu，当我连接到我的家庭网络时，它似乎抛出了一个错误，但我发现我已经连接到了 wifi。

看起来我在 /etc/netctl/my_profile 下编写的 wifi 配置文件已经在工作了。

这是我用来更新 pacman 包管理器的命令。 这似乎与进行 apt-get update 相同。

我还必须安装 wpa_supplicant 包。

这很简单。 我只是在 shell 中运行 xterm 时才发现这一点，这有点烦人。 最好有一个终端窗口来输出 X 正在做什么。

我现在遇到的最大问题是我的终端文本很小。 我改变了 X 的 dpi，这很有帮助，但我的终端仍然很糟糕。 不确定我是要处理它还是只是安装一个不同的终端。

我忘了它看起来多么平淡。 我真的想要macos的波兰级别，但如果这有意义的话我也不想要。

那好吧！ 我安装了 dmenu，因为这是我喜欢但忘记了的东西。 还安装了sudo。

我需要让我的 vpn 连接正常工作，因为我确实计划在我去意大利时做一些工作。

我将终端切换到 kitty，并且使用我正在使用的 dpi 设置开箱即用。 随着我的终端和 wifi 正常工作，而且我将 dpi 降低到 144，firefox 看起来也没有那么糟糕了，我认为我的 asahi linux 安装现在很容易使用。 我已经安装了很多东西，最大的东西是 vim 和 fish。 我真的想要一个具有鱼功能的 bash shell。

我也使用星舰提示，但我可能没有它。

我真的很喜欢用三个手指粘贴。

在这一点上，我认为我的 asahi linux 设置已经完成。 幸运的是，我没有遇到太多问题。 如此轻松地完成这项工作的人是疯了。

使用 feh 添加背景，将其添加到 i3 配置中，以便在我启动时加载。 下一步是添加任务栏，因为我想要某种底座。

安装rust，该死的速度明显更快。 我的 PC 是游戏设备，但 wsl2 用了 1.88 秒，而 mac 用了 0.16 秒。 一定有什么问题。 我需要检查我的 Windows 版本是否在做一些古怪的事情。



原文:

I got the ethernet adapter and voila! It worked immediately. I installed the dialog package and then used wifi-menu which seemed to throw an error when I connected to my home network but I found that I was already connected to the wifi.

It looks like my wifi profile that I wrote under /etc/netctl/my_profile was already working.

This is the command I used to update the pacman package manager. This seems to be the same as doing apt-get update.

I also had to install the wpa_supplicant package.

Once I got wifi working, I disconnected the ethernet adapter and installed X and i3-gaps. This was pretty straightforward. Xterm wasn't appearing because the xorg-xmessage package was missing. I only found this out when I ran xterm in the shell which was a bit annoying. It'd be nice to have a terminal window that would output what X is doing.

The biggest issue that I have now is that my terminal text is tiny. I changed the dpi of X which helped quite a bit but my terminal is still screwy. Not sure if I want to deal with it or just install a different terminal.

Firefox is also ugly as sin. I forgot how bland it looks. I really want the polish level of macos but I also don't if that makes sense.

Oh well! I installed dmenu as which is something I love that I forgot about. Also installed sudo.

I'll need to get my vpn connection to work working as I do plan on doing some work when I go to Italy.

I switched my terminal to kitty and that worked out of the box with the dpi setting I'm using. With my terminal and wifi working, and firefox not looking as bad now that I lowered the dpi to 144, I think my asahi linux install is pretty easy to use now. I've installed a whole slew of things, the biggest thing being vim and fish. I realllly want a bash shell with fish capabilities.

I also use the starship prompt but I could probably do without it.

I really like using three fingers to paste.

At this point I'd consider my asahi linux set up pretty much done. Luckily I didn't run into too many issues. The guys who got this working so easily are insane.

Used feh to add a background, added it to the i3 config so it loads when I start. The next step is add taskbar as I would like a dock of some sort.

Installed rust, Goddamn is it significantly faster. My PC is a gaming rig but wsl2 took 1.88s vs mac's 0.16s. Something must be wrong. I'll need to check if my windows version is doing something wacky.

item.title: 不要在 JavaScript 中使用 Switch 或 If-Else，而是试试这个 | Don’t Use Switch or If-Else in JavaScript, Instead, Try This
item.description:
而不是用对象在 JavaScript 中编写更好的 switch 和 if…else 语句

什么是switch语句？

开关问题

关于 switch 案例的奇怪之处在于，您必须在每个案例的末尾包含关键字 break 以防止控件移动到下一个案例。

除此之外，在映射复杂场景时这是一场灾难，这可能导致调试困难和嵌套错误。

每次我们编写 switch 语句时，都必须格外小心。

当您必须格外小心时，编写代码的乐趣就会减少，这会导致巨大的问题。

使用对象

让我们设置一个 match 函数来模拟 switch case

你可以这样做：

对象可以用作 switch 语句的替代。

使用 Object one 可以实现键控方法查找。

加起来

这并不是说 switch 语句没有用，或者使用对象字面量始终是最好的选择。 另一方面，作为开关的对象文字应该成为您的 javascript 工具箱中的另一个工具。

你可以在这里找到我们在这篇文章中实现的完整代码。
就是这样

我希望你喜欢它！

在评论部分留下任何问题、疑虑、建议或批评。 这激励我不断改进并在 dev 上写作

回头见！ 阅读更多：

7 JavaScript clean coding tips every developer should know 😎 每个开发人员都应该知道的 7 个 JavaScript 干净编码技巧😎

Top JavaScript VSCode Extensions for Faster Development 🔥 用于更快开发的顶级 JavaScript VSCode 扩展🔥



原文:

Instead of Write better switch and if…else statement in JavaScript with object

What is the switch statement?

The switch statement evaluates an expression, matching the expression’s value to a case clause, and executes statements associated with that case, as well as statements in cases that follow the matching case.

Problems with switch

The switch case is a little difficult to read and a bit ancient, and it’s prone to debugging problems.

The odd thing about the switch case is that you have to include the keyword break at the end of each case to prevent the control from moving to the next.

in addition to that, it’s a disaster when it comes to mapping complex scenarios, which can lead to difficult debugging and nested errors.

Every time we write a switch statement, we must exercise extreme caution.

The joy of writing code is curtailed when you have to be extra cautious and that causes a huge problem.

Using objects

Let’s set up a match function to simulate switch cases

And you can do something like this :

An object can be used as an alternative to the switch statement.

Using Object one can implement a keyed method lookup.

Summing up

This isn’t to say that the switch statement isn’t useful or that using object literals is always the best option. Object literals as switches, on the other hand, should become another tool in your javascript toolbox.

You can find the full code we implemented in this post here.
That’s it

I hope you enjoyed it!

See you later! ❤️

item.title: 从头开始拖放 - 滚动锚定和触摸手势的陷阱 | From scratch Drag and Drop - Pitfalls with Scroll Anchoring and touch gestures
item.description:
我一直在为我的网络应用程序编写一些从头开始的拖放代码。 我决定不使用 HTML 的内置拖放功能，因为我想完全控制拖放预览，而使用内置 API 似乎无法做到这一点。 我选择了pointer事件，因为它们与触摸和鼠标交互一起工作，并且因为它具有setPointerCapture 。

带有pointer事件的高级方法

  1. 制作一个在鼠标悬停时显示的拖动手柄元素。
  2. 在拖动手柄上的指针向下，调用document.body.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId) pointerdown所有鼠标事件直接发送到文档正文（因为原始元素在移动时可能会分离）。
  3. 在document.body上设置pointermove和pointerup处理程序以处理拖动操作。
  4. 使用一堆布局数学来查找列表中项目将被删除的位置，并交换元素以反映这一点。
  5. 当鼠标靠近滚动窗格的顶部或底部时，自动向上或向下滚动窗格（与鼠标在自动滚动区域内的距离成正比）。
  6. 在pointerup ，提交拖动操作并从document.body中分离所有临时事件处理程序。

神秘的卷轴变化

我得到了它的工作，但是当我注意到自动向上滚动时滚动疯狂地四处折断时，我感到很困惑。 经过一堆搜索和死胡同，我发现如果交换的元素位于滚动区域的顶部，则使用insertBefore()交换元素（这是我的框架实现列表顺序更新的方式）有时会改变滚动位置。

我进行了最少的复制，并注意到 Safari 没有问题。 经过大量无果的搜索后，我将其报告为浏览器错误，但事实证明这只是Scroll Anchoring的一个边缘案例。 他们选择一个锚元素（第一个可见元素），如果该节点移动，他们认为这可能是由于其上方发生了一些恶作剧，然后他们更新滚动值以跟踪该锚节点的移动。 除了在我的情况下，它选择了我在拖动时交换的元素之一作为锚元素并且把事情搞砸了。

幸运的是，有一个简单的解决方法：在列表元素上设置overflow-anchor: none 。 我不需要这种行为，因为列表中没有其他任何东西在改变大小。 这里有一个有趣的观察结果，即使没有触发自动滚动， overflow-anchor: none也可能有用：关闭滚动锚定将绕过锚点选择过程，并使滚动区域内的 DOM 更新更快一些。

抑制触摸手势

最后一个障碍是修复移动设备上的拖动：我需要在拖动过程中临时将touch-action: none应用于列表，这样我的指针操作就不会被自动检测到的触摸手势取消。



原文:

I've been writing some from-scratch drag and drop code for my web app. I decided not to go with the built-in drag/drop functionality of HTML because I wanted to fully control the drop preview, which does not seem to be possible with the built-in APIs. I went with the pointer events since those work with touch and mouse interactions, and because it has setPointerCapture.

High-level approach with pointer events

  1. Make a drag handle element that shows up on mouse hover.
  2. On pointerdown on the drag handle, call document.body.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId) to send all mouse events directly to document body (since the original element might get detached as it gets moved around).
  3. Set up pointermove and pointerup handlers on document.body to handle drag operations.
  4. Use a bunch of layout math to find where in the list the item would be dropped, and swap around elements to reflect that.
  5. When the mouse is close to the top or bottom of the scroll pane, automatically scroll the pane up or down (proportional to how far the mouse is inside the autoscroll zone).
  6. On pointerup, commit the drag operation and detach all the temporary event handlers from document.body.

Mysterious scroll changes

I got it working but was flummoxed when I noticed the scroll snapping around crazily when auto-scrolling up. After a bunch of searching and dead ends, I found that swapping elements with insertBefore() (which is how my framework was implementing list order updates) could sometimes change the scroll position if the swapped element was at the top of the scroll area.

I got a minimal repro going and noticed that Safari didn't have the issue. After a lot of fruitless searching I reported it as a browser bug but it turns out this is just an edge case with Scroll Anchoring. 😳

The problem that Scroll Anchoring is trying to solve is elements above the viewport out of view popping in or changing size and causing the view to jump around. They choose an anchor element (the first visible element) and if that node moves, they figure that probably means it was due to some shenanigans happening above it, then they update the scroll value to track that anchor node's movement. Except in my case it had chosen one of the elements that I was swapping around while dragging as an anchor element and was messing things up.

Fortunately, there's an easy fix: set overflow-anchor: none on the list element. I didn't need the behavior because nothing else in the list was changing size. An interesting observation here is that overflow-anchor: none could be useful even when the auto-scroll isn't triggering: turning off scroll anchoring will bypass the anchor selection process and make DOM updates inside the scroll area a little bit faster.

Suppressing touch gestures

The last hurdle was fixing up the drag on mobile: I needed to temporarily apply touch-action: none to the list during the drag so my pointer operation wouldn't get canceled by an automatically detected touch gesture.
