item.title: 使用 .NET Core 中的资源文件执行全球化和本地化 | Performing Globalization and Localization using resource file in .NET Core
item.description:
在这篇博客中，我们将讨论 .net core 的全球化和本地化。 如果你想让你的 web 应用程序可以用于各种不同的文化，那么你需要全球化。 使用全球化和本地化，我们可以构建这样一个网站或应用程序，以便全球任何地方的用户都可以使用它。 全球化支持不同的文化。 本地化提供本地化的资源和文化。

什么是资源文件？

资源文件可能包括一组图标、菜单、对话框、字符串表、用户定义的二进制数据和其他种类的项目。

如果我们正在处理全球化和本地化，那么我们需要使用资源文件。资源文件包含特定于文化的键/值对字符串。 在输出中，代码字符串将替换为特定文化的值。

什么是全球化？

在全球化中，不同的地区会有不同的语言。

什么是本地化？

本地化是根据当地文化制作任何行为的 Web 应用程序的过程。

本地化分为三种类型。

  • 查看本地化
  • 数据注释本地化
  • 控制器字符串中的本地化

在这里，我们将看到视图本地化和数据注释。 为了更好地理解，让我们看看这个例子。

例子： -

使用数据注释本地化

第 1 步： - 创建新项目

首先创建一个新的 asp.net core 项目。

新建 -> 项目 -> asp.net 核心 Web 应用程序 -> MVC。

之后，您的项目结构如下所示。 在上面的快照中，我们只需添加另一个文件夹资源。 我们将在其中放置资源文件。

步骤2：-更新启动文件

我们需要添加这个命名空间：

 using System.Globalization; using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Localization; using Microsoft.Extensions.Options;

在启动文件中添加本地化服务。

 services.AddLocalization(options => options.ResourcesPath = "Resources"); services.AddMvc() .AddViewLocalization(LanguageViewLocationExpanderFormat.Suffix) .AddDataAnnotationsLocalization(options => { options.DataAnnotationLocalizerProvider = (type, factory) => { var assemblyName = newAssemblyName(typeof(ApplicationResource).GetTypeInfo().Assembly.FullName); return factory.Create("ApplicationResource", assemblyName.Name); }; }); ervices.Configure<requestlocalizationoptions>(options => { var givenCultures = new[] { new CultureInfo("en"), new CultureInfo("fr"), new CultureInfo("hi"), }; options.DefaultRequestCulture = new RequestCulture("en"); options.SupportedCultures = givenCultures; options.SupportedUICultures = givenCultures; }); </requestlocalizationoptions>

在添加本地化中，在资源路径中添加我们的资源文件夹名称。 我们已命名资源。

现在配置请求本地化选项。 我们在其中制作了一系列文化或语言。

使用默认请求文化制作您自己的默认语言。

注意：-这里“en”表示英语，“hi”表示印地语，“fr”表示法语。

阅读更多： 在带有接口的 Asp.net Core 中对自定义中间件进行单元测试

第 3 步： - 创建视图模型

创建视图模型并将其命名为“userviewmodel”，如图所示。

 publicclassUserViewModel { [Display(Name = "FullName")] publicstring FullName { get; set; } [Display(Name = "City")] publicstring City { get; set; } [Display(Name = "MobileNo")] publicstring MobileNo { get; set; } }

为所有属性添加数据注释“显示名称”。

并制作一个资源文件，我们必须将这个显示名称放入其中。 我们将在下一步中看到它。

第 4 步： - 创建资源文件

在项目中添加一个新文件夹 Resources。

右键单击资源文件夹并添加 -> 添加新项目 -> 资源文件。

资源文件中视图模型的显示名称和键名称应相同。

第 5 步： - 添加控制器

添加 Culture 控制器以存储文化的值并在视图中显示。

 publicclassCultureController : Controller { public IActionResult Index () { returnView (); } [HttpPost] Public IActionResult SetLanguage(string culture, string returnUrl) { Response.Cookies.Append( CookieRequestCultureProvider.DefaultCookieName, CookieRequestCultureProvider.MakeCookieValue(new RequestCulture(culture)), new CookieOptions {Expires = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddYears(1) } ); return LocalRedirect(returnUrl); } }

现在添加 set language 方法以获取文化的值并将其存储在 cookie 中。 我们将从视图中指定此方法。 现在来看看它。

添加将使用用户视图模型并对其进行查看的用户控制器。

 publicclassUserController : Controller { public IActionResult Index() { return View(); } }

第 6 步： - 创建视图

最初添加将显示文化下拉列表的局部视图，我们可以从中选择语言。 将此视图放在共享文件夹中并放在下面的代码中。

 @using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder @using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Localization @using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Localization @using Microsoft.Extensions.Options @inject IViewLocalizer Localizer @inject IOptions<requestlocalizationoptions> LocOptions @{ var Culture = Context.Features.Get<irequestculturefeature>(); var Items = LocOptions.Value.SupportedUICultures .Select(c => new SelectListItem { Value = c.Name, Text = c.DisplayName }) .ToList(); }<div><form asp-action="SetLanguage" asp-controller="Culture" asp-route-returnurl="@Context.Request.Path" method="post"> Language :- <select asp-for="@requestCulture.RequestCulture.UICulture.Name" asp-items="Items" name="culture" onchange="this.form.submit();"></select></form></div> </irequestculturefeature></requestlocalizationoptions>

添加一些命名空间。 并在布局视图中添加这个局部视图。

 <xmp><ul class="navbar-nav flex-grow-1"><li> </li><li>@await Html.PartialAsync("Languagepartial")</li><li> </li></ul> </xmp>

用户控制器视图

<xmp> @model GlobalandLocal.Models.UserViewModel @using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Localization @inject IViewLocalizer ViewLocalizer @{ ViewData["Title"] = "Index"; }<hr /><div> Back to List</div> </xmp>

输出： -

我们已经看到了使用数据注释的本地化。 现在看看视图本地化。

想聘请ASP.Net Core 开发人员？ 现在联系。

使用视图本地化： -

现在对于视图本地化，我们只需要创建一个资源文件并在视图中注入 IView 本地化器。

现在查看我们之前创建的用户索引方法。 现在创建该索引方法的资源文件，如下所示。

注意： - 名称应该相同，否则不会执行。

在索引视图中，只需在注入器下方添加此代码。

 @model GlobalandLocal.Models.UserViewModel @using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Localization @inject IViewLocalizer ViewLocalizer @{ ViewData["Title"] = "Index"; }

@VIEWLOCALIZER["WELCOME"] @ViewLocalizer["Index"] @VIEWLOCALIZER["WELCOME"] @ViewLocalizer["索引"]

@ViewLocalizer["UserViewModel"] @ViewLocalizer["UserViewModel"]



原文:

In this blog, we will be goingto talk about the globalization and localization in .net core. If you want to make your web application in such a way that can be used for every different culture then you need globalization. Using globalization and localization we can build such a website or application so the user anywhere from global can use it. Globalization supports the different cultures. Localization provide the localized resource and cultures.

What is Resource File?

A resource file may include a troupe of icons, menus, dialog boxes, strings tables, user-defined binary data and other sorts of items.

If we are working with the globalization and localization then we need to use the resource file.Resource file contains key/value pair string which is specific to the culture. In the output, the code string will be replaced with the value of the specific culture.

What is Globalization?

Asp.net Core Globalization is the process of developing any web application or mobile application globalized. In globalization, there will be different languages for various geographies.

What is Localization?

Localization is the process of making any web applications that behaves depending upon the local culture.

There are three types of localization.

  • View localization
  • Data Annotation localization
  • Localization in controller string

Here we will see View localization and data annotation. For perfect understanding let’s see the example.

Example: -

Using Data Annotation localization

Step 1: - Create the new project

First of all make a new asp.net core project.

New -> project -> asp.net core web application -> MVC.

After that your project structure looks like this. In above snap we have to just add another folder resources. In which we will place our resource file.

Step 2: -Update the startup file

We need to add this name spaces:

    using System.Globalization;
    using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Localization;
    using Microsoft.Extensions.Options;

Add localization services in the startup file.

services.AddLocalization(options => options.ResourcesPath = "Resources");
    services.AddMvc()
    .AddViewLocalization(LanguageViewLocationExpanderFormat.Suffix)
    .AddDataAnnotationsLocalization(options =>
    {
        options.DataAnnotationLocalizerProvider = (type, factory) =>
    {
      var assemblyName = newAssemblyName(typeof(ApplicationResource).GetTypeInfo().Assembly.FullName);
      return factory.Create("ApplicationResource", assemblyName.Name);
        };
    });
    ervices.Configure<requestlocalizationoptions>(options =>
    {
    var givenCultures = new[]
        {
      new CultureInfo("en"),
      new CultureInfo("fr"),                    
      new CultureInfo("hi"),                    
        };
        options.DefaultRequestCulture = new RequestCulture("en");
        options.SupportedCultures = givenCultures;
        options.SupportedUICultures = givenCultures;
});
</requestlocalizationoptions>

In add localization, add our resource folder name in resource path. We have named the Resources.

Now configure request localization options. In which we have made the array of cultures or languages.

Make your own default language using default request culture.

Note: -Here “en” means English, “hi” means Hindi, and “fr” means French.

Read More: Unit Testing A Custom Middleware In Asp.net Core With Interface

Step 3: - Create a View model

Create the view model and name it “userviewmodel” as shown.

publicclassUserViewModel
    {
        [Display(Name = "FullName")]
publicstring FullName { get; set; }

        [Display(Name = "City")]
publicstring City { get; set; }

        [Display(Name = "MobileNo")]
publicstring MobileNo { get; set; }
    }

Add data annotation “Display Name” for all properties.

And make a resource file in which we have to put this display name in it. We will see it in next step.

Step 4: - Create Resource file

Add a new folder Resources in the project.

Right click on the Resources folder and Add -> Add new Item -> Resource file.

Display name of view model and key name should be same in resource file.

Step 5: - Add controller

Add Culture controller to store the value of culture and display in view.

publicclassCultureController : Controller
    {
  public IActionResult Index ()
        {
      returnView ();
        }
        [HttpPost]
    Public IActionResult SetLanguage(string culture, string returnUrl)
        {
            Response.Cookies.Append(
            CookieRequestCultureProvider.DefaultCookieName,
            CookieRequestCultureProvider.MakeCookieValue(new RequestCulture(culture)),
      new CookieOptions {Expires = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddYears(1) }
         );
    return LocalRedirect(returnUrl);
        }
   }

Now add set language method to take the value of culture and store it in cookie. We will designate this method from the view. Now make a view of it.

Add user controller that will use the user view model and make view of it.

  publicclassUserController : Controller
    {
  public IActionResult Index()
    {
    return View();
    }
}

Step 6: - Create views

Initially add the partial view which will display the culture dropdown from which we can select the languages. Place this view in the shared folder and place below code.

@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder
@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Localization
@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Localization
@using Microsoft.Extensions.Options

@inject IViewLocalizer Localizer
@inject IOptions<requestlocalizationoptions> LocOptions
@{
    var Culture = Context.Features.Get<irequestculturefeature>();
    var Items = LocOptions.Value.SupportedUICultures
        .Select(c => new SelectListItem { Value = c.Name, Text = c.DisplayName })
        .ToList();
}<div><form asp-action="SetLanguage" asp-controller="Culture" asp-route-returnurl="@Context.Request.Path" method="post">
        Language :-
        <select asp-for="@requestCulture.RequestCulture.UICulture.Name" asp-items="Items" name="culture" onchange="this.form.submit();"></select></form></div>
</irequestculturefeature></requestlocalizationoptions>

Add some namespaces. And add this partial view in the layout view.

<xmp><ul class="navbar-nav flex-grow-1"><li> </li><li>@await 
Html.PartialAsync("Languagepartial")</li><li> </li></ul>
</xmp>

View of User controller

<xmp>
@model GlobalandLocal.Models.UserViewModel
@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Localization
@inject IViewLocalizer ViewLocalizer
@{
    ViewData["Title"] = "Index";
}<hr /><div>
  Back to List</div>
</xmp>

Outputs: -

We have seen the localization using Data annotation. Now have a look at the view localization.

Looking to Hire ASP.Net Core Developer ? Contact Now.

Using View Localization: -

Now for view localization we have to just make a resource file and inject the IView localizer in the view.

Now make a view of user’s index method as we created earlier. Now create the resource file of that index method as shown below.

Note: - Name should be same either it will not be executed.

In index view just add this code below the injector.

@model GlobalandLocal.Models.UserViewModel

@using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Localization
@inject IViewLocalizer ViewLocalizer
@{
    ViewData["Title"] = "Index";
}

@VIEWLOCALIZER["WELCOME"] @ViewLocalizer["Index"]

@ViewLocalizer["UserViewModel"]
item.author: tarungurang

item.title: 软件组织对开发、测试、登台和生产环境的含义 | What Software organisations mean by Development, Testing, Staging, and Production environments
item.description:
当我第一次加入我的第一个软件开发团队时，我不明白为什么我们要把这些环境分开； 对我来说，如果一切正常，我们可以从开发环境转移到生产环境，这似乎是合乎逻辑的，但是理解为什么要设置这些环境很快改变了我的想法。

那么让我们来看看不同的环境、它们的用途，以及为什么使用它们很重要。 一旦您了解了它们的用途，就会清楚为什么我们拥有这些环境。

开发环境

在您的本地 PC 上，您将在此处

  • 使用许多生产力工具执行所有代码更新，这些工具可能会或可能不会与您的代码一起部署到生产环境中。
  • 创建新的提交和分支，以及查看队友的提交和分支的副本。

开发人员使该环境保持最新并包含应用程序的最新版本。 它将通过 API 链接到本地​​数据库和（或）数据源，允许您在不影响真实数据的情况下编写代码。 因为许多员工将在不同机器上的开发环境中工作，所以您很可能负责分支合并。

需要注意的是，您在开发环境中所做的任何事情都不会影响用户在访问应用程序时看到的内容。

测试或 QA（质量保证）环境

该环境使软件测试人员可以访问开发中应用程序的最新版本，从而使他们能够对其进行全面测试。 之后，测试人员被赋予了以下责任

  • 提高错误意识（如果发现）
  • 查看开发人员最近的错误修复。
  • 提交关于共同修订的报告，以便开发人员了解发现的问题以及测试人员提出的特定问题是否已在之前的开发代码中得到解决。

暂存环境

为了模仿生产环境，应用程序的新版本在上线之前在暂存环境中构建、测试和审查。 与生产环境中使用的硬件和服务器相似的硬件和服务器通常在软件生命周期的暂存阶段进行评估。 与开发站点相比，暂存站点用作与开发和生产环境分开的最终产品测试区。

在进行重大更新时，暂存环境可帮助您发现并解决可能出现的任何问题。 所有数据迁移和配置更改也将在这里进行测试。

在这种情况下，您还可以向客户展示事情的运作方式和外观（如果有的话）。 一旦上线，他们将能够看到一切将如何运作，并且他们将能够为您提供所需的任何输入。

生产环境

这是可供最终用户使用的最新版本的应用程序。 除预定更新外，不应更改此版本。

用户使用这个环境，组织在这个环境中赚钱，所以必须不惜一切代价避免这个阶段的任何错误。 这就是为什么您需要先完成其他两个环境中的所有测试。

值得注意的是，大多数小型软件团队（和初创公司）只有三个环境（开发、阶段和生产），而大公司在开发和阶段之间有一个测试或质量保证 (QA) 环境。

如果你觉得这篇文章有用，请点赞和分享，并在下面的评论部分留下你的想法和问题。



原文:

I couldn't understand why we had these environments separated when I first joined my first software development team; it seemed logical to me that if everything worked well, we could move from the Development environment to the Production environment, but understanding why these environments are set in place quickly changed my mind.

So let's take a look at the different environments, their uses, and why it is important to use them. It will become clear why we have these environments once you understand what they are intended for.

Development Environment

On your local PC, This is where you will

  • Carry out all of your code updates using a number of productivity tools that may or may not be deployed to production alongside your code.
  • Create new commits and branches, as well as see a copy of your teammates' commits and branches.

The developer keeps this environment up to date and contains the most recent version of the application. It'll be linked to a local database and (or) a data source via an API, allowing you to write code without affecting the real data. Because numerous employees will be working in the development environment on separate machines, you'll most likely be in charge of branch merging.

It's important to note that nothing you do in the development environment affects what users see when they visit the application.

Test or QA (Quality Assurance) Environment

This environment gives software testers access to the most recent version of the application from development, allowing them to completely test it. Following that, testers are given the responsibility of

  • Raising Bug awareness (if any are found)
  • Checking out developers' most recent bug fixes.
  • Submit a report on a common revision so that developers are aware of the issues discovered and whether specific concerns raised by testers have been addressed in the development code previously.

Staging Environment

To imitate the production environment, fresh versions of the application are constructed, tested, and reviewed in the staging environment before going live. Hardware and servers that are similar to those used in the production environment are commonly evaluated during the staging phase of the software lifecycle. In contrast to the development site, the staging site serves as a final Product Testing zone separate from the development and production environments.

When doing significant updates, the staging environment helps you to discover and resolve any issues that may surface. All data migrations and configuration changes will also be tested here.

This is when you can also show a client how things work and look if you have one. They'll be able to see how everything will operate once it's online, and they'll be able to provide you with any input you require.

Production Environment

This is the most recent version of the application that is available to end-users. Except for scheduled updates, this version should not be changed.

Users use this environment, and organisations make money in this environment, so any errors at this stage must be avoided at all costs. That's why you need to finish all of the tests in the other two environments first.

It's worth noting that most small software teams (and startups) have only three environments (dev, stage, and production), whereas large corporations have a testing or quality assurance (QA) environment between development and staging.

If you found this post useful, please like and share it, and leave your thoughts and questions in the comments section below.

item.title: 2022 年 5 大新技术趋势 | Top 5 New Technology Trends for 2022
item.description:
人工智能 (AI) 和机器学习

  • 机器人过程自动化 (RPA)
  • 网络安全
  • 物联网 (IoT)

    • 当今的技术瞬息万变，促进了更快的变革和进步，从而加快了变革的步伐。 然而，变化的不仅是技术趋势和不断发展的技术。 由于 COVID19 的爆发，今年发生了很大变化，这让IT 专业人员意识到他们在明天的非接触式世界中的工作将有所不同。

    这对你个人意味着什么？ 它涉及跟上最新的技术发展和趋势。

    它还涉及密切关注未来，以确定确保明天的工作搜索所需的能力，也包括学习如何到达那里。 由于全球疫情，全球大部分IT 员工都坐在家里。 如果您想在 2022 年充分利用在家中的时间，以下是您应该了解的 5 大技术趋势。

    1. 人工智能 (AI) 和机器学习

    什么是人工智能？

    人工智能类似于利用计算机研究人类智能的工作，但人工智能不必局限于生物可观察的方式。”

    简单地说，人工智能的目标是让计算机或计算机程序足够聪明，以模仿人类思维的行为。 太棒了对吧？

    知识工程的学习是人工智能研究的一个主要方面。 机器和程序需要关于世界的大量信息才能像人类一样运作和做出反应。

    人工智能赋予机器常识、解决问题和分析推理能力。

    什么是机器学习？

    人工智能和机器学习是当今世界两个常见且经常被误解的词。 人工智能是机器学习的一部分。

    如果过去观察到某种行为模式，您可以预测它是否会再次发生。 换句话说，如果没有先前的情况，则无法进行预测。

    从而评估数据中的模式。

    1. 机器人过程自动化 (RPA)

    软件机器人可以解释屏幕上显示的内容，输入正确的击键，导航系统，定位和提取数据，并像人类一样执行一系列预定义的操作。 另一方面，软件机器人可以比人类更快、更稳定地做到这一点，而无需起身伸展或休息。

    机器人过程自动化的好处

    通过机器人流程自动化，组织变得更加有利可图、敏捷和敏捷。 它还通过从日常工作中消除繁琐的任务来提高员工的满意度、敬业度和生产力。

    它还非常适合使用没有 API、虚拟桌面基础架构 (VDI) 或数据库访问的旧系统自动执行操作。

    网络安全是使用技术、流程和策略来防止对系统、网络、程序、设备和数据的网络攻击。

    其目标是限制网络攻击的风险并保护系统、网络和技术免受未经授权的使用。

    在电信，5G 是全球移动运营商于 2019 年推出的第五代宽带移动网络技术标准，是当今为大多数手机提供连接的 4G 网络。 它将是继任者。 根据 GSM 协会的数据，到 2025 年，5G 网络预计将在全球拥有超过 17 亿用户。

    小区内的所有 5G 无线设备都通过小区内的本地天线通过无线电波连接到互联网和电话网络。

    新网络将拥有更宽的带宽、更快的下载速度，最终将达到每秒 10 吉比特 (Gbps)。 随着带宽的增加，网络越来越多地被用作笔记本电脑和台式计算机的通用 Internet 服务提供商 (ISP)，与现有的 ISP（如有线互联网）竞争并在物联网领域吸引新的应用程序。 是期待。 只有 4G 功能的手机将无法利用支持 5G 的无线设备所需的新网络。

    物联网 (IoT) 不需要互连的计算设备、机械和数字机器、物体、动物或具有唯一标识符 (UID) 的人以及人与人之间的关系。

    能够通过网络发送数据的系统。 人机交互——需要人机交互或人机交互。

    物联网的东西可以是带有心脏监测器植入物的人，带有生物芯片转发器的牲畜，带有内置传感器的汽车，当轮胎压力过低时会提醒驾驶员，或者是其他自然或人工的对象。 它被分配了一个 Internet 协议 (IP) 地址，并且可以通过网络发送数据。

    不同行业的组织越来越多地使用物联网来提高工作效率、更好地了解客户、改善客户服务、改进决策制定并增加业务价值。

    让我们总结一下 下一波数字化变革已经到来，让未来的企业有机会在今天采取行动，为未来做好准备。

    欢迎来到 Metaverse Continuum，这是一系列数字增强的世界、现实和商业模式，准备在未来十年彻底改变生活和商业。

    它适用于业务的各个方面，从消费者到员工以及整个业务； 从真实到虚拟，反之亦然； 从 2D 到 3D； 从云和人工智能到增强现实、区块链、数字双胞胎、尖端技术等等。 作为互联网的下一次演进，元界将成为功能、用例、技术和体验的快速发展的连续体。

    Metaverse Continuum will transform the way businesses interact with their customers, the way they do the work, the products and services they provide, the way they produce and deliver, and the way they manage their organizations. Metaverse Continuum 将改变企业与客户互动的方式、工作方式、提供的产品和服务、生产和交付的方式以及管理组织的方式。



    原文:

    Top 5 New Technology Trends for 2022

    1. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning
    2. Robotic Process Automation (RPA)
    3. Cyber Security
    4. 5G
    5. Internet of Things (IoT)

    Today's technology is changing rapidly, enabling faster change and advancement and thus accelerating the pace of change. However, it is not only technological trends and evolving technologies that are changing; Much has changed this year due to the COVID19 outbreak, making IT professionals realize that their jobs in tomorrow's contactless world won't be the same.

    What does this mean for you personally? It involves keeping abreast of the latest technological developments and trends.

    It also involves keeping an eye on the future to identify the capabilities you will need to secure tomorrow's job search, also like learning how to get there. Due to the global epidemic, the majority of the global IT workforce is sitting at home. And if you want to make the most of your time at home in 2022, here are the top 5 tech trends you should know about.

    1. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning

    WHAT IS ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE?

    Artificial Intelligence is akin to the job of utilizing computers to study human intelligence, but AI does not have to be limited to biologically observable ways."

    Simply say, AI's goal is to make computers or computer programmes clever enough to mimic the behavior of the human mind. It's awesome Right?.

    The Learning of knowledge engineering is a principal aspect of AI research. Machines and programmes require a wealth of information about the world in order to function and react like humans.

    AI needs to have access to attributes, categories, objects, and the relationships between them to perform knowledge engineering . AI empowers machines with common sense, problem-solving, and analytical reasoning abilities.

    WHAT IS MACHINE LEARNING?

    Artificial intelligence and machine learning are two common and often misunderstood words in today's world. Artificial intelligence is a segment of machine learning.

    ML is the study of creating and implementing algorithms that can learn from the experiences of those who have gone before.

    If a pattern of behavior has been observed in the past, you can predict whether it will recur. In other words, if there is no previous case then no prediction can be made.

    ML can be used to solve difficult problems such as credit card fraud detection, self-driving car activation, facial detection and recognition.

    ML uses complex algorithms that run continuously on many datasets. And thus evaluate the patterns in the data.

    1. Robotic Process Automation (RPA)

    RPA is a software engineering that simplifies the creation, deployment, and management of software robots that mimic human movements while dealing with digital systems and software.

    The software robot can interpret what is displayed on the screen, enter the correct keystrokes, navigate the system, locate and extract data, and perform a series of predefined operations , like humans. Software robots, on the other hand, can do this faster and more stably than humans, without having to get up and stretch or take breaks.

    Benefits of Robotic Process Automation

    Organizations become more profitable, agile, and agile through robotic process automation. It also increases employee satisfaction, engagement and productivity by removing tedious tasks from their daily routine.

    RPA is non-intrusive and can be quickly applied to accelerate digital transformation. It's also great for automating operations with legacy systems that don't have APIs, virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI) or database access.

    Cybersecurity is the use of technology, processes, and policies to prevent cyberattacks on systems, networks, programs, devices, and data.

    Its goal is to limit the risk of cyberattacks and to secure systems, networks, and technology against unauthorized use.

    At Telecom, 5G is the 5th generation technology standard for broadband mobile networks launched by mobile operators around the world in 2019, and is a 4G network that provides connectivity to most mobile phones today. It will be the successor. According to the GSM Association, 5G networks are expected to have more than 1.7 billion subscribers worldwide by 2025.

    All 5G wireless devices in the cell are connected to the Internet and the telephone network via radio waves via the local antenna in the cell.

    The new network will have wider bandwidth, enable faster download speeds, and will eventually reach up to 10 gigabits per second (Gbps). Not only is 5G faster than existing networks, but it also has more bandwidth, allowing you to connect more and more different devices, improving the quality of Internet services in crowded areas.

    With increased bandwidth, networks are increasingly being used as a common Internet Service Provider (ISP) for laptops and desktop computers, competing with existing ISPs such as cable Internet and attracting new applications in the Internet of Things space. Is expected. (IoT) and enable machine-to-machine. Mobile phones with only 4G capabilities will not be able to take advantage of the new networks required by 5G-enabled wireless devices.

    The Internet of Things (IoT) does not require interconnected computing devices, mechanical and digital machines, objects, animals, or people with unique identifiers (UIDs), and person-to-person relationships.

    A system with the ability to send data over a network. Human Interaction-Needs human or human-computer interaction.

    IoT stuff can be people with heart monitor implants, livestock with biochip transponders, cars with built-in sensors that alert the driver when tire pressure is low, or other natural or artificial It is an object of. It is assigned an Internet Protocol (IP) address and can send data over the network.

    Increasingly, organizations in different industries are using the IoT to work more efficiently, better understand their customers, improve customer service, improve decision making, and increase business value.

    Lets conclude with The next wave of digital change is here, giving businesses of the future the opportunity to act today to be future-ready.

    Welcome to Metaverse Continuum, a series of digitally enhanced worlds, realities and business models poised to revolutionize life and business over the next decade.

    It applies to all aspects of business, from consumers to employees and throughout the business; from real to virtual and vice versa; from 2D to 3D; and from cloud and artificial intelligence to augmented reality, blockchain, digital twins, cutting-edge technology and more. As the next evolution of the Internet, Metaverse will be a rapidly emerging continuum of capabilities, use cases, technologies, and experiences.

    Metaverse Continuum will transform the way businesses interact with their customers, the way they do the work, the products and services they provide, the way they produce and deliver, and the way they manage their organizations.
item.author: woxro

item.title: 关于通知 | About notifications
item.description:
最近，我遇到了消除所有干扰的建议，即通知：邮件、社交网络、信使、应用程序、短信。

为什么这是必要的？
我相信很多人都遇到过这样的情况：你坐下来工作——你的 Facebook 朋友给你写信——你在午餐时间醒来，发现半天已经结束了。

为避免发生这种情况，您只需进入手机设置并在通知部分禁用所有内容。
信使中没有一条消息不需要即时响应，如果发生紧急情况 - 您将始终能够拨打电话。

您可以在 Maxim Dorofeev 的《绝地技巧》一书中阅读有关此主题的更多信息。 如何抚养你的猴子，清空收件箱并节省思想燃料。



原文:

Recently I have come across the advice to remove all distractions, which are notifications: mail, social networks, messengers, applications, text messages.

Why is this necessary?
I'm sure many have had the situation where you sit down to work - you Facebook friend writes to you - you wake up at lunchtime and realize that half a day is over.

To avoid this happening, you simply need to go into the settings of your phone and in the notification section to disable everything.
Not a single message in the messenger does not require an instant response, and if something urgent happens - you'll always be able to call.

You can read more on this topic in Maxim Dorofeev's book, Jedi techniques. How to raise your monkey, empty the inbox and save thought fuel.

item.title: 诊断不规则的心跳：什么是心电图监测？ | Diagnosing an Irregular Heartbeat: What is ECG Monitoring?
item.description:
在现实世界中，我们人类正忙于创造更美好的未来。 所以，我们忘记了照顾我们的身体健康。 最重要的是照顾我们的健康，而要照顾的主要身体部位之一就是“心脏” 。 心脏是我们身体的支撑系统； 它使我们全身的血液循环，并通过清除废物（二氧化碳）来提供营养和氧气。 如果我们有任何不安，我们应该立即就医，并赶到附近的医院。

当我们去医院时，我们应该告诉医生我们当时的感觉，身体的任何疼痛等的确切情况。为了诊断心脏问题，医生会适当地检查你的身体并询问有关的问题症状和您的病史。 医生可以通过任何设备进行测试，以查看实际的心脏状况并检查不规则的心跳。 因此，他们可以从治疗程序开始。 不规则的心跳会对心血管健康造成严重影响。 一些心跳异常是无害的，但有时它们可​​能非常严重，或者也可能危及生命。 不规则的心跳也被称为心律失常，它会导致严重的问题，如中风、心脏病发作和其他危险的并发症。 有多种方法可以诊断心律失常。

虽然到目前为止我们只讨论心电图（ECG 或 EKG）。

心律不齐的症状是诊断的挑战之一，因为它们经常根据身体活动和其他因素来来去去。 在常规医生预约期间，通过进行测试来查明确切的问题可能很困难。 这就是为什么心电图监测器有助于对心跳异常进行最准确的诊断。

在进行心电图程序时，称为电极的传感器可以检测到心脏的电活动，该心脏连接到胸部区域，有时还连接到手臂。 心电图方法有助于测量每个电相中心跳的时间和持续时间。 识别心血管系统功能的任何异常或异常，这使您的医生可以在一天中使用心电图设备跟踪您。 该设备还将有助于找到可能导致该问题的特定行为因素。



原文:

In the real world, we people are very busy in making a better future ahead. So, we forget to take care of our body's health. The most important thing is to look after our health and one of the main parts of the body to take care of is ‘the Heart’. The heart is the support system of our body; it circulates the blood in our whole body and it provides nutrients and oxygen by removing waste (Carbon dioxide). If we have any sort of uneasiness we should consult a doctor without any delay and rush to the nearby hospital.

While we are visiting the hospital we should tell the exact situation to the doctor about what we are feeling at that time, any kind of pain in the body, etc. To diagnose the heart issue the doctor will properly examine your body and ask questions about the symptoms and your medical history. The doctor could do tests through any device to see the actual heart condition and to check irregular heartbeats. So that, they can begin with the treatment procedure. Irregular heartbeats can cause severe implications for cardiovascular health. Some heartbeat irregularities are harmless but sometimes they can be very serious or they can be life-threatening also. Irregular heartbeats are also known as heart arrhythmias which can cause serious problems such as heart strokes, heart attacks, and other dangerous complications. There are various methods to diagnose heart arrhythmia.

Although as of now we will only discuss the Electrocardiogram (ECG or EKG).

The symptoms of the irregular heartbeat are one of the challenges of diagnosing since they frequently come and go depending on physical activity and other factors. During a regular doctor’s appointment pinpointing the exact issue by performing a test can be difficult. So that is why the ECG monitor helps to get the most accurate possible diagnosis for the heartbeat irregularities.

While doing the ECG procedure the sensor called electrodes can detect the electrical activity of the heart that is attached to the chest area and sometimes to the arms. An ECG method helps to measure the timings and duration of the heartbeat in each electrical phase. To identify any irregularities or abnormalities in the functioning of your cardiovascular system and this allows your doctor to follow you over the course of a day with an ECG device. The device would also help to find the specific behaviors factor which could be contributing to the issue.
item.author: fleekit75

item.title: Docker-compose con Gradle | Docker-compose con Gradle
item.description:
码头工人组成

摇篮

构建.gradle

 plugins { id "java" (1) .... id "com.avast.gradle.docker-compose" version "0.15.2" (2) }

构建.gradle

 dockerCompose { useComposeFiles.add("infra/docker-compose.yml") isRequiredBy(tasks.run) }

构建.gradle

 run.doFirst { dockerCompose.exposeAsEnvironment(run) def mysqlInfo = dockerCompose.servicesInfos.mysql.firstContainer (1) def redisInfo = dockerCompose.servicesInfos.cache.firstContainer (2) def businessInfo = dockerCompose.servicesInfos.business.firstContainer (3) environment "JDBC_URL", "mysql://${mysqlInfo.host}:${mysqlInfo.ports[3306]}/" environment "REDIS_HOST", redisInfo.host environment "REDIS_PORT", redisInfo.ports[6379] environment "SERVICE_API_URL", "http://${businessInfo.host}:${businessInfo.ports[8080]}/api/" }

笔记

Este plugin tiene unas cuantas configuraciones interesantes adicionales que iré investigando Este plugin tiene unas cuantas configuraciones interesantes adicionales que iréinvestigando



原文:

Probablemente este post a los puristas del TDD les resulte una aberración porque lo que deberías es fomentar el uso de los test para guiar el desarrollo de tu aplicación.

En esta idea una de las batallas que tienes que lidiar suele ser el tema de cómo preparar una infraestructura para estos test. Por ejemplo, tu aplicación requiere una base de datos y te toca usar un H2, pero puede ser más complicado y requerir además un Redis, un RabbitMQ etc por lo que terminas usando test-containers (todo un acierto)

Todo eso está muy bien pero a veces el desarrollo no puede, o no quieres, que sea guiado por los test. A veces tienes que montar todo el entorno y tirar de depurador para reproducir un comportamiento extraño, por ejemplo. Y casi siempre esto lo resuelves montando un docker-compose donde configuras toda la infra y/o servicios requeridos por tu aplicación, donde tienes que lidiar con puertos, persistencia , levantar los servicios etc

En este post vamos a ver cómo podemos "integrar" este docker-compose con nuestro build.gradle de tal forma que podamos levantar esos servicios e integrarlos en nuestra app de forma cómoda integrandola en nuestras task. Para ello vamos a usar el plugin com.avast.gradle.docker-compose

Docker-compose

Supongamos que nuestra arquitectura requiere de una base de datos MySQL, un Redis y un servicio propio tuyo (distinto del que estás trabajando). Probablemente nuestro docker-compose de desarrollo sería algo como:

version: "3.5"
services:

  mysql:
    image: mysql:5.6
    ports:
      - 3306
    environment:
      MYSQL_ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD: "yes"

  cache:
    image: redis:6.2-alpine
    restart: always
    ports:
      - 6379
    command: redis-server --save 20 1 --loglevel warning --requirepass tupwd

  business:
    image: pvidasoftware/greatapp
    ports:
      - 8080

Probablmente tu docker-compose tenga una configuracion distinta para los puertos y mapees un puerto del contenedor con un puerto fijo de tu maquina para poder así acceder al servicio. No es mala idea pero al final es un infierno de configuración cuando el proyecto es compartido entre varios miembros y hay que ponerse de acuerdo en qué puertos usamos para qué cosas

Gradle

En primer lugar vamos a incluir el plugin de avast en nuestro build:

build.gradle

plugins {
    id "java" (1)
    ....
    id "com.avast.gradle.docker-compose" version "0.15.2" (2)
}

| 1 | Si es un proyecto java sueles tener este plugin, pero no es necesario para este post |
| 2 | Añadimos el pluign de Avast, la ultima version a día de hoy |

Esto nos va añadir una serie de tasks en el grupo docker como por ejemplo composeUpy composeDown que obviamente sirven para gestionar un docker-compose que le indiquemos

En segundo lugar vamos a indicarle al plugin donde reside nuestro docker-compose

build.gradle

dockerCompose {
    useComposeFiles.add("infra/docker-compose.yml")
    isRequiredBy(tasks.run)
}

En este ejemplo le estamos diciendo que tenemos un docker-compose en el directorio infra y que la tarea que está interesada en controlar el estado del docker-compose es run (tipica tarea para correr el java)

Por último vamos a "enlazar" los servicios del docker con nuestra tarea que ejecuta la aplicación mediante variables de entorno:

build.gradle

run.doFirst {
    dockerCompose.exposeAsEnvironment(run)

    def mysqlInfo = dockerCompose.servicesInfos.mysql.firstContainer (1)
    def redisInfo = dockerCompose.servicesInfos.cache.firstContainer (2)
    def businessInfo = dockerCompose.servicesInfos.business.firstContainer (3)

    environment "JDBC_URL", "mysql://${mysqlInfo.host}:${mysqlInfo.ports[3306]}/"

    environment "REDIS_HOST", redisInfo.host
    environment "REDIS_PORT", redisInfo.ports[6379]

    environment "SERVICE_API_URL", "http://${businessInfo.host}:${businessInfo.ports[8080]}/api/"

}

| 1 | servicesInfos.mysql obtiene info del servicio llamado mysql en el docker-compose |
| 2 | servicesInfos.cache obtiene info del servicio llamado cache en el docker-compose |
| 3 | servicesInfos.business obtiene info del servicio llamado business en el docker-compose |

El plugin se encarga de levantar los servicios y proporcionarnos información sobre los puertos donde está escuchando cada uno, información que usamos para configurar nuestra tarea. En este ejemplo configuramos un jdbc, un host y un puerto y una url a un api que son las variables que se supone requiere nuestra aplicacion para poder ejecutarse correctamente.

Cuando ejecutemos la tarea run, los servicios se levantan, se ejecuta nuestra aplicación y cuando paramos la tarea, el plugin se encarga de parar el docker-compose (alguna vez me ha fallado y me ha tocado pararlos a mano usando la tarea composeDown)

NOTE

Este plugin tiene unas cuantas configuraciones interesantes adicionales que iré investigando
item.author: jagedn

item.title: 建筑商 | Builders
item.description:
笔记

信息

建造者

提示

Si por ejemplo la clase requiere de unos parámetros requeridos es fácil pedirlos en el constructor del Builder y declarar así explicitamente que son necesarios. Si por ejemplo la clase requiere de unos parametros requeridos es fácil pedirlos en el constructor del Builder y declarar así explicitamente que son necesarios。

"The Builder pattern is well suited to class hierarchies" “Builder 模式非常适合类层次结构”

Esta capacidad del patrón Builder no es fácil de ver de primeras (al menos a mí) pero es muy potente Esta capacidad del patrón Builder no es fácil de ver de primeas (al menos a mí) pero es muy potente

Cuando tenemos una jerarquía de objetos tipo clase base "A" y otras clases que extienden de ella, "B" y "C", podemos aplicar el patrón Builder usando genéricos de tal forma que reutilizemos al máximo los builders Cuando tenemos una jerarquía de objetos tipo clase base "A" y otras clases que extienden de ella, "B" y "C", podemos aplicar el patrón Builder usando genéricos de tal forma que reutilizemos al máximo los builders

 public abstract class A{ abstract static class Builder<T extends Builder<T>>{ public Builder(){ } public T prp1(int prp1){ this.prp1 = prp1; return self(); } abstract A build(); protected abstract T self(); } private A(Builder<?> builder){ // copio los atributos de A } } public class B extends A{ public static class Builder extends A.Builder<Builder>{ public Builder(){ } @Override public B build(){ return new B(this) } public T prp2(int prp2){ this.prp2 = prp2; return self(); } @Override protected Builder self(){ return this;} } private B(Builder builder){ // copio los atributos de B } }

El "truco" se encuentra en el Generic de la clase A: Builder<T extends Builder<T> > así como en el método self . El "truco" se encuentra en el Generic de la clase A: Builder<T extends Builder<T> > así como en el método self 。 Este es usado internamente por el builder para permitir al compilador poder concatenar las llamadas Este es usado internamente por el builder para permitir al compilador poder concatenar las llamadas

A a = new B.Builder().prp1(1).prp2(2).build



原文:

En el post anterior vimos cómo usar Factorias y las ventajas que estas pueden aportar sobre los constructores

En este post vamos a ver otra forma de construir objetos, los Builders.

"Consider a builder when faced with many constructor parameters"

Tanto el uso de factorías como el de constructores tienen un problema cuando el número de parámetros para construir una clase empieza a ser elevado

NOTE

Y cúando es elevado? pues cada cual tendrá su opinión pero en mi opinión más de tres parámetros ya es una señal de que esa clase va a necesitar más bien pronto que tarde otro parámetro más y sería buena idea aplicar un Builder

INFO

Cuando, tanto con factorías como constructores, empiezas a crear métodos con parámetros necesarios y luego otros métodos en los que vas añadiendo cada vez un parámetro opcional se llama "telescoping constructor"

class A{
    public static A newInstance(){
        return new A();
    }
    public static A newInstance(String nombre){
        return new A(nombre);
    }
    public static A newInstance(String nombre, int edad){
        return new A(nombre, edad...);
    }
    public static A newInstance(String nombre, int edad, Date nacimiento){
        return new A(nombre, edad...);
    }
    public static A newInstance(String nombre, int edad, Date nacimiento, Ciudad ciudad){
        return new A(nombre, edad...);
    }
    ...
}

Otra forma de inicializar objetos cuando el número de parámetros es numeroso es aplicando la aproximación de JavaBeanscreando métodos get/set para cada propiedad de la instancia. Sin embargo esta forma tiene muchos problemas como por ejemplo la validación de que todos los parámetros han sido proporcionados, es muy "verbose", dificil de seguir y no puede crear objetos inmutables.

Builder

Por todo ello el otro patrón que se aplica es creando un Builder

class A{

    private final int prp1;
    private final int prp2;
    ...
    private final int prpn;

    public int getPrp1() { return prp1;}
    // NO tenemos metodo setPrp1

    public static class Builder{
        private final int prp1;
        private final int prp2;
        ...
        private final int prpn;

        public Builder(){}

        public Builder prp1(int p){ prp1 = p; return this;}
        ...
        public Builder prpn(int p){ prpn = p; return this;}

        public A build(){
            // validar que tenemos todos los parametros necesarios
            return new A(this);
        }
    }
    private A(Builder builder){
        prp1 = builder.prp1;
        ...
        prpn = builder.prpn;
    }
}

y el código para crear una instancia A sería:

A a = new A.Builder().prpn(n).prp2(2).prp1(1).build();
TIP

Si por ejemplo la clase requiere de unos parámetros requeridos es fácil pedirlos en el constructor del Builder y declarar así explicitamente que son necesarios.

"The Builder pattern is well suited to class hierarchies"

Esta capacidad del patrón Builder no es fácil de ver de primeras (al menos a mí) pero es muy potente

Cuando tenemos una jerarquía de objetos tipo clase base "A" y otras clases que extienden de ella, "B" y "C", podemos aplicar el patrón Builder usando genéricos de tal forma que reutilizemos al máximo los builders

public abstract class A{

    abstract static class Builder<T extends Builder<T>>{

        public Builder(){
        }

        public T prp1(int prp1){
            this.prp1 = prp1;
            return self();
        }

        abstract A build();

        protected abstract T self();
    }

    private A(Builder<?> builder){
        // copio los atributos de A
    }

}

public class B extends A{

    public static class Builder extends A.Builder<Builder>{

        public Builder(){
        }

        @Override public B build(){
            return new B(this)
        }

        public T prp2(int prp2){
            this.prp2 = prp2;
            return self();
        }

        @Override protected Builder self(){ return this;}
    }

    private B(Builder builder){
        // copio los atributos de B
    }
}

El "truco" se encuentra en el Generic de la clase A: Builder<T extends Builder<T> > así como en el métodoself. Este es usado internamente por el builder para permitir al compilador poder concatenar las llamadas

A a = new B.Builder().prp1(1).prp2(2).build
item.author: jagedn

item.title: 说比写更快乐 | Speaking is more joyful than writing
item.description:
我记得我高中的时候，我曾经使用 Dragon Neutral Speech 软件写一些报告，我不明白为什么我没有继续这样做。

因为现在我遇到了很多人一样的创意障碍。 这个问题很有名，你知道的，你可能已经受苦了。

你有一个主题要写，即使你有你的帖子的整体结构，你也没有想法，而且这个问题更加严重，因为你进入了一个不允许你工作的循环。 你没有任何想法，盯着显示器的空白让你感到压力，然后你强迫自己写点东西，但又什么都没有想到，当你终于有东西要写时，突然想法的流动停止了，并且然后你再次进入循环。

这种情况让我们看不到真正的问题，那就是我们不习惯表达自己。 让你的想法出现在你身上也是一项必须培养的技能，而强迫我们写作阻碍了我们提高这种技能

这就是为什么我认为语音转文本技术会发挥作用，正如我在介绍段落中所说的那样，我曾经用一个来写一些报告，现在我不再使用它了，我什至不知道它是什么这背后的原因。 但今天我意识到现在我有同样的机会，但现在是在智能手机里。 这促使我再次开始写一些想法，但现在这些想法即使不是来自我的嘴，也不会来自我的手指。

这里的帖子完全由语音转文本技术完成，我还需要训练我的想法口才流利度，而不仅仅是我使用外语的流利度。 这也有助于培养演讲技巧和我对文本的阐述。

所以这更像是一个文本告诉你，如果你不写老式的方式。 不要因为字数打字而困扰自己，因为这会给您的思维过程带来麻烦，并且如果您开始口述自己的想法，也不要为每分钟说话的最大字数感到困扰，除非您已经有了文字在你的脑海中逐字逐句，你不会受益于语音给你的主要速度。



原文:

I remember my high school days when I used to write some reports using Dragon Neutral Speaking software I do not understand why I didn't continue doing that.

Because now I'm having the same creative blockage that many people have. This problem is famous, you know it, and you may have suffered from it.

You have a topic to write about and even though you have the overall structure of your posting you don't have ideas coming to you, and this problem is exacerbated because you enter into a loop that doesn't let you work. No idea comes to you, staring at the white space of your monitor stresses you back, then you force yourself to write something but again nothing comes to mind, and when you finally have something to write then suddenly the flow of the ideas stops, and then you get into the loop again.

This situation blinds us to the real problem and it is that we are not used to expressing ourselves. Making your ideas come to you is also a skill that has to be developed, and forcing us to write is hindering us from improving such skill

This is why I think that speech-to-text technologies come into play as I said in my introduction paragraph, I used to use one to write some reports and now I don't use it anymore I don't even know what is the reason behind this. But today I get the realization that now I have the same opportunity but now inside a smartphone. This pushes me to again start writing some ideas, but now those ideas are not going to come from my fingers if not from my mouth.

This posting here is done fully by speech-to-text technologies I also need to train my idea eloquence fluency, not just my fluency in using a foreign language. This also helps to develop speech skills and my elaboration of texts too.

So this is more a text to tell you that if you aren't writing the old-fashioned way. Do not distress yourself over word count typing because that will be a hassle to your thought process and if you start to dictate your ideas, also do not get distressed on the maximum words per minute that speaking will supposedly give you unless you already have the text word by word in your mind, you won't benefit of the primary speed that speech gives you.
item.author: ulisesac4

item.title: 如何获取 ASP.NET Core 的完整公共 URL | How to get the full public URL of ASP.NET Core
item.description:
在 ASP.NET Core 控制器、Razor 页面和 Razor 视图中，有一个Request属性可让您访问HttpRequest实例。 如果您需要知道 Web 应用程序的完整 URL 是什么，您可以通过抓取Scheme 、 Host 、 PathBase 、 Path和QueryString并像这样组合它们来将 URL 放在一起：

 $" { Request . Scheme } :// { Request . Host }{ Request . PathBase }{ Request . Path }{ Request . QueryString } "

也许您只对没有路径和查询字符串的 URL 感兴趣，在这种情况下，您可以从字符串插值中省略PathBase 、 Path和QueryString参数：

 $" { Request . Scheme } :// { Request . Host } "

如果您想为您的应用程序生成 URL，我建议您使用UrlHelper ，如这篇关于生成绝对 URL 的博客文章中所述。

如果您使用的是 Minimal API，则您无法访问Request属性，但您可以接受HttpRequest的实例作为端点回调的参数，如下所示：

 app . MapGet ( "/hello-world" , ( HttpRequest request ) => { return $" { request . Scheme } :// { request . Host }{ request . PathBase }{ request . Path }{ request . QueryString } " ; } );

如果没有 HTTP 请求怎么办？

如果您在代码中没有处理 HTTP 请求，例如当您使用BackgroundWorker进行一些后台处理时，您将需要获取方案并以其他方式托管。 您可以将所需的方案和主机存储到您的配置中，或者将它们组合在一个属性中并将其存储到您的配置中。 配置后，从配置中提取它并使用它来构建您的绝对 URL。

请注意，根据网络和基础架构配置，可以从多个 URL 请求 Web 应用程序。 因此，即使您可以简单地将完整的公共 URL 存储在配置中，它也可能不是用户使用的唯一 URL。



原文:

URLs are what makes the web the web. In ASP.NET Core Controllers, Razor Pages, and Razor views, there's a Request property which gives you access to the HttpRequest instance. The HttpRequest object gives you access to scheme, host (which is the domain and port), path, query string, body content, and headers of the incoming HTTP request.

If you need to know what the full URL is of your web application, you can put the URL together by grabbing the Scheme, Host, PathBase, Path, and QueryString and combining it like this:

$"{Request.Scheme}://{Request.Host}{Request.PathBase}{Request.Path}{Request.QueryString}"

Maybe you're only interested in the URL without the path and query string, in which case you can omit the PathBase, Path, and QueryString parameter from the string interpolation:

$"{Request.Scheme}://{Request.Host}"

If you want to generate URLs for your application, I suggest you use the UrlHelper as explained in this blog post about generating absolute URLs.

If you're using Minimal APIs, there's no Request property accessible to you, but you can accept an instance of HttpRequest as a parameter to your endpoint callback like this:

app.MapGet("/hello-world", (HttpRequest request) => { return $"{request.Scheme}://{request.Host}{request.PathBase}{request.Path}{request.QueryString}"; }
);

What if there's no HTTP request?

If there is no HTTP request that you're handling in your code, like when you're doing some background processing using a BackgroundWorker, you'll need to get the scheme and host some other way. You could store your desired scheme and host into your configuration, or combine them together in a single property and store it into your configuration. Once configured, extract it from configuration and use it to build your absolute URL.

Be mindful of the fact that web application can be requested from multiple URLs depending on the network and infrastructure configuration. So even though you could simply store the full public URL in configuration, that may not be the only URL that users use.
item.author: swimburger

item.title: Como evitar SQL 注入没有 MongoDB | Como evitar SQL Injection no MongoDB
item.description:
回购示例：

https://github.com/williamkoller/my-readings https://github.com/williamkoller/my-readings

Espero ter te ajudado de alguma maneira, pode comentar e compartilhar. Espero ter te ajudado de alguma maneira, pode comentar e compartilhar。



原文:

O que é SQL Injection?

SQL Injection é um ataque no qual código malicioso é passado para um servidor SQL, que o executa. O ataque pode resultar em acesso não autorizado a dados confidenciais, ou destruição de dados críticos.

Como saber se uma query não previne SQL Injection?!

Neste exemplo estou usando CodeQL, o que é CodeQL?!

  • É um mecanismo de análise de código que descobre vulnerabilidades.

Nesse exemplo na aba de security do github:

O code sacanner nos avisa, que se uma consulta de banco de dados (como uma consulta SQL ou NoSQL) for criada a partir de dados fornecidos pelo usuário sem higienização suficiente, um usuário mal-intencionado poderá executar consultas de banco de dados mal-intencionadas.

Como recomendação para queries NoSQL, podemos usar um operador como $eq do MongoDB

Iremos fazer um refactor no UsersRepository para evitar SQL Injection:

Depois de rodar o Github Actions, notamos que não tem mais alerta na aba de security no github:

E assim não temos mais alerta de SQL Injection;

Repo de exemplo:

https://github.com/williamkoller/my-readings

Espero ter te ajudado de alguma maneira, pode comentar e compartilhar.
item.author: williamkoller

item.title: 5 个有用的数据库命令行工具 | 5 Useful Database Command Line Tools
item.description:
托尼·拉里

发表于具有自动完成和语法突出显示的 MySQL 终端客户端。

特征

  • 在您键入 SQL 关键字以及数据库中的表、视图和列时自动完成。
  • 使用 Pygments 突出显示语法。
  • 智能完成（默认启用）将建议上下文相关的完成。
    • 支持多行查询。
    • 带有可选位置参数的收藏查询。 使用\fs alias query保存查询，并在需要时使用\f alias执行它。
    • 首次启动时会在~/.myclirc中自动创建配置文件。
    • 将每个查询及其结果记录到文件中（默认情况下禁用）。
    • 漂亮的打印表格数据（带颜色！）
    • 支持 SSL 连接

    用法

 $ mycli --help Usage: mycli [OPTIONS] [DATABASE] A MySQL terminal client with auto-completion and syntax highlighting. Examples: - mycli my_database - mycli -u my_user -h my_host.com my_database - mycli mysql://my_user@my_host.com:3306/my_database Options: -h, --host TEXT Host address of the database. -P, --port INTEGER Port number to use for connection. Honors $MYSQL_TCP_PORT. -u, --user TEXT User name to connect to the database. -S, --socket TEXT The socket file to use for connection. -p, --password TEXT Password to connect to the database. --pass TEXT Password to connect to the database. --ssh-user TEXT User name to connect to ssh server. --ssh-host TEXT Host name to connect to ssh server. --ssh-port INTEGER Port to connect to ssh server. --ssh-password TEXT Password to connect to ssh server. --ssh-key-filename TEXT Private key filename (identify file) for the ssh connection. --ssh-config-path TEXT Path to ssh configuration. --ssh-config-host TEXT Host to connect to ssh server reading from ssh configuration. --ssl-ca PATH CA file in PEM format. --ssl-capath TEXT CA directory. --ssl-cert PATH X509 cert in PEM format. --ssl-key PATH X509 key in PEM format. --ssl-cipher TEXT SSL cipher to use. --ssl-verify-server-cert Verify server's "Common Name" in its cert against hostname used when connecting. This option is disabled by default. -V, --version Output mycli's version. -v, --verbose Verbose output. -D, --database TEXT Database to use. -d, --dsn TEXT Use DSN configured into the [alias_dsn] section of myclirc file. --list-dsn list of DSN configured into the [alias_dsn] section of myclirc file. --list-ssh-config list ssh configurations in the ssh config (requires paramiko). -R, --prompt TEXT Prompt format (Default: "\t \u@\h:\d> "). -l, --logfile FILENAME Log every query and its results to a file. --defaults-group-suffix TEXT Read MySQL config groups with the specified suffix. --defaults-file PATH Only read MySQL options from the given file. --myclirc PATH Location of myclirc file. --auto-vertical-output Automatically switch to vertical output mode if the result is wider than the terminal width. -t, --table Display batch output in table format. --csv Display batch output in CSV format. --warn / --no-warn Warn before running a destructive query. --local-infile BOOLEAN Enable/disable LOAD DATA LOCAL INFILE. -g, --login-path TEXT Read this path from the login file. -e, --execute TEXT Execute command and quit. --init-command TEXT SQL statement to execute after connecting. --charset TEXT Character set for MySQL session. --password-file PATH File or FIFO path containing the password to connect to the db if not specified otherwise --help Show this message and exit.

具有自动完成和语法突出显示的 Postgres CLI

特征

  • 在您键入 SQL 关键字以及数据库中的表和列时自动完成。

  • 使用 Pygments 突出显示语法。

  • 智能完成（默认启用）将建议上下文相关的完成。

    • 对psql反斜杠命令的原始支持。

    • 或者

      用于通过 JDBC 向关系数据库发出 SQL 的 Shell 如果您安装了Coursier ，您可以使用以下命令快速连接到演示 Hypersonic 数据库：

       $ coursier launch sqlline:sqlline:1.12.0 org.hsqldb:hsqldb:2.5.0 net.hydromatic:foodmart-data-hsqldb:0.4 -M sqlline.SqlLine -- -u jdbc:hsqldb:res:foodmart -n FOODMART -p FOODMART -d org.hsqldb.jdbcDriver 0: jdbc:hsqldb:res:foodmart> select avg("shelf_height" * "shelf_width" * "shelf_depth") as "avg_volume" from "product"; +-------------------------+ | avg_volume | +-------------------------+ | 2147.3845245442353 | +-------------------------+ 1 row selected (0.01 seconds) 0: jdbc:hsqldb:res:foodmart> !quit

      交互式 Redis：具有自动完成和语法突出显示的 Redis 终端客户端。

      特征

      • 高级代码完成。 如果您运行命令KEYS然后运行DEL ，IRedis 将根据KEYS结果自动完成您的命令。
      • 命令验证。 例如尝试CLUSTER MEET IP PORT ，IRedis 将为您验证 IP 和 PORT。
      • 命令高亮，完全基于redis语法。 人性化的结果显示。
      • 管道功能，你可以使用你喜欢的shell工具来解析redis的响应，比如get json | jq . get json | jq . 支持长输出的寻呼机。
      • 支持通过 URL 连接， iredis --url redis://example.com:6379/1 。
      • 支持集群，IRedis 会在集群模式下自动重发命令进行MOVED响应。
      • 存储服务器配置： iredis -d prod-redis （更多信息请参见dsn ）。
      • 您无需调用type命令然后键入另一个命令来获取值。 使用 Ctrl + D 发送 EOF 以退出 IRedis。
      • 自动建议。 支持--encode=utf-8 ，解码 Redis 的字节响应。
      • 底部的命令提示，包括命令语法、支持的 redis 版本和时间复杂度。
      • 带有内置HELP命令的官方文档，试试HELP SET ！
      • 用纯 Python 编写，但 IRedis 使用PyOxidizer打包成单个二进制文件，您可以使用 cURL 下载和运行，它也可以正常工作，即使您没有 Python 解释器。
      • 隐藏AUTH命令的密码。
      • 说“再见！” 当你退出时给你！
      • 有关完整功能，请参阅： iredis.io

      或者您可以使用 cURL（或 wget）下载可执行二进制文件，解压缩，然后运行。 当您没有 python 解释器时它特别有用（例如，没有安装 Python 的官方 Redis docker 映像。）：

       wget https://github.com/laixintao/iredis/releases/latest/download/iredis.tar.gz \ && tar -xzf iredis.tar.gz \ && ./iredis

      如果在尝试运行usql时遇到以下或类似错误：

      中止陷阱：6

      查找更多命令行工具： https ://cliapps.com



原文:

Tony Larry

Posted on Apr 26

https://github.com/dbcli/mycli

10.3k

A Terminal Client for MySQL with AutoCompletion and Syntax Highlighting.

Features

  • Auto-completion as you type for SQL keywords as well as tables, views and columns in the database.
  • Syntax highlighting using Pygments.
  • Smart-completion (enabled by default) will suggest context-sensitive completion.
    • SELECT * FROM <tab> will only show table names.
    • SELECT * FROM users WHERE <tab> will only show column names.
  • Support for multiline queries.
  • Favorite queries with optional positional parameters. Save a query using \fs alias query and execute it with \f alias whenever you need.
  • Timing of sql statements and table rendering.
  • Config file is automatically created at ~/.myclirc at first launch.
  • Log every query and its results to a file (disabled by default).
  • Pretty prints tabular data (with colors!)
  • Support for SSL connections

Usage

$ mycli --help
Usage: mycli [OPTIONS] [DATABASE]

  A MySQL terminal client with auto-completion and syntax highlighting.

  Examples:
    - mycli my_database
    - mycli -u my_user -h my_host.com my_database
    - mycli mysql://my_user@my_host.com:3306/my_database

Options:
  -h, --host TEXT               Host address of the database.
  -P, --port INTEGER            Port number to use for connection. Honors
                                $MYSQL_TCP_PORT.

  -u, --user TEXT               User name to connect to the database.
  -S, --socket TEXT             The socket file to use for connection.
  -p, --password TEXT           Password to connect to the database.
  --pass TEXT                   Password to connect to the database.
  --ssh-user TEXT               User name to connect to ssh server.
  --ssh-host TEXT               Host name to connect to ssh server.
  --ssh-port INTEGER            Port to connect to ssh server.
  --ssh-password TEXT           Password to connect to ssh server.
  --ssh-key-filename TEXT       Private key filename (identify file) for the
                                ssh connection.

  --ssh-config-path TEXT        Path to ssh configuration.
  --ssh-config-host TEXT        Host to connect to ssh server reading from ssh
                                configuration.

  --ssl-ca PATH                 CA file in PEM format.
  --ssl-capath TEXT             CA directory.
  --ssl-cert PATH               X509 cert in PEM format.
  --ssl-key PATH                X509 key in PEM format.
  --ssl-cipher TEXT             SSL cipher to use.
  --ssl-verify-server-cert      Verify server's "Common Name" in its cert
                                against hostname used when connecting. This
                                option is disabled by default.

  -V, --version                 Output mycli's version.
  -v, --verbose                 Verbose output.
  -D, --database TEXT           Database to use.
  -d, --dsn TEXT                Use DSN configured into the [alias_dsn]
                                section of myclirc file.

  --list-dsn                    list of DSN configured into the [alias_dsn]
                                section of myclirc file.

  --list-ssh-config             list ssh configurations in the ssh config
                                (requires paramiko).

  -R, --prompt TEXT             Prompt format (Default: "\t \u@\h:\d> ").
  -l, --logfile FILENAME        Log every query and its results to a file.
  --defaults-group-suffix TEXT  Read MySQL config groups with the specified
                                suffix.

  --defaults-file PATH          Only read MySQL options from the given file.
  --myclirc PATH                Location of myclirc file.
  --auto-vertical-output        Automatically switch to vertical output mode
                                if the result is wider than the terminal
                                width.

  -t, --table                   Display batch output in table format.
  --csv                         Display batch output in CSV format.
  --warn / --no-warn            Warn before running a destructive query.
  --local-infile BOOLEAN        Enable/disable LOAD DATA LOCAL INFILE.
  -g, --login-path TEXT         Read this path from the login file.
  -e, --execute TEXT            Execute command and quit.
  --init-command TEXT           SQL statement to execute after connecting.
  --charset TEXT                Character set for MySQL session.
  --password-file PATH          File or FIFO path containing the password
                                to connect to the db if not specified otherwise
  --help                        Show this message and exit.

Pgcli - Python

https://github.com/dbcli/pgcli

10.3k

Postgres CLI with autocompletion and syntax highlighting

Features

  • Auto-completes as you type for SQL keywords as well as tables and columns in the database.

  • Syntax highlighting using Pygments.

  • Smart-completion (enabled by default) will suggest context-sensitive completion.

    • SELECT * FROM <tab> will only show table names.
    • SELECT * FROM users WHERE <tab> will only show column names.

  • Primitive support for psql back-slash commands.

  • Pretty prints tabular data.

$ pip install -U pgcli

or

$ sudo apt-get install pgcli # Only on Debian based Linux (e.g. Ubuntu, Mint, etc)
$ brew install pgcli # Only on macOS

Sqlline - Java

https://github.com/julianhyde/sqlline

502

Shell for issuing SQL to relational databases via JDBCIf you have Coursier installed, you can quickly connect to a demo Hypersonic database with:

$ coursier launch sqlline:sqlline:1.12.0 org.hsqldb:hsqldb:2.5.0 net.hydromatic:foodmart-data-hsqldb:0.4 -M sqlline.SqlLine -- -u jdbc:hsqldb:res:foodmart -n FOODMART -p FOODMART -d org.hsqldb.jdbcDriver
0: jdbc:hsqldb:res:foodmart> select avg("shelf_height" * "shelf_width" * "shelf_depth") as "avg_volume" from "product";
+-------------------------+
|       avg_volume        |
+-------------------------+
| 2147.3845245442353      |
+-------------------------+
1 row selected (0.01 seconds)
0: jdbc:hsqldb:res:foodmart> !quit

Iredis - Python

https://github.com/laixintao/iredis

1.8k

Interactive Redis: A Terminal Client for Redis with AutoCompletion and Syntax Highlighting.

Features

  • Advanced code completion. If you run command KEYS then run DEL, IRedis will auto-complete your command based on KEYS result.
  • Command validation. IRedis will validate command while you are typing, and highlight errors. E.g. try CLUSTER MEET IP PORT, IRedis will validate IP and PORT for you.
  • Command highlighting, fully based on redis grammar. Any valid command in IRedis shell is a valid redis command.
  • Human-friendly result display.
  • pipeline feature, you can use your favorite shell tools to parse redis' response, like get json | jq ..
  • Support pager for long output.
  • Support connection via URL, iredis --url redis://example.com:6379/1.
  • Support cluster, IRedis will auto reissue command for MOVED response in cluster mode.
  • Store server configuration: iredis -d prod-redis (see dsn for more).
  • peek command to check the key's type then automatically call get/lrange/sscan, etc, depending on types. You don't need to call the type command then type another command to get the value. peek will also display the key's length and memory usage.
  • Ctrl + C to cancel the current typed command, this won't exit IRedis, exactly like bash behaviour. Use Ctrl + D to send a EOF to exit IRedis.
  • Ctrl + R to open reverse-i-search to search through your command history.
  • Auto suggestions. (Like fish shell.)
  • Support --encode=utf-8, to decode Redis' bytes responses.
  • Command hint on bottom, include command syntax, supported redis version, and time complexity.
  • Official docs with built-in HELP command, try HELP SET!
  • Written in pure Python, but IRedis was packaged into a single binary with PyOxidizer, you can use cURL to download and run, it just works, even you don't have a Python interpreter.
  • Hide password for AUTH command.
  • Says "Goodbye!" to you when you exit!
  • For full features, please see: iredis.io

Or you can download the executable binary with cURL(or wget), untar, then run. It is especially useful when you don't have a python interpreter(E.g. the official Redis docker image which doesn't have Python installed.):

wget  https://github.com/laixintao/iredis/releases/latest/download/iredis.tar.gz \
 && tar -xzf iredis.tar.gz \
 && ./iredis

Usql - Go

https://github.com/xo/usql

7.2k

Universal command-line interface for SQL databases

If the following or similar error is encountered when attempting to run usql:

$ usql dyld: Library not loaded: /usr/local/opt/icu4c/lib/libicuuc.68.dylib Referenced from: /Users/user/.local/bin/usql Reason: image not found

Abort trap: 6

Find more command line tools: https://cliapps.com
item.author: devfan

item.title: 如果“太忙”错误地变得正常怎么办？ | What If "Too Busy" Is Wrongfully Becoming Normal?
item.description:
蒂娜黄

发表于你几乎见不到你的家人

  • 感觉一切都很重要
  • 你不断尝试满足他人的期望
  • 你不在场
  • 非必需品接管
  • 你觉得自己在生活的多个方面都失败了
  • 你从不休假
  • 你很难集中注意力超过 15 分钟
  • 你不开心不知道为什么
  • 你在做决定时犹豫不决
  • 当你需要帮助时，你不会寻求帮助
  • 你的工作区很乱
  • 你一直在尝试同时处理多项任务
  • 你必须提前几周安排事情
  • 你害怕早上起床
  • 你很难享受当下
  • 你感觉失去平衡
  • 你很多时候感到内疚
  • 你在日历上填满了你甚至不想做的事情
  • 你的思绪总是乱七八糟
  • 你在自动驾驶
  • 你没有维护你的人际关系
  • 你变得比平常更健忘
  • 你醒来很累和/或失眠
  • 你不记得不忙是什么感觉

    • 忙碌时有一个悖论。 它是这样的：

    任何有职业抱负的人都会努力完成出色的工作并因其才能而获得认可。 然后这个人“需求量很大”。 但是，您的需求越多，由于您拥有的工作量，就越难提供高质量的工作。 这就是为什么很容易将简单的忙碌误认为是在证明你做得很好。

    对假期信件的分析表明，自 1960 年代以来，提及“疯狂的日程安排”的情况急剧增加。 忙碌和缺乏休闲或假期不断在媒体上越来越“庆祝”。 广告过去常常以富人在游泳池或游艇上放松为特色，但不是以忙碌的商务女性或男性长时间工作而休闲时间非常有限。

    一般来说，忙碌的人被认为是“高地位”。 反过来，我们越是相信一个人的成功机会完全基于努力工作以及一个人的奉献精神和努力，我们就越倾向于认为那些不去休闲和 24/7 工作的人反而更有可能成为成功的。

    不断忙碌的方式会影响您的健康

    情绪健康：

    • 焦虑的
    • 强调
    • 不知所措
    • 不足
    • 伤心
    • 沮丧的
    • 生气的
    • 孤独
    • 绝望
    • 无能
    • 有罪的

    身体健康：

    • 肌肉紧张和/或疼痛
    • 躁动不安
    • 失眠
    • 头痛
    • 炎
    • 免疫功能受损
    • 疲劳
    • 性欲改变
    • 消化问题
    • 心血管疾病

    停止太忙的方法

    1. 练习正念作为与自己联系的一种方式，在没有判断的情况下观察你的想法，并让自己立足。
    2. 优先考虑与亲人共度时光。
    3. 请记住，改变从你开始。 您必须自己采取行动。
    4. 与他人划清界限，并提醒自己说“不”是可以的。 从长远来看，牺牲自己的幸福来过度扩张自己可能会产生严重的后果。
    5. 提醒自己，你的自我价值并非来自高效或忙碌。
    6. 定义你的优先事项。
    7. 设置提醒，提醒您呼吸并为自己腾出时间
    8. 整理你的日程安排。
    9. 从小事做起，在你的日子里引入平静的时刻。
    10. 为自己设定适当的期望，当涉及到你可以舒适地承担多少，并且知道这可能会随着时间的推移而改变。
    11. 分块安排您的工作时间和空闲时间。
    12. 专注于重要的事情。
    13. 质量优先于数量
    14. 强调实现，而不是做
    15. 在工作日设置参数



    原文:

    Tina Huynh

    Posted on Apr 26

    1. You hardly see your family
    2. Everything feels important
    3. You constantly try to meet others' expectations
    4. You aren't present
    5. The non-essentials take over
    6. You feel like you're failing in multiple areas of your life
    7. You never take vacations
    8. You have a hard time focusing for more than 15 minutes
    9. You're unhappy and don't know why
    10. You're hesitant when it comes to making decisions
    11. You don't ask for help when you need it
    12. Your workspace is messy
    13. You're constantly trying to multitask
    14. You have to schedule things weeks in advance
    15. You dread getting up in the morning
    16. You have a hard time simply enjoying the moment
    17. You feel out of balance
    18. You feel guilty a lot of the time
    19. You're filling your calendar with things you don't even want to do
    20. Your thoughts are in constant disarray
    21. You're on auto-pilot
    22. You aren't maintaining your relationships
    23. You're getting more forgetful than normal
    24. You wake up tired and/or are getting insomnia
    25. You can't remember what it feels like to not be busy

    There's a paradox when it comes to busyness. It goes like this:

    Anyone with professional ambition strives to do great work and be recognized for their talent. This person is then in "high demand". BUT the more in demand you are, the harder it is to provide quality work due to the quantity of work you have. This is why it's easy to mistake simple busyness for validation that you're doing a good job.

    An analysis of holiday letters indicates that references to "crazy schedules" have dramatically increased since the 1960s. Busyness and the lack of leisure or vacation is constantly being more and more "celebrated" all over the media. Advertising used to feature wealthy people relaxing by the pool or on a yacht but not they feature busy business women or men working long hours with very limited leisure time.

    Generally, the busy person is perceived as "high status". In turn the more we believe that one's opportunity for success is solely based on hard work and one's dedication and efforts alone, the more we tend to lean towards the idea that people who skip leisure and work 24/7 instead have a higher chance of being successful.

    Ways Being Constantly Busy is Affecting Your Well Being

    Emotional health:

    • anxious
    • stressed
    • overwhelmed
    • inadequate
    • sad
    • frustrated
    • angry
    • lonely
    • hopeless
    • incompetent
    • guilty

    Physical health:

    • muscle tension and/or pain
    • restlessness
    • insomnia
    • headaches
    • inflammation
    • compromised immune function
    • fatigue
    • change in sex drive
    • digestion issues
    • cardiovascular disease

    Ways to Stop Being Too Busy

    1. Practice mindfulness as a way to connect with yourself, observe your thoughts without judgment, and ground yourself.
    2. Make it a priority to spend time with your loved ones.
    3. Remember that change starts with you. You have to take action in your own hands.
    4. Set boundaries with others and remind yourself that it's okay to say "no". Sacrificing your well-being to overextend yourself can have severe consequences in the long term.
    5. Remind yourself that your self-worth does not come from being productive or busy.
    6. Define your priorities.
    7. Set a reminder that reminds you to breathe and take time for yourself
    8. Declutter your schedule.
    9. Start small and introduce moments of calm into your days.
    10. Set appropriate expectations for yourself when it comes to how much you can comfortably take on and know that this may change over time.
    11. Schedule your work time and your free time in chunks.
    12. Focus on what is essential.
    13. Prioritize quality over quantity
    14. Emphasize achieving, not doing
    15. Set parameters around your workday
item.author: tmchuynh

item.title: 如何为开源项目做贡献 - 你的第一个 GitHub PR | How to Contribute to Open Source Project - Your First GitHub PR
item.description:
如果您是技术初学者/非技术人员，并且想知道如何为开源项目做出贡献，那么这篇文章适合您。 我将以Bytebase的开源文档为例，指导您完成整个过程。

它是用于数据库模式生命周期管理的 GitLab。 它从 v1.0.2 开始开源其官方文档。

让我们开始吧; 确保您安装了Git并拥有一个GitHub帐户。 如果您对 git 有任何疑问，请咨询 google 或https://git-scm.com/docs 。

准备

  • 访问https://github.com/bytebase/bytebase.com ； 可以看到，其实 Bytebase 开源了整个网站，但是我们只关注 docs/.

  • 在您的项目文件夹下，执行 git clone 以下载整个存储库。

  • 是的，现在我们有了代码，这里是最流行的编辑器 code.visualstudio.com

  • 从 VSCode 打开文件夹

删除文件

执行删除

  • 我们的第一个示例是删除一个页面 - Bytebase 取消了v1.0.0中的一项功能，但相关文件仍然存在。

  • 在编辑器中，它非常简单——删除这个文件。

提交和推送

  • 删除后，我们用终端查看当前状态——文件被删除了，对，没错！

  • 更改已提交，但这还不够； 我们需要将其同步到远程分支，以便其他人可以看到。 而且......我们遇到了这个！

  • 这是因为我们现在在主分支。 如果我们构建自己的代码仓库，直接推送到主分支就可以了。 但是现在我们正在尝试对其他人的项目进行更改。 让我们在此处获取文档指南。 创建公关
    • 首先，我们需要创建一个新分支来将我们的本地副本推送到远程。 如果你和我一样——对 git 命令不太熟悉，google 随时为你服务。

    • 我们使用命令“git checkout -b xxx”来创建我们的分支

    • 设置上游并推送到远程“git push --set-upstream origin remove-sql-rollback-page”。

    • 让我们回到 GitHub； 单击“拉取请求”选项卡

    • 我们可以看到推送，但是让我们点击“New pull request”

    • 在这里我们可以看到我们的分支，点击！

    在这里，您可以进行比较，但对于我们的案例来说，这是纯粹的删除。 点击“创建拉取请求”！

    更新文件

    进行更新

    • 我们的第二种情况是更新。 当我们从谷歌文档中复制图片时，它会有自己生成的链接。 我们需要做的是用同一存储库中的 static/doc-assets/xxxx.png 替换它。

    • 首先，我下载所有相关图像并将它们移动到 static/docs-assets/

    • 文件名应加前缀以保持在一起

    • 然后更新相应的文件。 我将旧文件保留在那里以确保新图像看起来相同。

    • 执行 git status 查看状态。

    本地预览

    • 我们运行“pnpm i & pnpm dev”，它编译成功。 现在我们可以访问http://localhost:3000并预览整个站点。

    新公关

    • 对我来说一切都很好，现在让我们创建另一个分支，然后创建一个新 PR

    变更请求

    • 然后，我收到了我的第一个“更改请求”。

    • 是的！ 我忘了推送所有的图像......还有另一个提交和推送。

    • 删除字母“dddd”。

    • 然后，我需要再次向他们请求评论。

    LGTM and Merge LGTM 和合并



原文:

If you are a tech beginner / non-tech person and wonder how to contribute to an open-source project, this is THE article for you. I'll take Bytebase's open-sourced documentation as an example to guide you through the whole process.

Bytebase is an open-source database tool for DBAs and developers; it's the GitLab for database schema lifecycle management. It open-sourced its official documentation since v1.0.2.

Let's get started; make sure you install Git and have a GitHub account. If you have any questions about git, ask google or https://git-scm.com/docs.

Preparation

  • Visit https://github.com/bytebase/bytebase.com; as we can see, actually Bytebase open-sourced the whole website, but we only pay attention to docs/.

  • Under your project folder, do git clone to download the whole repository.

  • Yes, now we have the code, and here is the most popular editor code.visualstudio.com

  • Open the folder from VSCode

Delete a file

Do the Delete

  • Our first example is to remove a page - Bytebase sunset a feature in v1.0.0, but the related file is still there.

  • Within the editor, it's super straightforward - delete this file.

Commit and Push

  • After deletion, we use Terminal to view the current status - the file is deleted, yes, that's right!

  • Git commit with the message telling what this commit is for.

  • The changes are committed, and that's not enough; we need to synchronize it to the remote branch so that others can see. And ... We encountered this!

  • It's because we are now in the main branch. If we build our own code repository, it's OK to push directly to the main branch. But now we are trying to make changes to others' project. Let's get to the document guidance here. https://bytebase.com/docs/document-write-guide

Create a PR

  • "Create a new PR" - how? First, we need to create a new branch to push our local copy to remote. If you are similar to me - not so familiar with the git command, google is always there for you.

  • We use the command "git checkout -b xxx" to create our branch

  • Set the upstream and push to remote "git push --set-upstream origin remove-sql-rollback-page".

  • Let's get back to GitHub; click the "Pull requests" tab

  • We can see the push, but let's click "New pull request"

  • Here we can see our branch, click!

Here, you can make the comparison, but it's pure deletion for our case. Click "Create pull request"!

Update a file

Do the Update

  • Our second case is to update. When we copy images from google docs, it will have its self-generated link. What we need to do is to replace that with static/doc-assets/xxxx.png from the same repository.

  • Firstly, I download all the related images and move these to static/docs-assets/

  • The file names should be prefixed to stay together

  • Then update the corresponding file. I keep the old files there to ensure the new images look the same.

  • Do git status to view the status.

Preview Locally

  • We run "pnpm i & pnpm dev" and it compiles successfully. Now we can visit http://localhost:3000 and preview the whole site.

New PR

  • Everything seems fine to me, now let's create another branch and then a new PR

Change Requests

  • Then, I got my first "change request".

  • Yes! I forgot to push all the images ... And another commit and push.

  • Remove the letters "dddd".

  • Then, I need to re-request reviews from them again.

LGTM and Merge

item.title: 最有效的自学是例行公事 | The Most Efficient Self-Taught Learning is Routine
item.description:
我喜欢学习，但我讨厌花时间学习。

我们所有人一天只有 24 小时。 在工作、睡眠和其他一切之间，您可能已经在玩很多杂耍了！ 任何自学成才的人面临的挑战是找出可以始终如一地留出多少时间——将其融入你的日常工作中，适应你日常生活的节奏。

每隔一个星期六花两个小时潜入某件事是对您的常规节奏的一个很好的补充。 但如果这就是你所能做的，那么你将在每个时间块中花费很大一部分时间来试图记住你离开的地方。

如果你能在每个工作日留出半个小时，甚至一周只留出三天，你就会感觉到进步。 任何你能抓住的大块时间都会变得更有效率！

公平的警告是，最好的情况是，您将从您的个人空闲时间中抽出这段时间。 是的，这仍然很糟糕。 但这仍然比剥夺自己的睡眠或忽视人际关系要好。

尝试学习新技能的人通常会在他们有时间学习新技能时筋疲力尽。 我在我的日程安排中找到了解决这个问题的方法，早上第一件事就是做这件事，然后再去做任何其他工作。 这对我有用，因为我通常在工作开始前几个小时就已经醒了。

每个人的时间表都不一样。 只要您抽出时间并在日程安排上的任何地方保护它，您就可以沿着自己的道路前进。 不必每次都非常长，甚至不必每天都如此。 它只需要保持一致！



原文:

I love to learn, but I hate finding time for it.

We all only have 24 hours in a day. Between work, sleep, and everything else, you're probably juggling a lot already! The challenge for anyone doing self-taught learning is finding out how much time you can set aside consistently — fitting it into your regular routine, the cadence of your daily life.

Taking two hours to dive into something every other Saturday is a great supplement to your regular cadence. But if that's all you can do, you're gonna spend a good chunk of each time block just trying to remember where you left off.

If you can set aside even a half hour every weekday, or even just three days a week, you will feel the progress. And any big blocks of time you can snag will be infinitely more productive!

Fair warning that, best case, you'll be taking this time from your solo free time. Yes, that still sucks. But it's still better than depriving yourself of sleep, or neglecting your relationships.

It's common for people trying to learn new skills to be drained by the time they get time for it. I found a way around this in my schedule by doing it first thing in the morning, before I hop into any other work. This works for me because I'm already awake a couple hours before my job typically starts.

Everyone's schedule is different. As long as you're making the time and protecting it wherever it lands on your schedule, you're moving forward along your path. It doesn't have to be a huge long session each time, and it doesn't even have to be every day. It just needs to be consistent!
item.author: wingmatt

item.title: 建立和敏捷的心态并离开舒适区 | Building and agile mindset and leaving the comfort zone
item.description:
技术在不断发展，每天都在构建新产品，软件更新，框架版本。 但我们已经知道，我们如何利用这一优势？

努力离开我们的舒适区并开放学习新事物将帮助我们建立敏捷的思维方式，作为开发人员的一个很好的例子就是开始学习一门新的编程语言。 这将帮助我们更有分析能力，并且能够在不依赖特定解决方案的情况下解决任何问题。

敏捷的思维方式也将帮助我们提高沟通技巧，使团队能够更好地工作。
本周我开始实施这个策略。 作为前端开发人员，我已经使用 javascript 工作了一年，但我决定离开我的舒适区并开始学习 Ruby。 编程语言之间的情况非常不同，但归根结底，逻辑是相同的。

我们需要专注于建立和敏捷的思维方式，并在许多其他领域实施这一点。



原文:

Technology is in constant evolution, everyday new products are built, software updates, framework versions. But we already know that, how do we take the advantage?

Working on leaving our comfort zone and being open to learn new things is going to help us on building an agile mindset, a good example as a developer would be start learning a new programming language. This will help us to be more analytic and be capable of solve any problem without depending on a specific solution.

And agile mindset will also help us to improve our communication skills making posible working better as a team.
This week I started implementing this strategy. As frontend developer I've been working with javascript for a year but I decided to leave my comfort zone and started learning Ruby. Things are very different between programming languages but at the end of the day the logic is the same.

We need to focus on building and agile mindset and implementing this in many other areas.
